items.title: A, B, C are any points on the circle with centre O. If m(arc BC) = 110¬į and m(arc AB) = 125¬į, find measure arc AC. - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: A, B, C are any points on the circle with centre O. If m(arc BC) = 110¬į and m(arc AB) = 125¬į, find measure arc AC.
items.solution_text: m(arc AB) + m(arc BC) + m(arc AC) = 360¬į ......[Measure of complete circle is 360¬į] ‚ąī 125¬į + 110¬į + m(arc AC) = 360¬į ‚ąī m(arc AC) = 360¬į ‚Äď 125¬į ‚Äď 110¬į = 125¬į
items.solution_html:
m(arc AB) + m(arc BC) + m(arc AC) = 360¬į¬† ¬†......[Measure of complete circle is 360¬į]

‚ąī 125¬į + 110¬į + m(arc AC) = 360¬į

‚ąī m(arc AC) = 360¬į ‚Äď 125¬į ‚Äď 110¬į

= 125¬į

items.title: A ladder 10 m long reaches a window 8 m above the ground. Find the distance of the foot of the ladder from the base of wall. Complete the given activity. - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: A ladder 10 m long reaches a window 8 m above the ground. Find the distance of the foot of the ladder from the base of wall. Complete the given activity. Activity: As shown in figure suppose PR is the length of ladder = 10 m At P ‚Äď window, At Q ‚Äď base of wall, At R ‚Äď foot of ladder ‚ąī PQ = 8 m ‚ąī QR = ? In ‚ąÜPQR, m‚ą†PQR = 90¬į By Pythagoras Theorem, ‚ąī PQ 2 + `square` = PR 2 .....(I) Here, PR = 10, PQ = `square` From equation (I) 8 2 + QR 2 = 10 2 QR 2 = 10 2 ‚Äď 8 2 QR 2 = 100 ‚Äď 64 QR 2 = `square` QR = 6 ‚ąī The distance of foot of the ladder from the base of wall is 6 m.
items.solution_text: As shown in figure suppose PR is the length of ladder = 10 m, At P ‚Äď window, At Q ‚Äď base of wall, At R ‚Äď foot of ladder ‚ąī PQ = 8 m ‚ąī QR = ? In ‚ąÜPQR, m‚ą†PQR = 90¬į By Pythagoras Theorem, ‚ąī PQ 2 + QR 2 = PR 2 .....(I) Here, PR = 10, PQ = 8 From equation (I) 8 2 + QR 2 = 10 2 QR 2 = 10 2 ‚Äď 8 2 QR 2 = 100 ‚Äď 64 QR 2 = 36 QR = 6 ‚ąī The distance of foot of the ladder from the base of wall is 6 m.
items.solution_html:
As shown in figure suppose PR is the length of ladder = 10 m,

At P ‚Äď window, At Q ‚Äď base of wall, At R ‚Äď foot of ladder

‚ąī PQ = 8 m

‚ąī QR = ?

In ‚ąÜPQR, m‚ą†PQR = 90¬į

By Pythagoras Theorem,

‚ąī PQ2 + QR2 = PR2 ¬† ¬†.....(I)

Here, PR = 10, PQ = 8

From equation (I)

82 + QR2 = 102

QR2 = 102 ‚Äď 82

QR2 = 100 ‚Äď 64

QR2 = 36

QR = 6

‚ąī The distance of foot of the ladder from the base of wall is 6 m.

items.question: A line is parallel to one side of triangle which intersects remaining two sides in two distinct points then that line divides sides in same proportion. Given: In őĒABC line l || side BC and line l intersect side AB in P and side AC in Q. To prove: `"AP"/"PB" = "AQ"/"QC"` Construction: Draw CP and BQ Proof: őĒAPQ and őĒPQB have equal height. `("A"(őĒ"APQ"))/("A"(őĒ"PQB")) = (["______"])/"PB"` .....(i)[areas in proportion of base] `("A"(őĒ"APQ"))/("A"(őĒ"PQC")) = (["______"])/"QC"` .......(ii)[areas in proportion of base] őĒPQC and őĒPQB have [______] is common base. Seg PQ || Seg BC, hence height of őĒAPQ and őĒPQB. A(őĒPQC) = A(őĒ______) ......(iii) `("A"(őĒ"APQ"))/("A"(őĒ"PQB")) = ("A"(őĒ "______"))/("A"(őĒ "______"))` ......[(i), (ii), and (iii)] `"AP"/"PB" = "AQ"/"QC"` .......[(i) and (ii)]
items.solution_text: Proof: őĒAPQ and őĒPQB have equal height. `("A"(őĒ"APQ"))/("A"(őĒ"PQB"))` = `"AP"/"PB"` .....(i)[areas in proportion of base] `("A"(őĒ"APQ"))/("A"(őĒ"PQC"))` = `"AQ"/"QC"` .......(ii)[areas in proportion of base] őĒPQC and őĒPQB have PQ is common base. Seg PQ || Seg BC, hence height of őĒAPQ and őĒPQB. A(őĒPQC) = A(őĒ PQB ) ......(iii)[Areas of two triangles having equal base and height are equal] `("A"(őĒ"APQ"))/("A"(őĒ"PQB"))` = `("A"(őĒ"APQ"))/("A"(őĒ "PQC"))` ......[(i), (ii), and (iii)] `"AP"/"PB" = "AQ"/"QC"` .......[(i) and (ii)]
items.solution_html:
Proof: őĒAPQ and őĒPQB have equal height.

`("A"(őĒ"APQ"))/("A"(őĒ"PQB"))` = `"AP"/"PB"`¬† ¬†.....(i)[areas in proportion of base]

`("A"(őĒ"APQ"))/("A"(őĒ"PQC"))` = `"AQ"/"QC"`¬† .......(ii)[areas in proportion of base]

őĒPQC and őĒPQB have PQ is common base.

Seg PQ || Seg BC, hence height of őĒAPQ and őĒPQB.

A(őĒPQC) = A(őĒPQB)¬† ¬† ......(iii)[Areas of two triangles having equal base and height are equal]

`("A"(őĒ"APQ"))/("A"(őĒ"PQB"))` = `("A"(őĒ"APQ"))/("A"(őĒ "PQC"))`¬† ¬†......[(i), (ii), and (iii)]

`"AP"/"PB" = "AQ"/"QC"`¬† ¬†.......[(i) and (ii)]

items.title: őĒABC ~ őĒPQR, A(őĒABC) = 80 sq.cm, A(őĒPQR) = 125 sq.cm, then complete A(őĒABC)A(őĒPQR)=80125=[______][______], hence ABPQ=[______][______] - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: őĒABC ~ őĒPQR, A(őĒABC) = 80 sq.cm, A(őĒPQR) = 125 sq.cm, then complete `("A"(őĒ"ABC"))/("A"(őĒ"PQR")) = 80/125 = (["______"])/(["______"])`, hence `"AB"/"PQ" = (["______"])/(["______"])`
items.solution_text: `("A"(őĒ"ABC"))/("A"(őĒ"PQR")) = 80/125` = `16/25` ......(i)[Given] `("A"(őĒ"ABC"))/("A"(őĒ"PQR")) = "AB"^2/"PQ"^2` .....(ii)[Theorem of areas of similar triangles] ‚ąī `"AB"^2/"PQ"^2 = 16/25` .....[From (i) and (ii)] Hence `"AB"/"PQ"` = `4/5` ......[[Taking square root of both sides]
items.solution_html:
`("A"(őĒ"ABC"))/("A"(őĒ"PQR")) = 80/125` =¬† `16/25`¬† ......(i)[Given]

`("A"(őĒ"ABC"))/("A"(őĒ"PQR")) = "AB"^2/"PQ"^2`¬† ¬†.....(ii)[Theorem of areas of similar triangles]

‚ąī `"AB"^2/"PQ"^2 = 16/25`¬† ¬† ¬†.....[From (i) and (ii)]

Hence `"AB"/"PQ"` = `4/5`¬† ¬†......[[Taking square root of both sides]

items.title: An architecture have model of building. Length of building is 1 m then length of model is 0.75 cm. Then find length and height of model building whose actual length is 22.5 m and height is 10 m - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: An architecture have model of building. Length of building is 1 m then length of model is 0.75 cm. Then find length and height of model building whose actual length is 22.5 m and height is 10 m
items.solution_text: Rough Figure - Actual length/height Model length/height 1 m 0.75 cm 22.5 m ? (x cm) 10 m ? (y cm) The actual length of 1 m is shown as 0.75 cm in the model then let the actual length of 22.5 m is shown in the model by 'x' cm. `therefore 1/22.5 = 0.75/x` ‚ąī x = `0.75 xx 22.5` x = 16.875 cm Now, The actual length of 1 m is shown as 0.75 cm in the model then let the actual height of 10 m is shown in the model by 'y' cm. `therefore 1/10 = 0.75/"y"` ‚ąī y = 0.75 √ó 10 y = 7.5 cm
items.solution_html:
Rough Figure -

Actual
length/height		 Model
length/height
1 m 0.75 cm
22.5 m ? (x cm)
10 m ? (y cm)

The actual length of 1 m is shown as 0.75 cm in the model then let the actual length of 22.5 m is shown in the model by 'x' cm.

`therefore 1/22.5 = 0.75/x`

‚ąī x = `0.75 xx 22.5`

x = 16.875 cm

Now, The actual length of 1 m is shown as 0.75 cm in the model then let the actual height of 10 m is shown in the model by 'y' cm.

`therefore 1/10 = 0.75/"y"`

‚ąī y = 0.75 √ó 10

y = 7.5 cm

items.title: An exterior angle of a cyclic quadrilateral is congruent to the angle opposite to its adjacent interior angle, to prove the theorem complete the activity. - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: An exterior angle of a cyclic quadrilateral is congruent to the angle opposite to its adjacent interior angle, to prove the theorem complete the activity. Given: ÔÉć ABCD is cyclic, `square` is the exterior angle of ÔÉć ABCD To prove: ‚ą†DCE ‚ČÖ ‚ą†BAD Proof: `square` + ‚ą†BCD = `square` .....[Angles in linear pair] (I) ÔÉć ABCD is a cyclic. `square` + ‚ą†BAD = `square` ......[Theorem of cyclic quadrilateral] (II) By (I) and (II) ‚ą†DCE + ‚ą†BCD = `square` + ‚ą†BAD ‚ą†DCE ‚ČÖ ‚ą†BAD
items.solution_text: Proof: Given: ÔÉć ABCD is cyclic, ‚ą†DCE is the exterior angle of ÔÉć ABCD To prove: ‚ą†DCE ‚ČÖ ‚ą†BAD Proof: ‚ą†DCE + ‚ą†BCD = 180¬į .....[Angles in linear pair] (I) ÔÉć ABCD is a cyclic. ‚ą†BCD + ‚ą†BAD = 180¬į ......[Theorem of cyclic quadrilateral] (II) By (I) and (II) ‚ą†DCE + ‚ą†BCD = ‚ą†BCD + ‚ą†BAD ‚ą†DCE ‚ČÖ ‚ą†BAD
items.solution_html:
Proof:¬†

Given: ÔÉć ABCD is cyclic,

‚ą†DCE is the exterior angle of ÔÉć ABCD

To prove: ‚ą†DCE ‚ČÖ ‚ą†BAD

Proof: ‚ą†DCE + ‚ą†BCD = 180¬į ¬† ¬†.....[Angles in linear pair] (I)

ÔÉć ABCD is a cyclic.

‚ą†BCD + ‚ą†BAD = 180¬į ¬† ¬† ......[Theorem of cyclic quadrilateral] (II)

By (I) and (II)

‚ą†DCE + ‚ą†BCD = ‚ą†BCD + ‚ą†BAD

‚ą†DCE ‚ČÖ ‚ą†BAD

items.title: Areas of two similar triangles are 225 cm2 and 81 cm2. If side of smaller triangle is 12 cm, find corresponding side of major triangle. - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: Areas of two similar triangles are 225 cm 2 and 81 cm 2 . If side of smaller triangle is 12 cm, find corresponding side of major triangle.
items.solution_text: Let the areas of two similar triangles be A 1 and A 2 . A 1 = 225 cm 2 and A 2 = 81 cm 2 Let the corresponding sides of triangles be s 1 and s 2 respectively. s 1 = 12 cm `"A"_1/"A"_2 = "s"_1^2/"s"_2^2` ...[Theorem of areas of similar triangles] ‚ąī `225/81 = "s"_1^2/12^2` ‚ąī s 1 2 = `(225 xx 12^2)/81` ‚ąī s 1 = `(15 xx 12)/9` ...[Taking square root of both sides] ‚ąī s 1 = 20 cm ‚ąī The length of the corresponding side of the bigger triangle is 20 cm.
items.solution_html:
Let the areas of two similar triangles be A1 and A2.

A1 = 225 cm2 and A2 = 81 cm2

Let the corresponding sides of triangles be s1 and s2 respectively.

s1 = 12 cm

`"A"_1/"A"_2 = "s"_1^2/"s"_2^2`¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬†...[Theorem of areas of similar triangles]

‚ąī `225/81 = "s"_1^2/12^2`

‚ąī s12 = `(225 xx 12^2)/81`

‚ąī s1 = `(15 xx 12)/9`¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ...[Taking square root of both sides]

‚ąī s1 = 20 cm

‚ąī The length of the corresponding side of the bigger triangle is 20 cm.

items.title: Areas of two similar triangles are equal then prove that triangles are congruent. - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: Areas of two similar triangles are equal then prove that triangles are congruent
items.solution_text: Given: őĒABC ~ őĒPQR and A(őĒABC) = A(őĒPQR) To prove: őĒABC ‚ČÖ őĒPQR Proof: `("A"(őĒ"ABC"))/("A"(őĒ"PQR"))` = 1 ......(i) [Given] Also, `("A"(őĒ"ABC"))/("A"(őĒ"PQR")) = "AB"^2/"PQ"^2 = "BC"^2/"QR"^2 = "AC"^2/"PR"^2` ......[Theorem of areas of similar triangles] ‚ąī 1 = `"AB"^2/"PQ"^2 = "BC"^2/"QR"^2 = "AC"^2/"PR"^2` .....[From (i)] ‚ąī 1 = `"AB"^2/"PQ"^2` ‚ąī AB 2 = PQ 2 ‚ąī AB = PQ ......[Taking square root of both sides] i.e., seg AB ‚ČÖ seg PQ Similarly, seg BC ‚ČÖ seg QR and seg AC ‚ČÖ seg PR ‚ąī őĒABC ‚ČÖ őĒPQR ......[SSS test of congruency]
items.solution_html:
Given: őĒABC ~ őĒPQR and A(őĒABC) = A(őĒPQR)

To prove: őĒABC ‚ČÖ őĒPQR

Proof:

`("A"(őĒ"ABC"))/("A"(őĒ"PQR"))` = 1¬† ¬† ......(i) [Given]

Also, `("A"(őĒ"ABC"))/("A"(őĒ"PQR")) = "AB"^2/"PQ"^2 = "BC"^2/"QR"^2 = "AC"^2/"PR"^2`¬† ¬†......[Theorem of areas of similar triangles]

‚ąī 1 = `"AB"^2/"PQ"^2 = "BC"^2/"QR"^2 = "AC"^2/"PR"^2`¬† ¬† ¬†.....[From (i)]

‚ąī 1 = `"AB"^2/"PQ"^2`

‚ąī AB2 = PQ2

‚ąī AB = PQ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬†......[Taking square root of both sides]

i.e., seg AB ‚ČÖ seg PQ

Similarly, seg BC ‚ČÖ seg QR and seg AC ‚ČÖ seg PR

‚ąī őĒABC ‚ČÖ őĒPQR¬† ¬† ¬†......[SSS test of congruency]

items.title: Areas of two similar triangles are in the ratio 144: 49. Find the ratio of their corresponding sides. - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: Areas of two similar triangles are in the ratio 144: 49. Find the ratio of their corresponding sides.
items.solution_text: Let the areas of two similar triangles be A 1 , A 2 and their corresponding sides be S 1 , S 2 respectively. ‚ąī `"A"_1/"A"_2 = 144/49` ...(i)[Given] ‚ąī by the theorem of areas of similar triangles, ‚ąī `"A"_1/"A"_2 = "S"_1^2/"S"_2^2` ‚ąī `144/49 = "S"_1^2/"S"_2^2` ...[From (i)] ‚ąī by taking square root of both sides, ‚ąī `"S"_1/"S"_2 = 12/7` ‚ąī The ratio of the corresponding sides of the given triangles is 12: 7.
items.solution_html:
Let the areas of two similar triangles be A1, A2 and their corresponding sides be S1, S2 respectively.

‚ąī `"A"_1/"A"_2 = 144/49`¬† ¬† ...(i)[Given]

‚ąī by the theorem of areas of similar triangles,

‚ąī `"A"_1/"A"_2 = "S"_1^2/"S"_2^2`

‚ąī `144/49 = "S"_1^2/"S"_2^2`¬† ¬† ...[From (i)]

‚ąī by taking square root of both sides,

‚ąī `"S"_1/"S"_2 = 12/7`¬†

‚ąī The ratio of the corresponding sides of the given triangles is 12: 7.

items.title: As shown figure, ‚ą†DFE = 90¬į, FG ‚ä• ED, if GD = 8, FG = 12, then EG = ? - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: As shown figure, ‚ą†DFE = 90¬į, FG ‚ä• ED, if GD = 8, FG = 12, then EG = ?
items.solution_text: (i) In ‚ąÜDEF, ‚ą†DFE = 90¬į and seg FG ‚ä• hypotenuse ED ...[Given] ‚ąī FG 2 = EG √ó GD ......[By theorem of geometric mean] ‚ąī (12) 2 = EG √ó 8 ......[Given] ‚ąī 144 = EG √ó 8 ‚ąī EG = `144/8` ‚ąī EG = 18 units (ii) In ‚ąÜDGF, ‚ą†DGF = 90¬į .....[ ‚ł™ FG ‚ä• ED] ‚ąī FD 2 = FG 2 + GD 2 ......[Pythagoras theorem] ‚ąī FD 2 = (12) 2 + (8) 2 ......[Given] ‚ąī FD 2 = 144 + 64 ‚ąī FD 2 = 208 ‚ąī FD = `sqrt(16 xx 13)` ......[Taking square root of both sides] ‚ąī FD = `4sqrt(13)` units (iii) In EGF, ‚ą†EGF = 90¬į ......[‚ł™ FG ‚ä• ED] ‚ąī EF 2 = EG 2 + FG 2 ......[Pythagoras theorem] ‚ąī EF 2 = (18) 2 + (12) 2 ......[From (i) and given] ‚ąī EF 2 = 324 + 144 ‚ąī EF 2 = 468 ‚ąī EF = `sqrt(36 xx 13)` .......[Taking square root of both sides] ‚ąī EF = `6sqrt(13)` units
items.solution_html:
(i) In ‚ąÜDEF,

‚ą†DFE = 90¬į and seg FG ‚ä• hypotenuse ED¬† ...[Given]

‚ąī FG2 = EG √ó GD¬† ¬† ¬† ......[By theorem of geometric mean]

‚ąī (12)2 = EG √ó 8 ¬† ¬†......[Given]

‚ąī 144 = EG √ó 8

‚ąī EG = `144/8`

‚ąī EG = 18 units

(ii)¬† In ‚ąÜDGF,

‚ą†DGF = 90¬į¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬†.....[ ‚ł™ FG ‚ä• ED]

‚ąī FD2 = FG2 + GD2 ¬† ¬† ¬†......[Pythagoras theorem]

‚ąī FD2 = (12)2 + (8)2 ¬† ¬†......[Given]

‚ąī FD2 = 144 + 64

‚ąī FD2 = 208

‚ąī FD = `sqrt(16 xx 13)`¬† ¬† ......[Taking square root of both sides]

‚ąī FD = `4sqrt(13)` units

(iii) In EGF,

¬†‚ą†EGF = 90¬į¬† ¬† ......[‚ł™ FG ‚ä• ED]

‚ąī EF2 = EG2 + FG2 ¬† ¬†......[Pythagoras theorem]

‚ąī EF2 = (18)2 + (12)2 ¬† ......[From (i) and given]

‚ąī EF2 = 324 + 144

‚ąī EF2 = 468

‚ąī EF = `sqrt(36 xx 13)`¬† ¬† .......[Taking square root of both sides]

‚ąī EF = `6sqrt(13)` units

items.title: As shown in figure, LK = 62 then i. MK = ? ii. ML = ? iii. MN = ? - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: As shown in figure, LK = `6sqrt(2)` then MK = ? ML = ? MN = ?
items.solution_text: (i) In ‚ąÜMLK, ‚ą†MLK = 90¬į and ‚ą†LKM = 30¬į ......[Given] ‚ąī ‚ą†LMK = 60¬į ......[Remaining angle of a triangle] ‚ąī ‚ąÜMLK is a 30¬į ‚Äď 60¬į ‚Äď 90¬į triangle. ‚ąī LK = `sqrt(3)/2` MK ......[Side opposite to 60¬į] ‚ąī `6sqrt(2) = sqrt(3)/2` MK ......[Given] ‚ąī MK = `6sqrt(2) xx 2/sqrt(3)` = `(12sqrt(2))/sqrt(3)` = `(12 xx sqrt(2) xx sqrt(3))/(sqrt(3) xx sqrt(3))` ......[Multiply numerator and denominator by `sqrt(3)`] = `(12 xx sqrt(2 xx 3))/3` MK = `4sqrt(6)` units (ii) In ‚ąÜMLK, ‚ą†MLK = 90¬į ......[Given] ‚ąī MK 2 = ML 2 + LK 2 ......[Pythagoras theorem] ‚ąī `(4sqrt(6))^2 = "ML"^2 + (6sqrt(2))^2` ......[From (i) and given] ‚ąī (16 √ó 6) = ML 2 + (36 √ó 2) ‚ąī 96 = ML 2 + 72 ‚ąī ML 2 = 24 ‚ąī ML = `sqrt(24)` .......[Taking square root of both sides] ‚ąī ML = `sqrt(4 xx 6)` ‚ąī ML = `2sqrt(6)` units (iii) In ‚ąÜNKM, ‚ą†NKM = 90¬į and ‚ą†MNK = 45¬į ......[Given] ‚ąī ‚ą†KMN = 45¬į ......[Remaining angle of a triangle] ‚ąī ‚ąÜNKM is 45¬į ‚Äď 45¬į ‚Äď 90¬į triangle. ‚ąī MK = `1/sqrt(2)` MN ......[Theorem of 45¬į ‚Äď 45¬į ‚Äď 90¬į triangle] ‚ąī `4sqrt(6) = 1/sqrt(2)` MN ......[From (i)] ‚ąī MN = `4sqrt(6) xx sqrt(2)` = `4sqrt(6 xx 2)` = `4sqrt(4 xx 3)` = `4 xx2 xx sqrt(3)` ‚ąī MN = `8sqrt(3)` units
items.solution_html:
(i) In ‚ąÜMLK,

‚ą†MLK = 90¬į and ‚ą†LKM = 30¬į¬† ¬† ......[Given]

‚ąī ‚ą†LMK = 60¬į¬† ¬† ¬†......[Remaining angle of a triangle]

‚ąī ‚ąÜMLK is a 30¬į ‚Äď 60¬į ‚Äď 90¬į triangle.

‚ąī LK = `sqrt(3)/2` MK¬† ¬† ......[Side opposite to 60¬į]

‚ąī `6sqrt(2) = sqrt(3)/2` MK ¬† ......[Given]

‚ąī MK = `6sqrt(2) xx 2/sqrt(3)`

= `(12sqrt(2))/sqrt(3)`

= `(12 xx sqrt(2) xx sqrt(3))/(sqrt(3) xx sqrt(3))`¬† ¬†......[Multiply numerator and denominator by `sqrt(3)`]

= `(12 xx sqrt(2 xx 3))/3`

MK = `4sqrt(6)` units

¬†

(ii) In ‚ąÜMLK,

‚ą†MLK = 90¬į¬† ¬† ......[Given]

‚ąī MK2 = ML2 + LK2 ¬† ¬† ......[Pythagoras theorem]

‚ąī `(4sqrt(6))^2 = "ML"^2 + (6sqrt(2))^2`¬† ¬†......[From (i) and given]

‚ąī (16 √ó 6) = ML2 + (36 √ó 2)

‚ąī 96 = ML2 + 72

‚ąī ML2 = 24

‚ąī ML = `sqrt(24)`¬† ¬† ¬†.......[Taking square root of both sides]

‚ąī ML = `sqrt(4 xx 6)`

‚ąī ML = `2sqrt(6)` units

¬†

(iii)¬†In ‚ąÜNKM,

‚ą†NKM = 90¬į and ‚ą†MNK = 45¬į¬† ¬† ......[Given]

‚ąī ‚ą†KMN = 45¬į¬† ¬† ¬† ......[Remaining angle of a triangle]

‚ąī ‚ąÜNKM is 45¬į ‚Äď 45¬į ‚Äď 90¬į triangle.

‚ąī MK = `1/sqrt(2)` MN¬† ¬† ......[Theorem of 45¬į ‚Äď 45¬į ‚Äď 90¬į triangle]

‚ąī `4sqrt(6) = 1/sqrt(2)` MN¬† ¬† ¬†......[From (i)]

‚ąī MN = `4sqrt(6) xx sqrt(2)`

= `4sqrt(6 xx 2)`

= `4sqrt(4 xx 3)`

= `4 xx2 xx sqrt(3)`

‚ąī MN = `8sqrt(3)` units

items.solution_text: (3, 4, 5) Here, 5 2 = 25 3 2 + 4 2 = 9 + 16 = 25 ‚ąī 5 2 = 3 2 + 4 2 The square of the largest number is equal to the sum of the squares of the other two numbers. ‚ąī (3, 4, 5) is a Pythagoras triplet.

‚ąī (3, 4, 5) is a Pythagoras triplet.¬†

(7, 8, 15)

(6, 14, 15)

items.title: Complete the following activity to find the length of hypotenuse of right angled triangle, if sides of right angle are 9 cm and 12 cm. Activity: In ‚ąÜPQR, m‚ą†PQR = 90¬įBy Pythagoras Theorem, PQ2 + ‚Ė° = P - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: Complete the following activity to find the length of hypotenuse of right angled triangle, if sides of right angle are 9 cm and 12 cm. Activity: In ‚ąÜPQR, m‚ą†PQR = 90¬į By Pythagoras Theorem, PQ 2 + `square` = PR 2 ......(I) ‚ąī PR 2 = 9 2 + 12 2 ‚ąī PR 2 = `square` + 144 ‚ąī PR 2 = `square` ‚ąī PR = 15 ‚ąī Length of hypotenuse of triangle PQR is `square` cm.
items.solution_text: In ‚ąÜPQR, m‚ą†PQR = 90¬į By Pythagoras Theorem, PQ 2 + QR 2 = PR 2 ......(I) ‚ąī PR 2 = 9 2 + 12 2 ‚ąī PR 2 = 81 + 144 ‚ąī PR 2 = 225 ‚ąī PR = 15 ......[Taking square roots of both sides] ‚ąī Length of hypotenuse of triangle PQR is 15 cm.
items.solution_html:

‚ąī Length of hypotenuse of triangle PQR is 15 cm.

items.title: Find coordinates of midpoint of segment joining (‚Äď 2, 6) and (8, 2) - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: Find coordinates of midpoint of segment joining (‚Äď 2, 6) and (8, 2)
items.solution_text: Let A(x 1 , y 1 ) = A(‚Äď 2, 6), B(x 2 , y 2 ) = B(8, 2), C(x, y) be the midpoint of seg AB. ‚ąī x 1 = ‚Äď 2, y 1 = 6, x 2 = 8, y 2 = 2 By midpoint formula, C(x, y) = `((x_1 + x_2)/2, (y_1 + y_2)/2)` = `((-2 + 8)/2, (6 + 2)/2)` = `(6/2, 8/2)` = (3, 4) ‚ąī Coordinates of midpoint of segment joining (‚Äď 2, 6) and (8, 2) are (3, 4).
items.solution_html:
Let A(x1, y1) = A(‚Äď 2, 6), B(x2, y2) = B(8, 2), C(x, y) be the midpoint of seg AB.

‚ąī x1 = ‚Äď 2, y1 = 6, x2 = 8, y2 = 2

By midpoint formula,

C(x, y) = `((x_1 + x_2)/2, (y_1 + y_2)/2)`

= `((-2 + 8)/2, (6 + 2)/2)`

= `(6/2, 8/2)`

= (3, 4)

‚ąī Coordinates of midpoint of segment joining (‚Äď 2, 6) and (8, 2) are (3, 4).

items.title: Find coordinates of midpoint of the segment joining points (0, 2) and (12, 14). - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: Find coordinates of midpoint of the segment joining points (0, 2) and (12, 14)
items.solution_text: Let A(x 1 , y 1 ) = A(0, 2), B (x 2 , y 2 ) = B(12, 14) Let the co-ordinates of the midpoint be P(x, y). ‚ąī By midpoint formula, x = `(x_1 + x_2)/2 = (0 + 12)/2` = 6 y = `(y_1 + y_2)/2 = (2 + 14)/2 = 16/2` = 8 ‚ąī The co-ordinates of the midpoint of the segment joining (0, 2) and (12, 14) are (6, 8).
items.solution_html:
Let A(x1, y1) = A(0, 2), B (x2, y2) = B(12, 14)

Let the co-ordinates of the midpoint be P(x, y).

‚ąī By midpoint formula,

x = `(x_1 + x_2)/2 = (0 + 12)/2` = 6

y = `(y_1 + y_2)/2 = (2 + 14)/2 = 16/2` = 8

‚ąī The co-ordinates of the midpoint of the segment joining (0, 2) and (12, 14) are (6, 8).

items.question: Find distance between point A(‚Äď1, 1) and point B(5, ‚Äď7): Solution: Suppose A(x 1 , y 1 ) and B(x 2 , y 2 ) x 1 = ‚Äď1, y 1 = 1 and x 2 = 5, y 2 = ‚Äď 7 Using distance formula, d(A, B) = `sqrt((x_2 - x_1)^2 + (y_2 - y_1)^2` ‚ąī d(A, B) = `sqrt(square +[(-7) + square]^2` ‚ąī d(A, B) = `sqrt(square)` ‚ąī d(A, B) = `square`
items.solution_text: Suppose A(x 1 , y 1 ) and B(x 2 , y 2 ) x 1 = ‚Äď1, y 1 = 1 and x 2 = 5, y 2 = ‚Äď 7 Using distance formula, d(A, B) = `sqrt((x_2 - x_1)^2 + (y_2 - y_1)^2` ‚ąī d(A, B) = `sqrt([5 - (-1)]^2 + [(-7) + -1]^2` ‚ąī d(A, B) = `sqrt(6^2 + (-8)^2` ‚ąī d(A, B) = `sqrt(36 + 64)` ‚ąī d(A, B) = `sqrt(100)` ‚ąī d(A, B) = 10 units
items.solution_html:

items.title: Find distance between points O(0, 0) and B(‚Äď 5, 12) - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: Find distance between points O(0, 0) and B(‚Äď 5, 12)
items.solution_text: Let O(x 1 , y 1 ) = O(0, 0) and B(x 2 , y 2 ) = B(‚Äď 5, 12) ‚ąī x 1 = 0, y 1 = 0, x 2 = ‚Äď 5, y 2 = 12 By distance formula, d(O, B) = `sqrt((x_2 - x_1)^2 + (y_2 - y_1)^2` = `sqrt((-5 - 0)^2 + (12 - 0)^2` = `sqrt((-5)^2 + 12^2` = `sqrt(25 + 144)` = `sqrt(169)` ‚ąī d(O, B) = 13 units ‚ąī The distance between the points O and B is 13 units.

items.title: Find distance CD where C(‚Äď 3a, a), D(a, ‚Äď 2a) - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: Find distance CD where C(‚Äď 3a, a), D(a, ‚Äď 2a)
items.solution_text: Let C(x 1 , y 1 ) and D(x 2 , y 2 ) be the given points ‚ąī x 1 = ‚Äď 3a, y 1 = a, x 2 = a, y 2 = ‚Äď 2a By distance formula, d(C, D) = `sqrt((x_2 - x_1)^2 + (y_2 - y_1)^2` = `sqrt(["a" - (-3"a")]^2 + (-2"a" - "a")^2` = `sqrt(("a" + 3"a")^2 + (-2"a" - "a")^2` = `sqrt((4"a")^2 + (-3"a")^2` = `sqrt(16"a"^2 + 9"a"^2)` = `sqrt(25"a"^2)` ‚ąī d(C, D) = 5a units
items.solution_html:
Let C(x1, y1) and D(x2, y2) be the given points

‚ąī x1 = ‚Äď 3a, y1 = a, x2 = a, y2 = ‚Äď 2a

By distance formula,

d(C, D) = `sqrt((x_2 - x_1)^2 + (y_2 - y_1)^2`

= `sqrt(["a" - (-3"a")]^2 + (-2"a" - "a")^2`

= `sqrt(("a" + 3"a")^2 + (-2"a" - "a")^2`

= `sqrt((4"a")^2 + (-3"a")^2`

= `sqrt(16"a"^2 + 9"a"^2)`

= `sqrt(25"a"^2)`

‚ąī d(C, D) = 5a units

By distance formula,

= `sqrt((0 - 6)^2 + (0 - 8)^2`

= `sqrt(36 + 64)`

= `sqrt(100)`

= 10 cm

‚ąī The distance of point A(6, 8) from origin is 10 cm.

Let A(x1, y1) = A(4, 7), B(x2, y2) = B(8, 4), C(x3, y3) = C(7, 11)

Activity: As shown in figure ÔÉźLMNT is a reactangle. ‚ąī Area of rectangle = length √ó breadth ‚ąī Area of rectangle = `square` √ó breadth ‚ąī 192 = `square` √ó breadth ‚ąī Breadth = 12 cm Also, ‚ą†TLM = 90¬į ......[Each angle of reactangle is right angle] In ‚ąÜTLM, By Pythagoras theorem ‚ąī TM 2 = TL 2 + `square` ‚ąī TM 2 = 12 2 + `square` ‚ąī TM 2 = 144 + `square` ‚ąī TM 2 = 400 ‚ąī TM = 20
items.solution_text: As shown in figure ÔÉźLMNT is reactangle. ‚ąī Area of rectangle = length √ó breadth ‚ąī Area of rectangle = 16 √ó breadth ‚ąī 192 = 16 √ó breadth ‚ąī Breadth = `192/16` ‚ąī Breadth = 12 cm i.e., TL = 12 cm and LM = 16 cm ......(i) Also, ‚ą†TLM = 90¬į ......[Each angle of reactangle is right angle] In ‚ąÜTLM, By Pythagoras theorem ‚ąī TM 2 = TL 2 + LM 2 ‚ąī TM 2 = 12 2 + 16 2 ......[From (i)] ‚ąī TM 2 = 144 + 256 ‚ąī TM 2 = 400 ‚ąī TM = 20

items.title: Find the ratio in which Y-axis divides the point A(3, 5) and point B(‚Äď 6, 7). Find the coordinates of the point - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: Find the ratio in which Y-axis divides the point A(3, 5) and point B(‚Äď 6, 7). Find the coordinates of the point
items.solution_text: Let C be a point on Y-axis which divides seg AB in the ratio m : n Point C lies on the Y-axis. ‚ąī its X-coordinate is 0. Let C = (0, y) Here, A(x 1 , y 1 ) = A(3, 5) B(x 2 , y 2 ) = B(‚Äď 6, 7) By Section formula, x = `("m"x_2 + "n"x_1)/("m" + "n")` ‚ąī 0 = `(-6"m" + 3"n")/("m" + "n")` ‚ąī ‚Äď 6m + 3n = 0 ‚ąī 3n = 6m ‚ąī `"m"/"n" = 3/6` ‚ąī `"m"/"n" = 1/2` ......(i) ‚ąī m : n = 1 : 2 By section formula, y = `("m"y_2 + "n"y_1)/("m" + "n")` y = `(7"m" + 5"n")/("m" + "n")` = `(7"m" + 5(2"m"))/("m" + 2"m")` ......[From (i), n = 2m] = `(7"m" + 10"m")/(3"m")` = `(17"m")/(3"m")` ‚ąī Y-axis divides the seg AB in the ratio 1 : 2 and the co-ordinates of that point is `(0, 17/3)`.
items.solution_html:
Let C be a point on Y-axis which divides seg AB in the ratio m : n

Point C lies on the Y-axis.

‚ąī its X-coordinate is 0.

Let C = (0, y)

Here,

A(x1, y1) = A(3, 5)

B(x2, y2) = B(‚Äď 6, 7)

By Section formula,

x = `("m"x_2 + "n"x_1)/("m" + "n")`¬†

‚ąī 0 = `(-6"m" + 3"n")/("m" + "n")`

‚ąī ‚Äď 6m + 3n = 0

‚ąī 3n = 6m

‚ąī `"m"/"n" = 3/6`

‚ąī `"m"/"n" = 1/2`¬† ¬†......(i)

‚ąī m : n = 1 : 2

By section formula,

y =¬† `("m"y_2 + "n"y_1)/("m" + "n")`¬†

y = `(7"m" + 5"n")/("m" + "n")`

= `(7"m" + 5(2"m"))/("m" + 2"m")`¬† ......[From (i), n = 2m]

= `(7"m" + 10"m")/(3"m")`

= `(17"m")/(3"m")`

‚ąī Y-axis divides the seg AB in the ratio 1 : 2 and the co-ordinates of that point is `(0, 17/3)`.

items.question: From adjoining figure, ‚ą†ABC = 90¬į, ‚ą†DCB = 90¬į, AB = 6, DC = 8, then `("A"(őĒ"ABC"))/("A"(őĒ"BCD"))` = ?

items.title: From fig., seg PQ || side BC, AP = x + 3, PB = x ‚Äď 3, AQ = x + 5, QC = x ‚Äď 2, then complete the acitivity to find the vlaue of x.In őĒPQB, PQ || side BC APPB=AQ[______] .......[______] x+3x-3=x+5[___ - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: From fig., seg PQ || side BC, AP = x + 3, PB = x ‚Äď 3, AQ = x + 5, QC = x ‚Äď 2, then complete the activity to find the value of x. In őĒPQB, PQ || side BC `"AP"/"PB" = "AQ"/(["______"])` ...[______] `(x + 3)/(x - 3) = (x + 5)/(["______"])` (x + 3) [______] = (x + 5)(x ‚Äď 3) x 2 + x ‚Äď [______] = x 2 + 2x ‚Äď 15 x = [______]
items.solution_text: In őĒPQB, PQ || side BC `"AP"/"PB" = "AQ"/bb"QC"` ...[ Base proportionality theorem ] `(x + 3)/(x - 3)` = `(x + 5)/bb(x - 2)` (x + 3) (x ‚Äď 2) = (x + 5)(x ‚Äď 3) x 2 + x ‚Äď 6 = x 2 + 2x ‚Äď 15 ‚ąī x ‚Äď 6 = 2x ‚Äď 15 ‚ąī 2x ‚Äď x = 15 ‚Äď 6 ‚ąī x = 9
items.solution_html:
In őĒPQB,

PQ || side BC

`"AP"/"PB" = "AQ"/bb"QC"`¬† ¬† ...[Base proportionality theorem]

`(x + 3)/(x - 3)` = `(x + 5)/bb(x - 2)`

(x + 3) (x ‚Äď 2) = (x + 5)(x ‚Äď 3)

x2 + x ‚Äď¬†6 = x2 + 2x ‚Äď 15

‚ąī x ‚Äď 6 = 2x ‚Äď 15

‚ąī 2x ‚Äď x = 15 ‚Äď 6

‚ąī x = 9

items.title: From given figure, In ‚ąÜABC, AB ‚ä• BC, AB = BC, AC = 22 then l (AB) = ? - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: From given figure, In ‚ąÜABC, AB ‚ä• BC, AB = BC, AC = `2sqrt(2)` then l (AB) = ?
items.solution_text: AB = BC ......[Given] ‚ąī ‚ą†A = ‚ą†C ......[Isosceles triangle theorem] Let ‚ą†A = ‚ą†C = x ......(i) In ‚ąÜABC, ‚ą†A + ‚ą†B + ‚ą†C = 180¬į ......[Sum of the measures of the angles of a triangle is 180¬į] ‚ąī x + 90¬į + x = 180¬į ......[From (i)] ‚ąī 2x = 90¬į ‚ąī x = `90^circ/2` ......[From (i)] ‚ąī x = 45¬į ‚ąī ‚ą†BAC = ‚ą†BCA = 45¬į ‚ąī ‚ąÜABC is a 45¬į ‚Äď 45¬į ‚Äď 90¬į triangle. ‚ąī AB = BC = `1/sqrt(2) xx "AC"` ......[Side opposite to 45¬į] = `1/sqrt(2) xx 2sqrt(2)` ‚ąī `l("AB")` = 2 units
items.solution_html:
AB = BC¬† ¬† ¬†......[Given]

‚ąī ‚ą†A = ‚ą†C¬† ¬† ¬†......[Isosceles triangle theorem]

Let ‚ą†A = ‚ą†C = x¬† ¬† ......(i)

In ‚ąÜABC, ‚ą†A + ‚ą†B + ‚ą†C = 180¬į¬† ¬† ......[Sum of the measures of the angles of a triangle is 180¬į]

‚ąī x + 90¬į + x = 180¬į¬† ¬† ¬† ......[From (i)]

‚ąī 2x = 90¬į

‚ąī x = `90^circ/2`¬† ¬† ¬†......[From (i)]

‚ąī x = 45¬į

‚ąī ‚ą†BAC = ‚ą†BCA = 45¬į

‚ąī ‚ąÜABC is a 45¬į ‚Äď 45¬į ‚Äď 90¬į triangle.

‚ąī AB = BC = `1/sqrt(2) xx "AC"`¬† ¬†......[Side opposite to 45¬į]

= `1/sqrt(2) xx 2sqrt(2)`

‚ąī `l("AB")` = 2 units

items.question: From given figure, In ‚ąÜABC, AB ‚ä• BC, AB = BC, AC = `5sqrt(2)` , then what is the height of ‚ąÜABC?
items.solution_text: AB = BC ......[Given] ‚ąī ‚ą†A = ‚ą†C ......[Isosceles triangle theorem] Let ‚ą†A = ‚ą†C = x ......(i) In ‚ąÜABC, ‚ą†A + ‚ą†B + ‚ą†C = 180¬į ......[Sum of the measures of the angles of a triangle is 180¬į] ‚ąī x + 90¬į + x = 180¬į .......[From (i)] ‚ąī 2x = 90¬į ‚ąī x = `90^circ/2` .......[From (i)] ‚ąī x = 45¬į ‚ąī ‚ą†A = ‚ą†C = 45¬į ‚ąī ‚ąÜABC is a 45¬į ‚Äď 45¬į ‚Äď 90¬į triangle. ‚ąī AB = BC = `1/sqrt(2) xx "AC"` ......[Side opposite to 45¬į] = `1/sqrt(2) xx 5sqrt(2)` ‚ąī AB = BC = 5 units ‚ąī The height of ‚ąÜABC is 5 units.
items.solution_html:
AB = BC¬† ¬† ¬†......[Given]

‚ąī ‚ą†A = ‚ą†C¬† ¬† ¬†......[Isosceles triangle theorem]

Let ‚ą†A = ‚ą†C = x¬† ¬† ¬†......(i)

In ‚ąÜABC, ‚ą†A + ‚ą†B + ‚ą†C = 180¬į¬† ¬†......[Sum of the measures of the angles of a triangle is 180¬į]

‚ąī x + 90¬į + x = 180¬į¬† ¬† ¬†.......[From (i)]

‚ąī 2x = 90¬į

‚ąī x = `90^circ/2` ¬† ¬†.......[From (i)]

‚ąī x = 45¬į

‚ąī ‚ą†A = ‚ą†C = 45¬į

‚ąī ‚ąÜABC is a 45¬į ‚Äď 45¬į ‚Äď 90¬į triangle.

‚ąī AB = BC = `1/sqrt(2) xx "AC"`¬† ¬† ......[Side opposite to 45¬į]

= `1/sqrt(2) xx 5sqrt(2)`

‚ąī AB = BC = 5 units

‚ąī The height of ‚ąÜABC is 5 units.

items.title: From given figure, In ‚ąÜABC, If ‚ą†ABC = 90¬į ‚ą†CAB=30¬į, AC = 14 then for finding value of AB and BC, complete the following activity.Activity: In ‚ąÜABC, If ‚ą†ABC = 90¬į ‚ą†CAB=30¬į ‚ąī ‚ą†BCA = ‚Ė° By theorem of 30¬į - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: From given figure, In ‚ąÜABC, If ‚ą†ABC = 90¬į ‚ą†CAB=30¬į, AC = 14 then for finding value of AB and BC, complete the following activity. Activity: In ‚ąÜABC, If ‚ą†ABC = 90¬į, ‚ą†CAB=30¬į ‚ąī ‚ą†BCA = `square` By theorem of 30¬į ‚Äď 60¬į ‚Äď 90¬į triangle, ‚ąī `square = 1/2` AC and `square = sqrt(3)/2` AC ‚ąī BC = `1/2 xx square` and AB = `sqrt(3)/2 xx 14` ‚ąī BC = 7 and AB = `7sqrt(3)`
items.solution_text: In ‚ąÜABC, If ‚ą†ABC = 90¬į, ‚ą†CAB=30¬į ‚ąī ‚ą†BCA = 60¬į ......[Remaining angle of a triangle] By theorem of 30¬į ‚Äď 60¬į ‚Äď 90¬į triangle, ‚ąī BC = `1/2` AC .....[Side opposite to 30¬į] and AB = `sqrt(3)/2` AC .....[Side opposite to 60¬į] ‚ąī BC = `1/2 xx 14` and AB = `sqrt(3)/2 xx 14` ‚ąī BC = 7 and AB = `7sqrt(3)`
items.solution_html:
In ‚ąÜABC, If ‚ą†ABC = 90¬į, ‚ą†CAB=30¬į

‚ąī ‚ą†BCA = 60¬į¬† ¬† ¬†......[Remaining angle of a triangle]

By theorem of 30¬į ‚Äď 60¬į ‚Äď 90¬į triangle,

‚ąī¬†BC = `1/2` AC¬† ¬† ¬† .....[Side opposite to 30¬į]

and AB = `sqrt(3)/2` AC ¬† .....[Side opposite to 60¬į]

items.solution_text: In ‚ąÜPQR, if ‚ą†QPR = 90¬į, PM ‚ä• QR, ......[Given] In ‚ąÜPMQ, by Pythagoras Theorem, ‚ąī PM 2 + QM 2 = PQ 2 ......(I) ‚ąī PQ 2 = 10 2 + 8 2 ‚ąī PQ 2 = 100 + 64 ‚ąī PQ 2 = 164 ‚ąī PQ = `sqrt(164)` Here, ‚ąÜQPR ~ ‚ąÜQMP ~ ‚ąÜPMR ‚ąī ‚ąÜQMP ~ ‚ąÜPMR ‚ąī `"PM"/"RM" = "QM"/"PM"` ......[Corresponding sides of similar triangles] ‚ąī PM 2 = RM √ó QM ‚ąī 10 2 = RM √ó 8 RM = `100/8` = `25/2` And, QR = QM + MR QR = 8 + `25/2 = 41/2`

And,

items.question: From the figure given alongside, find the length of the median AD of triangle ABC. Complete the activity. Solution: Here A(‚Äď1, 1), B(5, ‚Äď 3), C(3, 5) and suppose D(x, y) are coordinates of point D. Using midpoint formula, x = `(5 + 3)/2` ‚ąī x = `square` y = `(-3 + 5)/2` ‚ąī y = `square` Using distance formula, ‚ąī AD = `sqrt((4 - square)^2 + (1 - 1)^2` ‚ąī AD = `sqrt((square)^2 + (0)^2` ‚ąī AD = `sqrt(square)` ‚ąī The length of median AD = `square`
items.solution_text: Here A(‚Äď1, 1), B(5, ‚Äď 3), C(3, 5) and suppose D(x, y) are coordinates of point D. D is the midpoint of seg BC. Using midpoint formula, x = `(x_1 + x_2)/2` x = `(5 + 3)/2` ‚ąī x = `8/2` ‚ąī x = 4 y = `(y_1 + y_2)/2` y = `(-3 + 5)/2` ‚ąī y = `2/2` ‚ąī y = 1 Using distance formula, ‚ąī AD = `sqrt((4 - (-1))^2 + (1 - 1)^2` ‚ąī AD = `sqrt((5)^2 + (0)^2` ‚ąī AD = `sqrt(25)` ‚ąī The length of median AD = 5 cm
items.solution_html:
Here A(‚Äď1, 1), B(5, ‚Äď 3), C(3, 5) and suppose D(x, y) are coordinates of point D. D is the midpoint of seg BC.

Using midpoint formula,

x = `(x_1 + x_2)/2`

x = `(5 + 3)/2`

‚ąī x = `8/2`

‚ąī x =¬†4

y = `(y_1 + y_2)/2`

y = `(-3 + 5)/2`

‚ąī y = `2/2`

‚ąī y =¬†1

Using distance formula,

‚ąī AD = `sqrt((4 - (-1))^2 + (1 - 1)^2`

‚ąī AD = `sqrt((5)^2 + (0)^2`

‚ąī AD = `sqrt(25)`

‚ąī The length of median AD = 5 cm

items.title: Given: A circle inscribed in a right angled őĒABC. If ‚ą†ACB = 90¬į and the radius of the circle is r. To prove: 2r = a + b ‚Äď c - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: Given: A circle inscribed in a right angled őĒABC. If ‚ą†ACB = 90¬į and the radius of the circle is r. To prove: 2r = a + b ‚Äď c
items.solution_text: Proof: In given figure, `{:("AF" = "AE"),("FB" = "BD"),("EC" = "DC"):}}` .....(i) [Tangent Segment theorem] In ‚ĖĘODCE, ‚ą†ECD = 90¬į ......[‚ą†ACB = 90¬į, A‚ÄďE‚ÄďC, B-D‚ÄďC] `{:(‚ą†"ODC" = 90^circ),(‚ą†"OEC" = 90^circ):}}` ......[Tangent theorem] ‚ąī ‚ą†EOD = 90¬į ‚Ä¶[Ramining angle of ‚ĖĘODCE] ‚ąī ‚ĖĘODCE is a rectangle. . Also, OE = OD = r ......[Radii of the same circle] ‚ąī ‚ĖĘODCE is a square ......`[("A Rectangle is square if it's"),("adjcent sides are congruent")]` ‚ąī OE = OD = CD = CE = r ......(ii) [sides of the square] Consider R.H.S. = a + b ‚Äď c = BC + AC ‚Äď AB = (BD + DC) + (AE + EC) ‚Äď (AF + FB) ......[B‚ÄďD‚ÄďC, A‚ÄďE‚ÄďC, A‚ÄďF‚ÄďB] = (FB + r) + (AF + r) ‚Äď (AF + FB) ......[From (i) and (ii)] = FB + r + AF + r ‚Äď AF - FB = 2r = L.H.S ‚ąī 2r = a + b ‚Äď c
items.solution_html:
Proof: In given figure,

`{:("AF" = "AE"),("FB" = "BD"),("EC" = "DC"):}}`¬† .....(i) [Tangent Segment theorem]

In ‚ĖĘODCE,

‚ą†ECD = 90¬į ¬† ¬†......[‚ą†ACB = 90¬į, A‚ÄďE‚ÄďC, B-D‚ÄďC]

`{:(‚ą†"ODC" = 90^circ),(‚ą†"OEC" = 90^circ):}}`¬† ......[Tangent theorem]

‚ąī ‚ą†EOD = 90¬į ‚Ä¶[Ramining angle of ‚ĖĘODCE]

‚ąī ‚ĖĘODCE is a rectangle.

. Also, OE = OD = r¬† ......[Radii of the same circle]

‚ąī ‚ĖĘODCE is a square¬† ¬† ¬†......`[("A Rectangle is square if it's"),("adjcent sides are congruent")]`

‚ąī OE = OD = CD = CE = r¬† ¬† ......(ii) [sides of the square]

Consider R.H.S. = a + b ‚Äď c

= BC + AC ‚Äď AB

= (BD + DC) + (AE + EC) ‚Äď (AF + FB) ......[B‚ÄďD‚ÄďC, A‚ÄďE‚ÄďC, A‚ÄďF‚ÄďB]

= (FB + r) + (AF + r) ‚Äď (AF + FB)¬† ¬†......[From (i) and (ii)]

= FB + r + AF + r ‚Äď AF - FB

= 2r

= L.H.S

‚ąī 2r = a + b ‚Äď c

items.title: If 3 sin őł = 4 cos őł, then sec őł = ? - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: If 3 sin őł = 4 cos őł, then sec őł = ?
items.solution_text: 3 sin őł = 4cos őł .....[Given] ‚ąī `(sintheta)/(costheta) = 4/3` ‚ąī tan őł = `4/3` We know that, 1 + tan 2 őł = sec 2 őł ‚ąī `1 + (4/3)^2` = sec 2 őł ‚ąī `1 + 16/9` = sec 2 őł ‚ąī sec 2 őł = `(9 + 16)/9` ‚ąī sec 2 őł = `25/9` ‚ąī sec őł = `5/3` ......[Taking square root of both sides]
items.solution_html:
3 sin őł = 4cos őł¬† ¬†.....[Given]

‚ąī `(sintheta)/(costheta) = 4/3`

‚ąī tan őł = `4/3`

We know that,

1 + tan2őł = sec2őł

‚ąī¬† `1 + (4/3)^2` = sec2őł

‚ąī `1 + 16/9`¬†= sec2őł

‚ąī¬†sec2őł = `(9 + 16)/9`

‚ąī sec2őł = `25/9`

‚ąī sec őł = `5/3`¬† ¬†......[Taking square root of both sides]

items.title: If 3 sin A + 5 cos A = 5, then show that 5 sin A ‚Äď 3 cos A = ¬Ī 3 - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: If 3 sin A + 5 cos A = 5, then show that 5 sin A ‚Äď 3 cos A = ¬Ī 3
items.solution_text: 3 sin A + 5 cos A = 5 ....[Given] ‚ąī (3 sin A + 5 cos A) 2 = 25 ......[Squaring both the sides] ‚ąī 9 sin 2 A + 30 sin A cos A + 25 cos 2 A = 25 ‚ąī 9(1 ‚Äď cos 2 A) + 30 sin A cos A + 25(1 ‚Äď sin 2 A) = 25 ‚ąī 9 ‚Äď 9 cos 2 A + 30 sin A cos A + 25 ‚Äď 25 sin 2 A = 25 ‚ąī 25 sin 2 A ‚Äď 30 sin A cos A + 9 cos 2 A = 9 ‚ąī (5 sin A ‚Äď 3 cos A) 2 = 9 ......[‚ąĶ a 2 ‚Äď 2ab + b 2 = (a ‚Äď b) 2 ] ‚ąī 5 sin A ‚Äď 3 cos A = ¬Ī 3 .....[Taking square root of both sides]
items.solution_html:
3 sin A + 5 cos A = 5¬† ¬†....[Given]

‚ąī (3 sin A + 5 cos A)2 = 25¬† ¬†......[Squaring both the sides]

‚ąī 9 sin2A + 30 sin A cos A + 25 cos2A = 25

‚ąī 9(1 ‚Äď cos2A) + 30 sin A cos A + 25(1 ‚Äď sin2A) = 25

‚ąī 9 ‚Äď 9 cos2A + 30 sin A cos A + 25 ‚Äď 25 sin2A = 25

‚ąī 25 sin2A ‚Äď 30 sin A cos A + 9 cos2A = 9

‚ąī (5 sin A ‚Äď 3 cos A)2 = 9¬† ¬†......[‚ąĶ a2 ‚Äď 2ab + b2 = (a ‚Äď b)2]

‚ąī 5 sin A ‚Äď 3 cos A = ¬Ī 3¬† ¬†.....[Taking square root of both sides]

items.title: If 5 sec őł ‚Äď 12 cosec őł = 0, then find values of sin őł, sec őł - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: If 5 sec őł ‚Äď 12 cosec őł = 0, then find values of sin őł, sec őł
items.solution_text: 5 sec őł ‚Äď 12 cosec őł = 0 ......[Given] ‚ąī 5 sec őł = 12 cosec őł ‚ąī `5/costheta = 12/sintheta` ......`[because sectheta = 1/costheta, "cosec" theta = 1/sintheta]` ‚ąī `sintheta/costheta = 12/5` ‚ąī tan őł = `12/5` We know that, 1 + tan 2 őł = sec 2 őł ‚ąī `1 + (12/5)^2` = sec 2 őł ‚ąī `1 + 144/25` = sec 2 őł ‚ąī `(25 + 144)/25` = sec 2 őł ‚ąī sec 2 őł = `169/25` ‚ąī secőł = `13/5` ......[Taking square root of both sides] Now, cos őł = `1/sectheta` = `1/((13/5))` ‚ąī cos őł = `5/13` We know that, sin 2 őł + cos 2 őł = 1 ‚ąī `sin^2theta + (5/13)^2` = 1 ‚ąī `sin^2theta + 25/169` = 1 ‚ąī sec 2 őł = `1 - 25/169` ‚ąī sec 2 őł = `(169 - 25)/169` ‚ąī sec 2 őł = `144/169` ‚ąī sin őł = `12/13` ......[Taking square root of both sides] ‚ąī sin őł = `12/13`, sec őł = `13/5`.
items.solution_html:
5 sec őł ‚Äď 12 cosec őł = 0¬† ¬† ......[Given]

‚ąī 5 sec őł = 12 cosec őł

‚ąī `5/costheta = 12/sintheta`¬† ¬† ......`[because sectheta = 1/costheta, "cosec"¬† theta = 1/sintheta]`

‚ąī `sintheta/costheta = 12/5`

‚ąī tan őł = `12/5`

We know that,

1 + tan2őł = sec2őł

‚ąī `1 + (12/5)^2` = sec2őł

‚ąī `1 + 144/25` = sec2őł

‚ąī `(25 + 144)/25` = sec2őł

‚ąī sec2őł = `169/25`

‚ąī secőł = `13/5`¬† ¬†......[Taking square root of both sides]

Now, cos őł = `1/sectheta`

= `1/((13/5))`

‚ąī cos őł = `5/13`

We know that,

sin2őł + cos2őł = 1

‚ąī `sin^2theta + (5/13)^2` = 1

‚ąī `sin^2theta + 25/169` = 1

‚ąī sec2őł = `1 - 25/169`

‚ąī sec2őł = `(169 - 25)/169`

‚ąī sec2őł = `144/169`

‚ąī sin őł = `12/13` ......[Taking square root of both sides]

‚ąī sin őł = `12/13`, sec¬†őł = `13/5`.

items.title: If a triangle having sides 50 cm, 14 cm and 48 cm, then state wheather given triangle is right angled triangle or not. - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: If a triangle having sides 50 cm, 14 cm and 48 cm, then state whether given triangle is right angled triangle or not
items.solution_text: The sides of the triangle are 50 cm, 14 cm and 48 cm. The longest side of the triangle is 50 cm. ‚ąī (50) 2 = 2500 Now, sum of the squares of the remaining sides is, (14) 2 + (48) 2 = 196 + 2304 = 2500 ‚ąī (50) 2 = (14) 2 + (48) 2 ‚ąī Square of the longest side is equal to the sum of the squares of the remaining two sides. ‚ąī The given sides will form a right angled triangle. .......[Converse of Pythagoras theorem]
items.solution_html:
The sides of the triangle are 50 cm, 14 cm and 48 cm.

The longest side of the triangle is 50 cm.

‚ąī (50)2 = 2500

Now, sum of the squares of the remaining sides is,

(14)2 + (48)2 = 196 + 2304 = 2500

‚ąī (50)2 = (14)2 + (48)2

‚ąī Square of the longest side is equal to the sum of the squares of the remaining two sides.

‚ąī The given sides will form a right angled triangle. .......[Converse of Pythagoras theorem]

items.title: If ‚ąÜABC ~ ‚ąÜPQR and AB : PQ = 3 : 4 then A(‚ąÜABC) : A(‚ąÜPQR) = ? - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: If ‚ąÜABC ~ ‚ąÜPQR and AB : PQ = 3 : 4 then A(‚ąÜABC) : A(‚ąÜPQR) = ?
items.solution_text: 9: 16 In ‚ąÜABC and ‚ąÜPQR, ‚ąÜABC ~ ‚ąÜPQR AB : PQ = 3 : 4 ...(Given) by theorem of areas of similar triangles, `"A(‚ąÜABC)"/"A(‚ąÜPQR)" = ("AB"^2)/("PQ"^2)` `"A(‚ąÜABC)"/"A(‚ąÜPQR)" = 3^2/4^2` `"A(‚ąÜABC)"/"A(‚ąÜPQR)" = 9/16`. ‚ąī A(‚ąÜABC) : A(‚ąÜPQR) = 9 : 16.
items.solution_html:
9: 16

In ‚ąÜABC and ‚ąÜPQR,

‚ąÜABC ~ ‚ąÜPQR

AB : PQ = 3 : 4¬† ¬† ...(Given)

by theorem of areas of similar triangles,

`"A(‚ąÜABC)"/"A(‚ąÜPQR)" = ("AB"^2)/("PQ"^2)`

`"A(‚ąÜABC)"/"A(‚ąÜPQR)" = 3^2/4^2`

`"A(‚ąÜABC)"/"A(‚ąÜPQR)" = 9/16`.

‚ąī A(‚ąÜABC) : ¬†A(‚ąÜPQR) = 9 : 16.

items.question: If cos őł = `24/25`, then sin őł = ?
items.solution_text: cos őł = `24/25` ......[Given] We know that, sin 2 őł + cos 2 őł = 1 ‚ąī `sin^2theta + (24/25)^2` = 1 ‚ąī `sin^2theta + 576/625` = 1 ‚ąī sin 2 őł = `1 - 576/625` ‚ąī sin 2 őł = `(625 - 576)/625` ‚ąī sin 2 őł = `49/625` ‚ąī sin őł = `7/25` ......[Taking square root of both sides]

‚ąī sin őł = `7/25`¬† ¬† ......[Taking square root of both sides]

items.title: If point P(1, 1) divide segment joining point A and point B(‚Äď1, ‚Äď1) in the ratio 5 : 2, then the coordinates of A are ______ - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: If point P(1, 1) divide segment joining point A and point B(‚Äď1, ‚Äď1) in the ratio 5 : 2, then the coordinates of A are ______
items.solution_text: (6, 6) Let A(x 1 , y 1 ) and B(x 2 , y 2 ) = B(-1, -1) P(x, y) = P(1, 1) divides AB in ratio 5 : 2. ‚ąī x 1 = 1, y 1 = 1, x 2 = ‚Äď1, y 2 = ‚Äď1, a = 5, b = 2. ‚ąī By section formula, ‚ąī x = `(ax_2 + bx_1)/(a + b)` ‚ąī `1 = (5(-1) + 2x_1)/(5 + 2)` ‚ąī 7 = -5 + 2x 1 ‚ąī 2x 1 = 7 + 5 ‚ąī 2x 1 = 12 ‚ąī x 1 = `12/2` ‚ąī x 1 = 6 ‚ąī Co-ordinates of A are (6, 6).
items.solution_html:
(6, 6)

Let A(x1, y1) and B(x2, y2) = B(-1, -1)

P(x, y) = P(1, 1) divides AB in ratio 5 : 2.

‚ąī x1 = 1, y1 = 1, x2 = ‚Äď1, y2 = ‚Äď1, a = 5, b = 2.

‚ąī By section formula,

‚ąī x = `(ax_2 + bx_1)/(a + b)`

‚ąī `1 = (5(-1) + 2x_1)/(5 + 2)`

‚ąī¬† 7 = -5 + 2x1

‚ąī 2x1 = 7 + 5

‚ąī 2x1 = 12

‚ąī x1 = `12/2`

‚ąī x1 = 6

‚ąī Co-ordinates of A are (6, 6).

items.title: If point P divides segment AB in the ratio 1 : 3 where A(‚Äď 5, 3) and B(3, ‚Äď 5), then the coordinates of P are ______ - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: If point P divides segment AB in the ratio 1 : 3 where A(‚Äď 5, 3) and B(3, ‚Äď 5), then the coordinates of P are ______
items.solution_html:
(‚Äď 3, 1)

Let A(x1, y1) = A(-5, 3) and B(x2, y2) = B(3, -5),

a : b = 1 : 3

‚ąī x1 = -5, y1 = 3, x2 = 3, y2 = ‚Äď5, a = 1, b = 3.

‚ąī By section formula,

‚ąī x = `(ax_2 + bx_1)/(a + b)`

‚ąī x = `(1(3) + 3(-5))/(1 + 3)`

‚ąī x = `(3 - 15)/(4)`

‚ąī x = `(- 12)/(4)`

‚ąī x = -3

‚ąī y = `(ay_2 + by_1)/(a + b)`

‚ąī y = `(1(-5) + 3(3))/(1 + 3)`

‚ąī y = `(-5 + 9)/(4)`

‚ąī y = `(4)/(4)`

‚ąī y = 1

‚ąī Co-ordinates of P are (-3, 1).

items.question: If point P is midpoint of segment joining point A(‚Äď 4, 2) and point B(6, 2), then the coordinates of P are ______
items.solution_html:
(1, 2)

A(x1, y1) = A( ‚Äď4, 2), B(x2, y2) = B(6, 2)

Here, x1 = ‚Äď 4, y1 = 2, x2 = 6, y2 = 2

‚ąī Co-ordinates of the midpoint of seg AB

= `((x_1 + x_2)/2, (y_1 + y_2)/2)`

= `((-4 + 6)/2, (2 + 2)/2)`

= `(2/2, 4/2)`

= (1, 2)

items.title: If sec őł = 4140, then find values of sin őł, cot őł, cosec őł - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: If sec őł = `41/40`, then find values of sin őł, cot őł, cosec őł
items.solution_text: sec őł = `41/40` ......[Given] ‚ąī cos őł = `1/sectheta = 1/(41/40)` ‚ąī cos őł = `40/41` We know that, sin 2 őł + cos 2 őł = 1 ‚ąī `sin^2theta + (40/41)^2` = 1 ‚ąī `sin^2theta + 1600/1681` = 1 ‚ąī sin 2 őł = `1 - 1600/1681` ‚ąī sin 2 őł = `(1681- 1600)/1681` ‚ąī sin 2 őł = `81/1681` ‚ąī sin őł = `9/41` .......[Taking square root of both sides] Now, cosec őł = `1/sintheta` = `1/((9/41))` = `41/9` cot őł = `costheta/sintheta` = `((40/41))/((9/41))` = `40/9` ‚ąī sin őł = `9/41`, cot őł = `40/9`, cosec őł = `41/9`
items.solution_html:
sec őł = `41/40`¬† ¬† ¬†......[Given]

‚ąī cos őł = `1/sectheta = 1/(41/40)`

‚ąī cos őł = `40/41`

We know that,

sin2őł + cos2őł = 1

‚ąī `sin^2theta + (40/41)^2` = 1

‚ąī `sin^2theta + 1600/1681` = 1

‚ąī sin2őł = `1 - 1600/1681`

‚ąī sin2őł = `(1681- 1600)/1681`

‚ąī sin2őł = `81/1681`

‚ąī sin őł = `9/41`¬† ¬†.......[Taking square root of both sides]

Now, cosec őł = `1/sintheta`

= `1/((9/41))`

= `41/9`

cot őł = `costheta/sintheta`

= `((40/41))/((9/41))`

= `40/9`

‚ąī sin őł = `9/41`, cot őł = `40/9`, cosec őł = `41/9`

items.title: If sin őł + cos őł = 3, then show that tan őł + cot őł = 1 - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: If sin őł + cos őł = `sqrt(3)`, then show that tan őł + cot őł = 1
items.solution_text: sin őł + cos őł = `sqrt(3)` ......[Given] ‚ąī (sin őł + cos őł) 2 = 3 ......[Squaring on both sides] ‚ąī sin 2 őł + 2sinőł cosőł + cos 2 őł = 3 ......[‚ąĶ (a + b) 2 = a 2 + 2ab + b 2 ] ‚ąī (sin 2 őł + cos 2 őł) + 2sinőł cosőł = 3 ‚ąī 1 + 2 sin őł cos őł = 3 ......[‚ąĶ sin 2 őł + cos 2 őł = 1] ‚ąī 2 sin őł cos őł = 2 ‚ąī sin őł cos őł = 1 ......(i) tan őł + cot őł = `sintheta/costheta + costheta/sintheta` = `(sin^2theta + cos^2theta)/(costhetasintheta)` = `1/(sintheta costheta)` ......[‚ąĶ sin 2 őł + cos 2 őł = 1] = `1/1` ......[From (i)] = 1
items.solution_html:
sin őł + cos őł = `sqrt(3)`¬† ¬† ¬†......[Given]

‚ąī (sin őł + cos őł)2 = 3¬† ¬† ......[Squaring on both sides]

‚ąī sin2őł + 2sinőł cosőł + cos2őł = 3¬† ......[‚ąĶ (a + b)2 = a2 + 2ab + b2]

‚ąī (sin2őł + cos2őł) + 2sinőł cosőł = 3

‚ąī 1 + 2 sin őł cos őł = 3¬† ¬†......[‚ąĶ sin2őł + cos2őł = 1]

‚ąī 2 sin őł cos őł = 2

‚ąī sin őł cos őł = 1¬† ¬†......(i)

tan őł + cot őł = `sintheta/costheta + costheta/sintheta`

= `(sin^2theta + cos^2theta)/(costhetasintheta)`

= `1/(sintheta costheta)`¬† ¬†......[‚ąĶ sin2őł + cos2őł = 1]

= `1/1`¬† ¬† ......[From (i)]

= 1

items.question: If tan őł = 1, then sin őł . cos őł = ?
items.solution_text: tan őł = 1 ......[Given] ‚ąī őł = 45¬į ......[‚ąĶ tan45¬į = 1] ‚ąī sin őł . cos őł = sin 45¬į cos 45¬į = `1/sqrt(2)*1/sqrt(2)` = `1/2`
items.solution_html:
tan őł = 1¬† ¬† ......[Given]

‚ąī őł = 45¬į¬† ¬† ¬†......[‚ąĶ tan45¬į = 1]

‚ąī sin őł . cos őł = sin 45¬į cos 45¬į

= `1/sqrt(2)*1/sqrt(2)`

= `1/2`

items.title: If tan őł = 724, then to find value of cos őł complete the activity given below. Activity: sec2őł = 1 + ‚Ė° ......[Fundamental tri. identity] sec2őł = 1 + ‚Ė°2 sec2őł = 1 + ‚Ė°576 sec2őł = ‚Ė°576 sec őł = ‚Ė° cos őł - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: If tan őł = `7/24`, then to find value of cos őł complete the activity given below. Activity: sec 2 őł = 1 + `square` ......[Fundamental tri. identity] sec 2 őł = 1 + `square^2` sec 2 őł = 1 + `square/576` sec 2 őł = `square/576` sec őł = `square` cos őł = `square` .......`[cos theta = 1/sectheta]`
items.solution_text: sec 2 őł = 1 + tan 2 őł ......[Fundamental tri. identity] ‚ąī sec 2 őł = 1 + `(7/24)^2` ‚ąī sec 2 őł = 1 + `49/576` ‚ąī sec 2 őł =`(576 + 49)/576` ‚ąī sec 2 őł = `625/576` ‚ąī sec őł = `25/24` ‚ąī cos őł = `24/25` .......`[cos theta = 1/sectheta]`
items.solution_html:
sec2őł = 1 + tan2őł ¬† ¬†......[Fundamental tri. identity]

‚ąī sec2őł = 1 + `(7/24)^2`

‚ąī sec2őł = 1 + `49/576`

‚ąī sec2őł =`(576 + 49)/576`

‚ąī sec2őł = `625/576`

‚ąī sec őł = `25/24`

‚ąī cos őł = `24/25` ¬† ¬† .......`[cos theta = 1/sectheta]`

items.question: If tan őł = `9/40`, complete the activity to find the value of sec őł. Activity: sec 2 őł = 1 + `square` ......[Fundamental trigonometric identity] sec 2 őł = 1 + `square^2` sec 2 őł = 1 + `square` sec őł = `square`
items.solution_text: sec 2 őł = 1 + tan 2 őł ......[Fundamental trigonometric identity] ‚ąī sec 2 őł = 1 + `(9/40)^2` ‚ąī sec 2 őł = 1 + `81/1600` ‚ąī sec 2 őł = `1681/1600` ‚ąī sec őł = `41/40`
items.solution_html:
sec2őł = 1 + tan2őł ¬† ¬† ......[Fundamental trigonometric identity]

‚ąī sec2őł = 1 + `(9/40)^2`

‚ąī sec2őł = 1 + `81/1600`¬†

‚ąī sec2őł = `1681/1600`

‚ąī sec őł = `41/40`

items.question: If tan őł √ó A = sin őł, then A = ?
items.solution_text: tan őł √ó A = sin őł .....[Given] ‚ąī `(sin theta)/(cos theta) xx "A"` = sin őł ‚ąī`1/(cos theta) xx "A"` = 1 ‚ąī A = cos őł

‚ąī`1/(cos theta) xx "A"` = 1

‚ąī A = cos őł

items.title: If tan őł + cot őł = 2, then tan2őł + cot2őł = ? - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: If tan őł + cot őł = 2, then tan 2 őł + cot 2 őł = ?
items.solution_text: tan őł + cot őł = 2 ....[Given] ‚ąī (tan őł + cot őł) 2 = 4 .....[Squaring both sides] ‚ąī tan 2 őł + 2tan őł.cot őł + cot 2 őł = 4 ......[‚ąĶ (a + b) 2 = a 2 + 2ab + b 2 ] ‚ąī tan 2 őł + 2(1) + cot 2 őł = 4 ......[‚ąĶ tan őł ‚čÖ cot őł = 1] ‚ąī tan 2 őł + cot 2 őł = 4 ‚Äď 2 ‚ąī tan 2 őł + cot 2 őł = 2
items.solution_html:
tan őł + cot őł = 2¬† ¬†....[Given]

‚ąī (tan őł + cot őł)2 = 4¬† ¬† .....[Squaring both sides]

‚ąī tan2őł + 2tan őł.cot őł + cot2őł = 4¬† ¬†......[‚ąĶ (a + b)2 = a2 + 2ab + b2]

‚ąī tan2őł + 2(1) + cot2őł = 4¬† ¬† ......[‚ąĶ tan őł ‚čÖ cot őł = 1]

‚ąī tan2őł + cot2őł = 4 ‚Äď 2

‚ąī tan2őł + cot2őł = 2

items.title: If tan őł ‚Äď sin2őł = cos2őł, then show that sin2 őł = 12 - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: If tan őł ‚Äď sin 2 őł = cos 2 őł, then show that sin 2 őł = `1/2`.
items.solution_text: tan őł ‚Äď sin 2 őł = cos 2 őł ......[Given] ‚ąī tan őł = sin 2 őł + cos 2 őł ‚ąī tan őł = 1 ....[‚ąĶ sin 2 őł + cos 2 őł = 1] But, tan 45¬į = 1 ‚ąī tan őł = tan 45¬į ‚ąī őł = 45¬į sin 2 őł = sin 2 45¬į = `(1/sqrt(2))^2` = `1/2`
items.solution_html:
tan őł ‚Äď sin2őł = cos2őł¬† ¬†......[Given]

‚ąī tan őł = sin2őł + cos2őł

‚ąī tan őł = 1¬† ¬† ....[‚ąĶ sin2őł + cos2őł = 1]

But, tan 45¬į = 1

‚ąī tan őł = tan 45¬į

‚ąī őł = 45¬į

sin2őł = sin245¬į

= `(1/sqrt(2))^2`

= `1/2`

items.title: If the distance between point L(x, 7) and point M(1, 15) is 10, then find the value of x - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: If the distance between point L(x, 7) and point M(1, 15) is 10, then find the value of x
items.solution_text: Let L(x 1 , y 1 ) = L(x, 7) and M (x 2 , y 2 ) = M(1, 15) x 1 = x, y 1 = 7, x 2 = 1, y 2 = 15 By distance formula, d(L, M) = `sqrt((x_2 - x_1)^2 + (y_2 - y_1)^2` ‚ąī d(L, M) = `sqrt((1 - x)^2 + (15 - 7)^2` ‚ąī 10 = `sqrt((1 - x)^2 + 8^2` ‚ąī 100 = (1 ‚Äď x) 2 + 64 ......[Squaring both sides] ‚ąī (1 ‚Äď x) 2 = 100 ‚Äď 64 ‚ąī (1 ‚Äď x) 2 = 36 ‚ąī 1 ‚Äď x = `+- sqrt(36)` .....[Taking square root of both sides] ‚ąī 1 ‚Äď x = ¬Ī 6 ‚ąī 1 ‚Äď x = 6 or 1 ‚Äď x = ‚Äď 6 ‚ąī x = ‚Äď 5 or x = 7 ‚ąī The value of x is ‚Äď 5 or 7.
items.solution_html:
Let L(x1, y1) = L(x, 7) and M (x2, y2) = M(1, 15)

x1 = x, y1 = 7, x2 = 1, y2 = 15

By distance formula,

d(L, M) = `sqrt((x_2 - x_1)^2 + (y_2 - y_1)^2`

‚ąī d(L, M) = `sqrt((1 - x)^2 + (15 - 7)^2`

‚ąī 10 = `sqrt((1 - x)^2 + 8^2`

‚ąī 100 = (1 ‚Äď x)2 + 64¬† ¬† ......[Squaring both sides]

‚ąī (1 ‚Äď x)2 = 100 ‚Äď 64

‚ąī (1 ‚Äď x)2 = 36

‚ąī 1 ‚Äď x = `+-¬† sqrt(36)`¬† .....[Taking square root of both sides]

‚ąī 1 ‚Äď x = ¬Ī 6

‚ąī 1 ‚Äď x = 6 or 1 ‚Äď x = ‚Äď 6

‚ąī x = ‚Äď 5 or x = 7

‚ąī The value of x is ‚Äď 5 or 7.

items.title: If the length of the segment joining point L(x, 7) and point M(1, 15) is 10 cm, then the value of x is ______ - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: If the length of the segment joining point L(x, 7) and point M(1, 15) is 10 cm, then the value of x is ______
items.solution_text: 7 or ‚Äď 5 Here, x 1 = x, y 1 = 7, x 2 = 1, y 2 = 15 By distance formula, d(L, M) = `sqrt((x_2 - x_1)^2 + (y_2 - y_1)^2)` ‚ąī d(L, M) = `sqrt((1 - x)^2 + (15 - 7)^2)` ‚ąī 10 = `sqrt((1 - x)^2 + 8^2)` ‚ąī 100 = (1 - x) 2 + 64 ...[Squaring both sides] ‚ąī (1 - x) 2 = 100 - 64 ‚ąī (1 - x) 2 = 36 ‚ąī 1 - x = `+-sqrt(36)` ...[Taking square root of both sides] ‚ąī 1 - x = `+-6` ‚ąī 1 - x = 6 or 1 - x = -6 ‚ąī x = -5 or x = 7 ‚ąī The value of x is -5 or 7.
items.solution_html:
7 or ‚Äď 5

Here, x1 = x, y1 = 7, x2 = 1, y2 = 15

By distance formula,

d(L, M) = `sqrt((x_2 - x_1)^2 + (y_2 - y_1)^2)`

‚ąī d(L, M) = `sqrt((1 - x)^2 + (15 - 7)^2)`

‚ąī 10 = `sqrt((1 - x)^2 + 8^2)`

‚ąī 100 = (1 - x)2 + 64¬† ¬† ¬† ...[Squaring both sides]

‚ąī (1 - x)2 = 100 - 64

‚ąī (1 - x)2 = 36

‚ąī 1 - x = `+-sqrt(36)`¬† ...[Taking square root of both sides]

‚ąī 1 - x = `+-6`

‚ąī 1 - x = 6 or 1 - x = -6

‚ąī x = -5 or x = 7

‚ąī The value of x is -5 or 7.

items.title: If the point P (6, 7) divides the segment joining A(8, 9) and B(1, 2) in some ratio, find that ratio Solution: Point P divides segment AB in the ratio m : n. A(8, 9) = (x1, y1), B(1 - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: If the point P (6, 7) divides the segment joining A(8, 9) and B(1, 2) in some ratio, find that ratio Solution: Point P divides segment AB in the ratio m: n. A(8, 9) = (x 1 , y 1 ), B(1, 2 ) = (x 2 , y 2 ) and P(6, 7) = (x, y) Using Section formula of internal division, ‚ąī 7 = `("m"(square) - "n"(9))/("m" + "n")` ‚ąī 7m + 7n = `square` + 9n ‚ąī 7m ‚Äď `square` = 9n ‚Äď `square` ‚ąī `square` = 2n ‚ąī `"m"/"n" = square`
items.solution_text: Point P divides segment AB in the ratio m : n. A(8, 9) = (x 1 , y 1 ), B(1, 2 ) = (x 2 , y 2 ) and P(6, 7) = (x, y) Using Section formula of internal division, y = `("m"y_2 + "n"y_1)/("m" + "n")` ‚ąī 7 = `("m"(2) - "n"(9))/("m" + "n")` ‚ąī 7m + 7n = 2m + 9n ‚ąī 7m ‚Äď 2m = 9n ‚Äď 7n ‚ąī 5m = 2n ‚ąī `"m"/"n"` = `bb(2/5)`
items.solution_html:
Point P divides segment AB in the ratio m : n.

A(8, 9) = (x1, y1), B(1, 2 ) = (x2, y2) and P(6, 7) = (x, y)

Using Section formula of internal division,

y = `("m"y_2 + "n"y_1)/("m" + "n")`

‚ąī 7 = `("m"(2) - "n"(9))/("m" + "n")`

‚ąī 7m + 7n = 2m + 9n

‚ąī 7m ‚Äď 2m = 9n ‚Äď 7n

‚ąī 5m = 2n

‚ąī `"m"/"n"` = `bb(2/5)`

items.title: If the sum of X-coordinates of the vertices of a triangle is 12 and the sum of Y-coordinates is 9, then the coordinates of centroid are - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: If the sum of X-coordinates of the vertices of a triangle is 12 and the sum of Y-coordinates is 9, then the coordinates of centroid are ______
items.solution_text: (4, 3) Let P(x 1 , y 1 ), Q(x 2 , y 2 ), R(x 3 , y 3 ) be the co-ordinates of the vertices of a triangle. Given that x 1 + x 2 + x 3 = 12 and y 1 + y 2 + y 3 = 9 ‚ąī Co-ordinates of the centroid S(x, y) are `x = (x_1 + x_2 + x_3)/3 "and" y = (y_1 + y_2 + y_3)/3` `x = (12)/3 "and" y = (9)/3` `x = 4 "and" y = 3` ‚ąī S(x, y) = (4, 3).
items.solution_html:
(4, 3)

Let P(x1, y1), Q(x2, y2), R(x3, y3) be the co-ordinates of the vertices of a triangle.

Given that x1 + x2 + x3 = 12 and y1 + y2 + y3 = 9

‚ąī Co-ordinates of the centroid S(x, y) are

`x = (x_1 + x_2 + x_3)/3¬† "and"¬† y = (y_1 + y_2 + y_3)/3`

`x = (12)/3¬† "and"¬† y = (9)/3`

`x = 4¬† "and"¬† y = 3`

‚ąī S(x, y) = (4, 3).

items.title: If ‚ąÜXYZ ~ ‚ąÜPQR and A(‚ąÜXYZ) = 25 cm2, A(‚ąÜPQR) = 4 cm2 then XY : PQ = ? - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: If ‚ąÜXYZ ~ ‚ąÜPQR and A(‚ąÜXYZ) = 25 cm 2 , A(‚ąÜPQR) = 4 cm 2 then XY : PQ = ?
items.solution_text: 5: 2 In ‚ąÜXYZ and ‚ąÜPQR, ‚ąÜXYZ ~ ‚ąÜPQR A(‚ąÜXYZ) = 25 cm 2 , A(‚ąÜPQR) = 4 cm 2 ...(Given) by the theorem of areas of similar triangles, `"A(‚ąÜXYZ)"/"A(‚ąÜPQR)" = "XY"^2/"PQ"^2` ‚ąī `25/4 = "XY"^2/"PQ"^2` ‚ąī `"XY"/"PQ" = 5/2` ‚ąī XY : PQ = 5 : 2.
items.solution_html:
5: 2

In ‚ąÜXYZ and ‚ąÜPQR,

‚ąÜXYZ ~ ‚ąÜPQR¬†¬†

A(‚ąÜXYZ) = 25 cm2, A(‚ąÜPQR) = 4 cm2¬†¬† ...(Given)

by the theorem of areas of similar triangles,

`"A(‚ąÜXYZ)"/"A(‚ąÜPQR)" = "XY"^2/"PQ"^2`

‚ąī `25/4 = "XY"^2/"PQ"^2`

‚ąī `"XY"/"PQ" = 5/2`

‚ąī XY : PQ = 5 : 2.

items.title: In a cyclic ‚ĖĘABCD, twice the measure of ‚ą†A is thrice the measure of ‚ą†C. Find the measure of ‚ą†C? - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: In a cyclic ‚ĖĘABCD, twice the measure of ‚ą†A is thrice the measure of ‚ą†C. Find the measure of ‚ą†C?
items.solution_text: 72¬ļ Explanation: ABCD is a cyclic quadrilateral. 2‚ą†A = 3‚ą†C ...(1) (Given) Now, ‚ą†A + ‚ą†C = 180¬ļ ....(Opposite angles of a cyclic quadrilateral are supplementary) ‚áí `3/2` ‚ą†C + ‚ą†C = 180¬ļ ...[From (1)] ‚Äč‚áí `5/2`‚ą†C = 180¬ļ ‚áí ‚ą†C = `(2 xx 180¬ļ)/5` ‚áí ‚ą†C = 72¬ļ Thus, the measure of ‚ą†C is 72¬ļ.
items.solution_html:
72¬ļ

Explanation:

ABCD is a cyclic quadrilateral.

2‚ą†A = 3‚ą†C ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬†...(1) (Given)

Now,

‚ą†A + ‚ą†C = 180¬ļ¬† ¬† ¬†....(Opposite angles of a cyclic quadrilateral are supplementary)

‚áí `3/2` ‚ą†C + ‚ą†C = 180¬ļ¬† ¬† ¬†...[From (1)]

‚Äč‚áí `5/2`‚ą†C = 180¬ļ

‚áí ‚ą†C = `(2 xx 180¬ļ)/5`

‚áí ‚ą†C = 72¬ļ

Thus, the measure of ‚ą†C is 72¬ļ.

In őĒCPA and őĒCQB ‚ą†CPA ‚ČÖ [‚ą† ______] ...[each 90¬į] ‚ą†ACP ‚ČÖ [‚ą† ______] ...[common angle] őĒCPA ~ őĒCQB ......[______ similarity test] `"AP"/"BQ" = (["______"])/"BC"` .......[corresponding sides of similar triangles] `7/8 = (["______"])/12` AC √ó [______] = 7 √ó 12 AC = 10.5

items.solution_html:
In őĒCPA and őĒCQB

‚ą†CPA ‚ČÖ ‚ą†CQB¬† ¬† ...[each 90¬į]

‚ą†ACP ‚ČÖ ‚ą†BCQ¬†¬† ...[common angle]

őĒCPA ~ őĒCQB¬† ¬† ¬†...[AA similarity test]

`"AP"/"BQ"` = `"AC"/"BC"`¬† ¬†...[corresponding sides of similar triangles]

`7/8` = `"AC"/12`

AC √ó¬†8 = 7 √ó 12

AC = `(7 xx 12)/8`

AC = `(7 xx 3)/2`

AC = `21/2`

AC = 10.5.

items.question: In figure, M is the centre of the circle and seg KL is a tangent segment. If MK = 12, KL = `6sqrt(3)`, then find (i) Radius of the circle. (ii) Measures of ‚ą†K and ‚ą†M.
items.solution_text: (i) Line KL is the tangent to the circle at point L and seg ML is the radius. ......[Given] ‚ąī ‚ą†MLK = 90¬į ......(i) [Tangent theorem] In ‚ąÜMLK, ‚ą†MLK = 90¬į ‚ąī MK 2 = ML 2 + KL 2 .....[Pythagoras theorem] ‚ąī 12 2 = ML 2 + `(6sqrt(3))^2` ‚ąī 144 = ML 2 + 108 ‚ąī ML 2 = 144 ‚ąí 108 ‚ąī ML 2 = 36 ‚ąī ML = `sqrt(36)` ‚ąī ML = 6 units ......[Taking square root of both sides] ‚ąī Radius of the circle is 6 units. (ii) We know that, ML = `1/2` MK, ‚ąī ‚ą†K = 30¬į .....(ii) [Converse of 30¬į‚ąí60¬į‚ąí90¬į theorem] In ‚ąÜMLK, ‚ą†L = 90¬į .....[From (i)] ‚ą†K = 30¬į .....[From (ii)] ‚ąī ‚ą†M = 60¬į ......[Remaining angle of ‚ąÜMLK]
items.solution_html:
(i)

Line KL is the tangent to the circle at point L and seg ML is the radius.¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬†......[Given]

‚ąī ‚ą†MLK = 90¬į¬† ¬† ¬† ......(i) [Tangent theorem]

In ‚ąÜMLK,

‚ą†MLK = 90¬į

‚ąī MK2 = ML2 + KL2 ¬† ¬†.....[Pythagoras theorem]

‚ąī 122 = ML2 + `(6sqrt(3))^2`

‚ąī 144 = ML2 + 108

‚ąī ML2 = 144 ‚ąí 108

‚ąī ML2 = 36

‚ąī ML = `sqrt(36)`

‚ąī ML = 6 units¬† ¬† ¬†......[Taking square root of both sides]

‚ąī Radius of the circle is 6 units.

(ii)

We know that,

ML = `1/2` MK,

‚ąī ‚ą†K = 30¬į¬† ¬† ¬†.....(ii) [Converse of 30¬į‚ąí60¬į‚ąí90¬į theorem]

In ‚ąÜMLK,

‚ą†L = 90¬į¬† ¬† ¬†.....[From (i)]

‚ą†K = 30¬į ¬† ¬†.....[From (ii)]

‚ąī ‚ą†M = 60¬į¬† ¬† ......[Remaining angle of ‚ąÜMLK]

m(arc DE) = 360¬į ‚Äď 270¬į¬† ...[From (i) and given]

‚ąī m(arc DE) = 90¬į¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬†...(ii)

‚ąī m(arc DEF) = m(arc DE) + m(arc EF)

= 90¬į + 70¬į¬† ¬† ¬† ¬†...[From (i) and (ii)]

‚ąī m(arc DEF) = 160¬į¬†

items.question: In the adjoining figure, the circle with center D touches the sides of ‚ą†ACB at A and B. If ‚ą†ACB = 52¬į, find the measure of ‚ą†ADB.

ADBC is a quadrilateral. ‚ąī ‚ą†ACB + ‚ą†CAD + ‚ą†CBD + ‚ą†ADB = 360¬į ......[The sum of all angles of a quadrilateral is 360¬į.] ‚ąī 52¬į + 90¬į + 90¬į + ‚ą†ADB = 360¬į ......[Tangent theorem] ‚ąī ‚ą†ADB + 232¬į = 360¬į ‚ąī ‚ą†ADB = 360¬į ‚Äď 232¬į ‚ąī ‚ą†ADB = 128¬į
items.solution_html:

‚ąī ‚ą†ADB + 232¬į = 360¬į

‚ąī ‚ą†ADB = 360¬į ‚Äď 232¬į

‚ąī ‚ą†ADB = 128¬į

items.title: In the Adjoining Figure Circles with Centres X and Y Touch Each Other at Point Z. a Secant Passing Through Z Intersects the Circles at Points a and B Respectively. Prove That, Radius Xa || Radius Yb. - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: In the adjoining figure circles with centres X and Y touch each other at point Z. A secant passing through Z intersects the circles at points A and B respectively. Prove that, radius XA || radius YB. Fill in the blanks and complete the proof. Construction: Draw segments XZ and YZ. Proof : By theorem of touching circles, points X, Z, Y are `square`. ‚ąī ‚ą†XZA ‚ČÖ `square` ...(opposite angles) Let ‚ą†XZA = ‚ą†BZY = a ...(I) Now, seg XA ‚ČÖ seg XZ ...[Radii of the same circle] ‚ąī‚ą†XAZ = `square` = a ...[isosceles triangle theorem](II) Similarly, seg YB ‚ČÖ seg YZ ...[Radii of the same circle] ‚ąī‚ą†BZY = `square` = a ...[isosceles triangle theorem ] (III) ‚ąī from (I), (II), (III), ‚ą†XAZ = `square` ‚ąī radius XA || radius YZ ...[`square`]
items.solution_text: Construction : Draw segments XZ and YZ. Proof : By theorem of touching circles, points X, Z, Y are collinear . ‚ąī ‚ą†XZA ‚ČÖ ‚ą†BZY ...(opposite angles) Let ‚ą†XZA = ‚ą†BZY = a .....(I) Now, seg XA ‚ČÖ seg XZ ...[Radii of the same circle] ‚ąī‚ą†XAZ = ‚ą†XZA = a ....[isosceles triangle theorem](II) Similarly, seg YB ‚ČÖ seg YZ ....[Radii of the same circle] ‚ąī‚ą†BZY = ‚ą†ZBY = a ...[isosceles triangle theorem](III) ‚ąī from (I), (II), (III), ‚ą†XAZ = ‚ą†ZBY ‚ąī radius XA || radius YB ...[Alternate angle test]
items.solution_html:
Construction: Draw segments XZ and¬†YZ.
Proof:

By theorem of touching circles, points X, Z, Y are¬†collinear.
‚ąī¬†‚ą†XZA¬†‚ČÖ¬†‚ą†BZY¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬†...(opposite angles)
Let ‚ą†XZA = ‚ą†BZY = a¬† ¬† ¬† ¬†.....(I)
Now, seg XA ‚ČÖ seg XZ ¬† ¬† ¬†...[Radii of the same circle]
‚ąī‚ą†XAZ =¬†‚ą†XZA = a¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬†....[isosceles triangle theorem](II)
Similarly, seg YB ‚ČÖ seg YZ ....[Radii of the same circle]
‚ąī‚ą†BZY =¬†‚ą†ZBY = a¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬†...[isosceles triangle theorem](III)
‚ąī from (I), (II), (III),¬†
‚ą†XAZ = ‚ą†ZBY
‚ąī radius XA || radius YB¬† ¬† ...[Alternate angle test]

items.title: In the figure, a circle touches all the sides of quadrilateral ABCD from the inside. The center of the circle is O. If AD‚ä• DC and BC = 38, QB = 27, DC = 25, then find the radius of the circle. - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: In the figure, a circle touches all the sides of quadrilateral ABCD from the inside. The center of the circle is O. If AD‚ä• DC and BC = 38, QB = 27, DC = 25, then find the radius of the circle.
items.solution_text: Given: AD ‚ä• DC BC = 38, QB = 27, DC = 25 To find: Radius of the circle, i.e., OP. Solution: BC = 38 ......[Given] ‚ąī BQ + QC = 38 ......[B‚ÄďQ‚ÄďC] ‚ąī 27 + QC = 38 .......[Given] ‚ąī QC = 38 ‚Äď 27 ‚ąī QC = 11 units ......(i) Now, QC = SC ......[Tangent segment theorem] ‚ąī SC = 11 units .....(ii) [From (i)] DC = 25 .......[Given] ‚ąī DS + SC = 25 ......[D‚ÄďS‚ÄďC] ‚ąī DS + 11 = 25 ......[From (ii)] ‚ąī DS = 25 ‚Äď 11 ‚ąī DS = 14 units ......(iii) In ‚ĖĘDSOP, ‚ą†P = ‚ą†S = 90¬į ......[Tangent theorem] ‚ą†D = 90¬į ......[Given] ‚ąī ‚ą†O = 90¬į ......[Remaning angle of ‚ĖĘDSOP] ‚ąī ‚ĖĘDSOP is a rectangle. Also, OP = OS ......[Radii of the same circle] ‚ąī ‚ĖĘDSOP is a square .......`[("A rectangle is square if its"),("adjacent sides are congruent")]` ‚ąī OS = DS = DP = PO .....(iv) [Sides of the square] ‚ąī OP = 14 units ......[From (iii) and (iv)] ‚ąī The radius of the circle is 14 units.
items.solution_html:
Given: AD ‚ä• DC

BC = 38, QB = 27, DC = 25

To find: Radius of the circle, i.e., OP.

Solution:

BC = 38¬† ¬† ¬† ......[Given]

‚ąī BQ + QC = 38¬† ¬† ......[B‚ÄďQ‚ÄďC]

‚ąī 27 + QC = 38¬† ¬† .......[Given]

‚ąī QC = 38 ‚Äď 27

‚ąī QC = 11 units¬† ¬† ¬† ......(i)

Now, QC = SC¬† ¬† ......[Tangent segment theorem]

‚ąī SC = 11 units¬† ¬† .....(ii) [From (i)]

DC = 25 ¬† ¬† .......[Given]

‚ąī DS + SC = 25¬† ¬† ......[D‚ÄďS‚ÄďC]

‚ąī DS + 11 = 25¬† ¬†......[From (ii)]

‚ąī DS = 25 ‚Äď 11

‚ąī DS = 14 units¬† ¬†......(iii)

In ‚ĖĘDSOP,

‚ą†P = ‚ą†S = 90¬į¬† ¬† ......[Tangent theorem]

‚ą†D = 90¬į ¬† ¬†......[Given]

‚ąī ‚ą†O = 90¬į¬† ¬† ......[Remaning angle of ‚ĖĘDSOP]

‚ąī ‚ĖĘDSOP is a rectangle.

Also, OP = OS¬† ¬† ......[Radii of the same circle]

‚ąī ‚ĖĘDSOP is a square¬† ¬† .......`[("A rectangle is square if its"),("adjacent sides are congruent")]`

‚ąī OS = DS = DP =¬† PO¬† ¬† .....(iv) [Sides of the square]

‚ąī OP = 14 units¬† ¬† ......[From (iii) and (iv)]

‚ąī The radius of the circle is 14 units.

items.title: In the figure, a circle with center P touches the semicircle at points Q and C having center O. If diameter AB = 10, AC = 6, then find the radius x of the smaller circle. - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: In the figure, a circle with center P touches the semicircle at points Q and C having center O. If diameter AB = 10, AC = 6, then find the radius x of the smaller circle.
items.solution_text: Given: AB = 10 units, AC = 6 units, PC = PQ = x unit. To find: x Solution: Diameter AB = 10 ...[Given] ‚ąī Radius = `1/2 xx "AB" = 1/2 xx 10` = 5. ‚ąī OQ = AO = OB = 5 ......(i) AC = AO + OC .....[A‚ÄďO‚ÄďC] ‚ąī OC = AC ‚Äď AO ‚ąī OC = 6 ‚Äď 5 ......[Given and (i)] ‚ąī OC = 1 ......(ii) OQ = OP + PQ ......[O‚ÄďP‚ÄďQ] ‚ąī OP = OQ ‚Äď PQ = 5 ‚Äď x ......(iii) [From (i) and given] Note that seg AB is tangent to the given smaller circle at point C. ‚ąī ‚ą†PCO = 90¬į ......[Tangent theorem] ‚ąī In ‚ąÜPCO, ‚ą†PCO = 90¬į ‚ąī OP 2 = PC 2 + OC 2 ......[Pythagoras theorem] ‚ąī (5 ‚Äď x) 2 = x 2 + (1) 2 ......[From (ii) and (iii)] ‚ąī 25 ‚Äď 10x + x 2 = x 2 + 1 ‚ąī 10x = 24 ‚ąī x = `24/10` = 2.4 ‚ąī The radius x of the smaller circle is 2.4 units
items.solution_html:
Given: AB = 10 units, AC = 6 units, PC = PQ = x unit.

To find: x

Solution:

Diameter AB = 10¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ...[Given]

‚ąī Radius = `1/2 xx "AB"¬† = 1/2 xx 10` = 5.

‚ąī OQ = AO = OB = 5¬† ¬† ......(i)

AC = AO + OC¬† ¬† .....[A‚ÄďO‚ÄďC]

‚ąī OC = AC ‚Äď AO

‚ąī OC = 6 ‚Äď 5¬† ¬† ¬†......[Given and (i)]

‚ąī OC = 1¬† ¬† ¬†......(ii)

OQ = OP + PQ¬† ¬† ......[O‚ÄďP‚ÄďQ]

‚ąī OP = OQ ‚Äď PQ

= 5 ‚Äď x¬† ¬† ¬† ......(iii) [From (i) and given]

Note that seg AB is tangent to the given smaller circle at point C.

‚ąī ‚ą†PCO = 90¬į¬† ¬† ¬†......[Tangent theorem]

‚ąī In ‚ąÜPCO,

‚ą†PCO = 90¬į

‚ąī OP2 = PC2 + OC2 ¬† ¬†......[Pythagoras theorem]

‚ąī (5 ‚Äď x)2 = x2 + (1)2 ¬† ......[From (ii) and (iii)]

‚ąī 25 ‚Äď 10x + x2 = x2 + 1

‚ąī 10x = 24

‚ąī x = `24/10` = 2.4

‚ąī The radius x of the smaller circle is 2.4 units

items.title: In the figure, őĒABC is an equilateral triangle. The angle bisector of ‚ą†B will intersect the circumcircle őĒABC at point P. Then prove that: CQ = CA. - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: In the figure, őĒABC is an equilateral triangle. The angle bisector of ‚ą†B will intersect the circumcircle őĒABC at point P. Then prove that: CQ = CA.
items.solution_text: ‚ąÜABC is an equilateral triangle. ‚ąī ‚ą†ABC = ‚ą†ACB = ‚ą†BAC = 60¬į ......(i) [Angles of an equilateral triangle] ‚ą†CBP = `1/2` ‚ą†ABC ......[Ray BP bisects ‚ą†B] ‚ąī ‚ą†CBP = `1/2 xx 60^circ` ......[From (i)] ‚ąī ‚ą†CBP = 30¬į ‚ą†CBP = ‚ą†CAP = 30¬į ......[Angles inscribed in the same arc] ‚ąī ‚ą†CAQ = 30¬į .....(ii) [A‚ąíP‚ąíQ] In ‚ąÜABQ, ‚ą†BAQ = ‚ą†BAC + ‚ą†CAQ .....[Angle addition property] ‚ąī ‚ą†BAQ = 60¬į + 30¬į .....[From (i) and (ii)] ‚ąī ‚ą†BAQ = 90¬į Also, ‚ą†ABQ = 60¬į ......[From (i) and B‚ąíC‚ąíQ] ‚ąī ‚ą†BQA = 30¬į .....[Remaining angle of ‚ąÜABQ] ‚ąī ‚ą†CQA = 30¬į ......(iii) [B‚ąíC‚ąíQ] In ‚ąÜCQA, ‚ą†CAQ = ‚ą†CQA .......[From (ii) and (iii)] ‚ąī CQ = CA ......[Converse of isosceles triangle theorem]
items.solution_html:
‚ąÜABC is an equilateral triangle.

‚ąī ‚ą†ABC = ‚ą†ACB = ‚ą†BAC = 60¬į¬† ¬†......(i) [Angles of an equilateral triangle]

‚ą†CBP = `1/2` ‚ą†ABC¬† ¬† ¬† ......[Ray BP bisects ‚ą†B]

‚ąī ‚ą†CBP = `1/2 xx 60^circ`¬† ¬†......[From (i)]

‚ąī ‚ą†CBP = 30¬į

‚ą†CBP = ‚ą†CAP = 30¬į¬† ¬† ......[Angles inscribed in the same arc]

‚ąī ‚ą†CAQ = 30¬į¬† ¬† .....(ii) [A‚ąíP‚ąíQ]

In ‚ąÜABQ,

‚ą†BAQ = ‚ą†BAC + ‚ą†CAQ¬† ¬†.....[Angle addition property]

‚ąī ‚ą†BAQ = 60¬į + 30¬į¬† ¬†.....[From (i) and (ii)]

‚ąī ‚ą†BAQ = 90¬į

Also, ‚ą†ABQ = 60¬į¬† ......[From (i) and B‚ąíC‚ąíQ]

‚ąī ‚ą†BQA = 30¬į¬† ¬† .....[Remaining angle of ‚ąÜABQ]

‚ąī ‚ą†CQA = 30¬į¬† ¬†......(iii) [B‚ąíC‚ąíQ]

In ‚ąÜCQA,

‚ą†CAQ = ‚ą†CQA¬† ¬† .......[From (ii) and (iii)]

‚ąī CQ = CA¬† ¬† ......[Converse of isosceles triangle theorem]

items.title: In the figure, ‚ĖĘABCD is a cyclic quadrilateral. If m(arc ABC) = 230¬į, then find ‚ą†ABC, ‚ą†CDA, ‚ą†CBE. - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: In the figure, ‚ĖĘABCD is a cyclic quadrilateral. If m(arc ABC) = 230¬į, then find ‚ą†ABC, ‚ą†CDA, ‚ą†CBE.
items.solution_text: m(arc ABC) = 230¬į .....(i) [Given] ‚ąī m(arc ADC) + m(arc ABC) = 360¬į .......[Degree measure of a circle is 360¬į] ‚ąī m(arc ADC) = 360¬į ‚Äď m(arc ABC) ‚ąī m(arc ADC) = 360¬į ‚Äď 230¬į .......[From (i)] ‚ąī m(arc ADC) = 130¬į ‚ą†ABC = `1/2` m (arc ADC) ......[Inscribed angle theorem] = `1/2 xx 130^circ` = 65¬į Now, ‚ą†CDA = `1/2` m (arc ABC) ......[Inscribed angle theorem] ‚ąī ‚ą†CDA = `1/2 xx 230^circ` ‚ąī ‚ą†CDA = 115¬į ......(ii) ‚ą†CBE = ‚ą†CDA ......(iiii) [The exterior angle of a cyclic quadrilateral is equal to the interior opposite angle] ‚ąī ‚ą†CBE = 115¬į .....[From (ii) and (iii)] ‚ąī ‚ą†ABC = 65¬į, ‚ą†CDA = 115¬į, ‚ą†CBE = 115¬į.
items.solution_html:
m(arc ABC) = 230¬į ¬† .....(i) [Given]

‚ąī m(arc ADC) + m(arc ABC) = 360¬į ¬† .......[Degree measure of a circle is 360¬į]

‚ąī m(arc ADC) = 360¬į ‚Äď m(arc ABC)

‚ąī m(arc ADC) = 360¬į ‚Äď 230¬į¬† ¬†.......[From (i)]

‚ąī m(arc ADC) = 130¬į

‚ą†ABC = `1/2` m (arc ADC)¬† ¬†......[Inscribed angle theorem]

= `1/2 xx 130^circ`

= 65¬į

Now, ‚ą†CDA = `1/2` m (arc ABC)¬† ¬†......[Inscribed angle theorem]

‚ąī ‚ą†CDA = `1/2 xx 230^circ`

‚ąī ‚ą†CDA = 115¬į¬† ¬† ......(ii)

‚ą†CBE = ‚ą†CDA¬† ¬† ......(iiii) [The exterior angle of a cyclic quadrilateral is equal to the interior opposite angle]

‚ąī ‚ą†CBE = 115¬į ¬† ¬† .....[From (ii) and (iii)]

‚ąī ‚ą†ABC = 65¬į, ‚ą†CDA = 115¬į, ‚ą†CBE = 115¬į.

items.title: In the figure, chord LM ‚ČÖ chord LN, ‚ą†L = 35¬į. Find (i) m(arc MN) (ii) m(arc LN) - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: In the figure, chord LM ‚ČÖ chord LN, ‚ą†L = 35¬į. Find (i) m(arc MN) (ii) m(arc LN)
items.solution_text: (i) ‚ą†L = `1/2` m(arc MN) ...[Inscribed angle theorem] ‚ąī 35¬į = `1/2` m(arc MN) ‚ąī 2 √ó 35¬į = m(arc MN) ‚ąī m(arc MN) = 70¬į. (ii) In ‚ąÜLMN, chord LM ‚ČÖ chord LN ‚ąī ‚ą†M = ‚ą†N ...[Isosceles triangle theorem] ‚ąī ‚ą†L + ‚ą†M + ‚ą†N = 180¬į ...[Sum of the measures of angles of a triangle is 180¬į] ‚ąī 35¬į + ‚ą†M + ‚ą†M = 180¬į ‚ąī 2‚ą†M = 180¬į ‚Äď 35¬į = 145¬į ‚ąī ‚ą†M = `145^circ/2` Now, m(arc LN) = 2 √ó ‚ą†M ......[Inscribed angle theorem] = `2 xx 145^circ/2` = 145¬į
items.solution_html:
(i) ‚ą†L = `1/2` m(arc MN)¬† ¬† ¬†...[Inscribed angle theorem]

‚ąī 35¬į = `1/2` m(arc MN)

‚ąī 2 √ó 35¬į = m(arc MN)

‚ąī m(arc MN) = 70¬į.

(ii)¬†In ‚ąÜLMN,

chord LM ‚ČÖ chord LN

‚ąī ‚ą†M = ‚ą†N¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ¬†...[Isosceles triangle theorem]

‚ąī ‚ą†L + ‚ą†M + ‚ą†N = 180¬į¬† ¬†...[Sum of the measures of angles of a triangle is 180¬į]

‚ąī 35¬į + ‚ą†M + ‚ą†M = 180¬į

‚ąī 2‚ą†M = 180¬į ‚Äď 35¬į = 145¬į

‚ąī ‚ą†M = `145^circ/2`

Now, m(arc LN) = 2 √ó ‚ą†M¬† ¬†......[Inscribed angle theorem]

= `2 xx 145^circ/2`

= 145¬į¬†

items.title: In the figure, if ‚ą†ABC = 35¬į, then find m(arc AXC)? - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: In the figure, if ‚ą†ABC = 35¬į, then find m(arc AXC)?

items.solution_html:

‚ąī m(arc AXC) = 2 √ó 35¬į

‚ąī m(arc AXC) = 70¬į

items.title: In the figure, O is the center of the circle. Line AQ is a tangent. If OP = 3, m(arc PM) = 120¬į, then find the length of AP. - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: In the figure, O is the center of the circle. Line AQ is a tangent. If OP = 3, m(arc PM) = 120¬į, then find the length of AP.
items.solution_text: Given: line AQ is a tangent. OP = 3, m(arc PM) = 120¬į To find: AP In the given figure, arc PMQ is a semicircle. ‚ąī m(arc PMQ) = 180¬į ......[Measure of semicircular arc is 180¬į] ‚ąī m(arc PM) + m(arc MQ) = 180¬į .....[Arc addition property] ‚ąī 120¬į + m(arc MQ) = 180¬į ......[Given] ‚ąī m(arc MQ) = 180¬į ‚Äď 120¬į ‚ąī m(arc MQ) = 60¬į .......(i) ‚ą†MPQ = `1/2` m(arc MQ) .....[Inscribed angle theorem] ‚ąī ‚ą†MPQ = `1/2 xx 60^circ` ......[From (i)] ‚ąī ‚ą†MPQ = 30¬į i.e., ‚ą†APQ = 30¬į ......(ii) [A ‚Äď M ‚Äď P] In ‚ąÜPQA, ‚ą†PQA = 90¬į ......[Tangent theorem] ‚ą† APQ = 30¬į ......[From (ii)] ‚ąī ‚ą†PAQ = 60¬į ......[Remaning angle of ‚ąÜPQA] ‚ąī ‚ąÜPAQ is 30¬į ‚Äď 60¬į ‚Äď 90¬į triangle. ‚ąī PQ = `sqrt(3)/2` AP ......[Side opposite to 60¬į] ‚ąī (PO + OQ) = `sqrt(3)/2` AP ......[P ‚Äď O ‚Äď Q] ‚ąī (3 + 3) = `sqrt(3)/2` AP ......[Radii of same circle and op = 3] ‚ąī AP = `(6 xx 2)/sqrt(3)` ‚ąī AP = `(6 xx 2 xx sqrt(3))/(sqrt(3) xx sqrt(3))` ......[Multiply and divide by `sqrt(3)`] ‚ąī AP = `(6 xx 2 xx sqrt(3))/3` ‚ąī AP = `2 xx 2sqrt(3)` ‚ąī AP = `4sqrt(3)` units
items.solution_html:
Given: line AQ is a tangent.

OP = 3, m(arc PM) = 120¬į

To find: AP

In the given figure, arc PMQ is a semicircle.

‚ąī m(arc PMQ) = 180¬į¬† ¬† ¬†......[Measure of semicircular arc is 180¬į]

‚ąī m(arc PM) + m(arc MQ) = 180¬į¬† ¬† .....[Arc addition property]

‚ąī 120¬į + m(arc MQ) = 180¬į¬† ¬†......[Given]

‚ąī m(arc MQ) = 180¬į ‚Äď 120¬į

‚ąī m(arc MQ) = 60¬į¬† ¬† .......(i)

‚ą†MPQ = `1/2` m(arc MQ)¬† ¬† .....[Inscribed angle theorem]

‚ąī ‚ą†MPQ = `1/2 xx 60^circ`¬† ¬† ¬†......[From (i)]

‚ąī ‚ą†MPQ = 30¬į

i.e., ‚ą†APQ = 30¬į¬† ¬† ......(ii) [A ‚Äď M ‚Äď P]

In ‚ąÜPQA, ‚ą†PQA = 90¬į¬† ¬† ......[Tangent theorem]

‚ą† APQ = 30¬į¬† ¬† ¬†......[From (ii)]

‚ąī ‚ą†PAQ = 60¬į¬† ¬† ......[Remaning angle of ‚ąÜPQA]

‚ąī ‚ąÜPAQ is 30¬į ‚Äď 60¬į ‚Äď 90¬į triangle.

‚ąī PQ = `sqrt(3)/2` AP¬† ¬† ......[Side opposite to 60¬į]

‚ąī (PO + OQ) = `sqrt(3)/2` AP¬† ¬† ......[P ‚Äď O ‚Äď Q]

‚ąī (3 + 3) = `sqrt(3)/2` AP¬† ¬† ¬†......[Radii of same circle and op = 3]

‚ąī AP = `(6 xx 2)/sqrt(3)`

‚ąī AP = `(6 xx 2 xx sqrt(3))/(sqrt(3) xx sqrt(3))`¬† ......[Multiply and divide by `sqrt(3)`]

‚ąī AP = `(6 xx 2 xx sqrt(3))/3`

‚ąī AP = `2 xx 2sqrt(3)`

‚ąī AP = `4sqrt(3)` units

items.title: In the figure, O is the centre of the circle and ‚ą†AOB = 90¬į, ‚ą†ABC = 30¬į. Then find ‚ą†CAB. - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: In the figure, O is the centre of the circle, and ‚ą†AOB = 90¬į, ‚ą†ABC = 30¬į. Then find ‚ą†CAB.
items.solution_text: Given: ‚ą†AOB = 90¬į, ‚ą†ABC = 30¬į To find: ‚ą†CAB Solution: In given figure, ‚ą†AOB = m (arc AB) ......[Definition of measure of minor arc] ‚ąī m(arc AB) = 90¬į ......(i) Also, ‚ą†ACB = `1/2` m(arc AB) .....[Inscribed angle theorem] ‚ąī ‚ą†ACB = `1/2 xx 90^circ` ......[From (i)] ‚ąī ‚ą†ACB = 45¬į .....(ii) In ‚ąÜACB, ‚ą†CAB + ‚ą†ABC + ‚ą†ACB = 180¬į ......`[("Sum of the measures of"),("angles of a triangle is" 180^circ)]` ‚ąī ‚ą†CAB + 30¬į + 45¬į = 180¬į ......[From (ii)] ‚ąī ‚ą†CAB + 75¬į = 180¬į ‚ąī ‚ą†CAB = 180¬į ‚Äď 75¬į ‚ą†CAB = 105¬į
items.solution_html:
Given: ‚ą†AOB = 90¬į, ‚ą†ABC = 30¬į

To find: ‚ą†CAB

Solution:

In given figure,¬†

‚ą†AOB = m (arc AB)¬† ¬†......[Definition of measure of minor arc]

‚ąī m(arc AB) = 90¬į¬† ¬† ¬†......(i)

Also, ‚ą†ACB = `1/2` m(arc AB)¬† ¬† .....[Inscribed angle theorem]

‚ąī ‚ą†ACB = `1/2 xx 90^circ`¬† ¬† ......[From (i)]

‚ąī ‚ą†ACB = 45¬į¬† ¬† .....(ii)

In ‚ąÜACB,

‚ą†CAB + ‚ą†ABC + ‚ą†ACB = 180¬į¬† ¬† ......`[("Sum of the measures of"),("angles of a triangle is"¬† 180^circ)]`

‚ąī ‚ą†CAB + 30¬į + 45¬į = 180¬į¬† ¬† ......[From (ii)]

‚ąī ‚ą†CAB + 75¬į = 180¬į

‚ąī ‚ą†CAB = 180¬į ‚Äď 75¬į

‚ą†CAB = 105¬į

items.title: In the figure, PQRS is cyclic, side PQ ‚ČÖ side RQ, ‚ą†PSR = 110¬į. Find (i) measure of ‚ą†PQR (ii) m(arc PQR) (iii) m(arc QR) - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: In the figure, PQRS is cyclic, side PQ ‚ČÖ side RQ, ‚ą†PSR = 110¬į. Find (i) measure of ‚ą†PQR (ii) m(arc PQR) (iii) m(arc QR)
items.solution_text: (i) ‚ĖĘPQRS is a cyclic quadrilateral. ...[Given] ‚ąī ‚ą†PSR + ‚ą†PQR = 180¬į ...[Opposite angles of a cyclic quadrilateral are supplementary] ‚ąī 110¬į + ‚ą†PQR = 180¬į ‚ąī ‚ą†PQR = 180¬į ‚ąí 110¬į ‚ąī m‚ą†PQR = 70¬į (ii) ‚ą†PSR = `1/2` m(arc PQR) .....[Inscribed angle theorem] ‚ąī 110¬į = `1/2` m(arc PQR) ‚ąī m(arc PQR) = 220¬į (iii) In ‚ąÜPQR, Side PQ ‚ČÖ side RQ ...[Given] ‚ąī ‚ą†PRQ ‚ČÖ ‚ą†QPR ...[Isosceles triangle theorem] Let ‚ą†PRQ = ‚ą†QPR = x Now, ‚ą†PQR + ‚ą†QPR + ‚ą†PRQ = 180¬į ...[Sum of the measures of angles of a triangle is 180¬į] ‚ąī ‚ą†PQR + x + x = 180¬į ‚ąī 70¬į + 2x = 180¬į ‚ąī 2x = 180¬į ‚ąí 70¬į ‚ąī 2x = 110¬į ‚ąī x = `110^circ/2` ‚ąī x = 55¬į ‚ąī ‚ą†PRQ = ‚ą†QPR = 55¬į ......(i) But, ‚ą†QPR = `1/2` m(arc QR) .....[Inscribed angle theorem] ‚ąī 55¬į = `1/2` m(arc QR) ‚ąī m(arc QR) = 110¬į
items.solution_html:
(i) ‚ĖĘPQRS is a cyclic quadrilateral.¬† ¬† ...[Given]

‚ąī ‚ą†PSR + ‚ą†PQR = 180¬į¬† ¬† ...[Opposite angles of a cyclic quadrilateral are supplementary]

‚ąī 110¬į + ‚ą†PQR = 180¬į

‚ąī ‚ą†PQR = 180¬į ‚ąí 110¬į

‚ąī m‚ą†PQR = 70¬į

(ii)¬†‚ą†PSR = `1/2` m(arc PQR)¬† ¬† ¬†.....[Inscribed angle theorem]

‚ąī 110¬į = `1/2` m(arc PQR)

‚ąī m(arc PQR) = 220¬į

(iii) In ‚ąÜPQR,

Side PQ ‚ČÖ side RQ¬† ¬† ¬†...[Given]

‚ąī ‚ą†PRQ ‚ČÖ ‚ą†QPR¬† ¬† ¬†...[Isosceles triangle theorem]

Let ‚ą†PRQ = ‚ą†QPR = x

Now, ‚ą†PQR + ‚ą†QPR + ‚ą†PRQ = 180¬į ¬† ¬†...[Sum of the measures of angles of a triangle is 180¬į]

‚ąī ‚ą†PQR + x + x = 180¬į

‚ąī 70¬į + 2x = 180¬į

‚ąī 2x = 180¬į ‚ąí 70¬į

‚ąī 2x = 110¬į

‚ąī x = `110^circ/2`

‚ąī x = 55¬į

‚ąī ‚ą†PRQ = ‚ą†QPR = 55¬į¬† ¬† ......(i)

But, ‚ą†QPR = `1/2` m(arc QR)¬† ¬†.....[Inscribed angle theorem]

‚ąī 55¬į = `1/2` m(arc QR)

‚ąī m(arc QR) = 110¬į

items.title: In the figure, quadrilateral ABCD is cyclic. If m(arc BC) = 90¬į and ‚ą†DBC = 55¬į, then find the measure of ‚ą†BCD. - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: In the figure, quadrilateral ABCD is cyclic. If m(arc BC) = 90¬į and ‚ą†DBC = 55¬į, then find the measure of ‚ą†BCD.
items.solution_text: Given: m(arc BC) = 90¬į, ‚ą†DBC = 55¬į To find: ‚ą†BCD Solution: ‚ą†BDC = `1/2` m(arc BC) ......[Inscribed angle theorem] ‚ąī ‚ą†BDC = `1/2 xx 90^circ` ......[Given] ‚ąī ‚ą†BDC = 45¬į ......(i) In ‚ąÜBCD, ‚ą†BDC + ‚ą†DBC + ‚ą†BCD = 180¬į ......[Sum of the measures of all angles of a triangle is 180¬į] ‚ąī 45¬į + 55¬į + ‚ą†BCD = 180¬į .....[From (i) and given] ‚ąī 100¬į + ‚ą†BCD = 180¬į ‚ąī ‚ą†BCD = 180¬į ‚Äď 100¬į ‚ąī ‚ą†BCD = 80¬į ‚ąī The measure of ‚ą†BCD is 80¬į.
items.solution_html:
Given: m(arc BC) = 90¬į, ‚ą†DBC = 55¬į

To find: ‚ą†BCD

Solution:

‚ą†BDC = `1/2` m(arc BC)¬† ¬† ......[Inscribed angle theorem]

‚ąī ‚ą†BDC = `1/2 xx 90^circ`¬† ¬† ......[Given]

‚ąī ‚ą†BDC = 45¬į¬† ¬† ......(i)

In ‚ąÜBCD,

‚ą†BDC + ‚ą†DBC + ‚ą†BCD = 180¬į¬† ¬†......[Sum of the measures of all angles of a triangle is 180¬į]

‚ąī 45¬į + 55¬į + ‚ą†BCD = 180¬į¬† ¬†.....[From (i) and given]

‚ąī 100¬į + ‚ą†BCD = 180¬į

‚ąī ‚ą†BCD = 180¬į ‚Äď 100¬į

‚ąī ‚ą†BCD = 80¬į

‚ąī The measure of ‚ą†BCD is 80¬į.

items.title: In the figure, seg AB is a diameter of a circle with centre O. The bisector of ‚ą†ACB intersects the circle at point D. Prove that, seg AD ‚ČÖ seg BD. - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: In the figure, seg AB is a diameter of a circle with centre O. The bisector of ‚ą†ACB intersects the circle at point D. Prove that, seg AD ‚ČÖ seg BD. Complete the following proof by filling in the blanks. Proof: Draw seg OD. ‚ą†ACB = `square` ......[Angle inscribed in semicircle] ‚ą†DCB = `square` ......[CD is the bisector of ‚ą†C] m(arc DB) = `square` ......[Inscribed angle theorem] ‚ą†DOB = `square` ......[Definition of measure of an arc](i) seg OA ‚ČÖ seg OB ...... `square` (ii) ‚ąī Line OD is `square` of seg AB ......[From (i) and (ii)] ‚ąī seg AD ‚ČÖ seg BD
items.solution_text: Proof: ‚ą†ACB = 90¬į ......[Angle inscribed in semicircle] ‚ą†DCB = ‚ą†DCA = 45¬į ......[CD is the bisector of ‚ą†C] m(arc DB) = 2‚ą†DCB = 90¬į ......[Inscribed angle theorem] ‚ą†DOB = m(arc DB) = 90¬į ......[Definition of measure of an arc](i) seg OA ‚ČÖ seg OB ...... items.title: In the given figure, chord AB ‚ČÖ chord CD, Prove that, arc AC ‚ČÖ arc BD. - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: In the given figure, chord AB ‚ČÖ chord CD, Prove that, arc AC ‚ČÖ arc BD.
items.solution_text: Chord AB ‚ČÖ Chord CD ...(Given) ‚ąī Arc ACB ‚ČÖ Arc CBD ...(Arcs corresponding to congruent chords.) ‚ąī m(arc ACB) = m(arc CBD) ...(1) But m(arc ACB) = m(arc AC) + m(arc CB) ...(2) and m(arc CBD) = m(arc CB) + m(arc BD) ...(3) From (1), (2), and (3), we get, m(arc AC) + m(arc CB) = m(arc CB) + m(arc BD) ‚ąī m(arc AC) = m(arc BD) ‚ąī arc AC ‚ČÖ arc BD Hence, proved.
items.solution_html:
Chord AB ‚ČÖ Chord CD¬† ¬†...(Given)

‚ąī Arc ACB ‚ČÖ Arc CBD¬† ¬†...(Arcs corresponding to congruent chords.)

‚ąī m(arc ACB) = m(arc CBD)¬† ¬†...(1)

But m(arc ACB) = m(arc AC) + m(arc CB)¬† ¬†...(2)

and m(arc CBD) = m(arc CB) + m(arc BD)¬† ¬†...(3)

From (1), (2), and (3), we get,

m(arc AC) + m(arc CB) = m(arc CB) + m(arc BD)

‚ąī m(arc AC) = m(arc BD)

‚ąī arc AC ‚ČÖ arc BD

Hence, proved.
