items.title: Point P(‚Äď 4, 6) divides point A(‚Äď 6, 10) and B(m, n) in the ratio 2:1, then find the coordinates of point B - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: Point P(‚Äď 4, 6) divides point A(‚Äď 6, 10) and B(m, n) in the ratio 2:1, then find the coordinates of point B
items.solution_text: By section formula ‚Äď 4 = `(2 xx "m" + 1 xx (-6))/(2 + 1)` ‚ąī ‚Äď 4 = `(2"m" - 6)/3` ‚ąī ‚Äď12 = 2m ‚Äď 6 ‚ąī 2m = ‚Äď 6 ‚ąī m = ‚Äď 3 6 = `(2 xx "n" + 1 xx 10)/(2 + 1)` ‚ąī 6 = `(2"n" + 10)/3` ‚ąī 18 = 2n + 10 ‚ąī 2n = 8 ‚ąī n = 4 Co-ordinates of point B are (‚Äď 3, 4).
items.solution_html:
By section formula

‚Äď 4 = `(2 xx "m" + 1 xx (-6))/(2 + 1)`

‚ąī ‚Äď 4 = `(2"m" - 6)/3`

‚ąī ‚Äď12 = 2m ‚Äď 6

‚ąī 2m = ‚Äď 6

‚ąī m = ‚Äď 3

6 = `(2 xx "n" + 1 xx 10)/(2 + 1)`

‚ąī 6 = `(2"n" + 10)/3`

‚ąī 18 = 2n + 10

‚ąī 2n = 8

‚ąī n = 4

Co-ordinates of point B are (‚Äď 3, 4).

items.title: Prove that (1 ‚Äď cos2A) . sec2B + tan2B(1 ‚Äď sin2A) = sin2A + tan2B - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: Prove that (1 ‚Äď cos 2 A) . sec 2 B + tan 2 B(1 ‚Äď sin 2 A) = sin 2 A + tan 2 B
items.solution_text: L.H.S = (1 ‚Äď cos 2 A) . sec 2 B + tan 2 B(1 ‚Äď sin 2 A) = `sin^2"A"* 1/(cos^2"B") + (sin^2"B")/(cos^2"B") (1 - sin^2"A")` ......`[(because sin^2"A" + cos^2"A" = 1),(therefore 1 - cos^2"A" = sin^2"A")]` = `(sin^2"A")/(cos^2"B") + (sin^2"B")/(cos^2"B") - (sin^2"A"sin^2"B")/(cos^2"B")` = `(sin^2"A")/(cos^2"B") - (sin^2"A"sin^2"B")/(cos^2"B") + (sin^2"B")/(cos^2"B")` = `(sin^2"A")/(cos^2"B") (1 - sin^2"B") + tan^2"B"` = `(sin^2"A")/(cos^2"B") (cos^2"B") + tan^2"B"` = sin 2 A + tan 2 B = R.H.S ‚ąī (1 ‚Äď cos 2 A) . sec 2 B + tan 2 B (1 ‚Äď sin 2 A) = sin 2 A + tan 2 B
items.solution_html:
L.H.S = (1 ‚Äď cos2A) . sec2B + tan2B(1 ‚Äď sin2A)

= `sin^2"A"* 1/(cos^2"B") + (sin^2"B")/(cos^2"B") (1 - sin^2"A")`¬† ¬† ¬†......`[(because sin^2"A" + cos^2"A" = 1),(therefore 1 - cos^2"A" = sin^2"A")]`

= `(sin^2"A")/(cos^2"B") + (sin^2"B")/(cos^2"B") - (sin^2"A"sin^2"B")/(cos^2"B")`

= `(sin^2"A")/(cos^2"B") - (sin^2"A"sin^2"B")/(cos^2"B") + (sin^2"B")/(cos^2"B")`

= `(sin^2"A")/(cos^2"B") (1 - sin^2"B") + tan^2"B"`

= `(sin^2"A")/(cos^2"B") (cos^2"B") + tan^2"B"`

= sin2A + tan2B

= R.H.S

‚ąī (1 ‚Äď cos2A) . sec2B + tan2B (1 ‚Äď sin2A) = sin2A + tan2B

items.title: Prove that 1+cosA1-cosA = cosec A + cot A - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: Prove that `sqrt((1 + cos "A")/(1 - cos"A"))` = cosec A + cot A
items.solution_text: L.H.S = `sqrt((1 + cos "A")/(1 - cos"A"))` = `sqrt((1 + cos "A")/(1 - cos "A") xx (1 + cos "A")/(1 + cos "A"))` ......[On rationalising the denominator] = `sqrt((1 + cos "A")^2/(1 - cos^2 "A"))` = `sqrt((1 + cos "A")^2/(sin^2 "A")` ......`[(because sin^2"A" + cos^2"A" = 1),(therefore 1 - cos^2"A" = sin^2"A")]` = `(1 + cos"A")/"sin A"` = `1/"sin A" + "cos A"/"sin A"` = cosec A + cot A = R.H.S ‚ąī `sqrt((1 + cos "A")/(1 - cos"A"))` = cosec A + cot A
items.solution_html:
L.H.S = `sqrt((1 + cos "A")/(1 - cos"A"))`

= `sqrt((1 + cos "A")/(1 - cos "A") xx (1 + cos "A")/(1 + cos "A"))`¬† ¬†......[On rationalising the denominator]

= `sqrt((1 + cos "A")^2/(1 - cos^2 "A"))`

= `sqrt((1 + cos "A")^2/(sin^2 "A")`¬† ¬† ......`[(because sin^2"A" + cos^2"A" = 1),(therefore 1 - cos^2"A" = sin^2"A")]`

= `(1 + cos"A")/"sin A"`

= `1/"sin A" + "cos A"/"sin A"`

= cosec A + cot A

= R.H.S

‚ąī `sqrt((1 + cos "A")/(1 - cos"A"))` = cosec A + cot A

items.title: Prove that 1+secAsec A=sin2A1-cosA - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: Prove that `(1 + sec "A")/"sec A" = (sin^2"A")/(1 - cos"A")`
items.solution_text: L.H.S = `(1 + sec "A")/"sec A"` = `1/"sec A" + "sec A"/"sec A"` = cos A + 1 = `(1 + cos "A") xx (1 - cos"A")/(1 - cos"A")` = `(1 - cos^2"A")/(1 - cos"A")` = `(sin^2"A")/(1 - cos"A")` .......`[(because sin^2"A" + cos^2"A" = 1),(therefore 1 - cos^2"A" = sin^2"A")]` = R.H.S ‚ąī `(1 + sec "A")/"sec A" = (sin^2"A")/(1 - cos"A")`
items.solution_html:
L.H.S = `(1 + sec "A")/"sec A"`

= `1/"sec A" + "sec A"/"sec A"`

= cos A + 1

= `(1 + cos "A") xx (1 -¬† cos"A")/(1 - cos"A")`

= `(1 - cos^2"A")/(1 - cos"A")`

= `(sin^2"A")/(1 - cos"A")`¬† ¬† ¬†.......`[(because sin^2"A" + cos^2"A" = 1),(therefore 1 - cos^2"A" = sin^2"A")]`

= R.H.S

‚ąī `(1 + sec "A")/"sec A" = (sin^2"A")/(1 - cos"A")`

items.title: Prove that 1+sinőł1-sinőł = (sec őł + tan őł)2 - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: Prove that `(1 + sintheta)/(1 - sin theta)` = (sec őł + tan őł) 2
items.solution_text: L.H.S = `(1 + sintheta)/(1 - sin theta)` = `((1 + sintheta)/(costheta))/((1 - sintheta)/(costheta))` ......[Dividing numerator and denominator by cos őł] = `(1/costheta + (sintheta)/(costheta))/(1/costheta - (sintheta)/(costheta)` = `(sectheta + tantheta)/(sectheta - tantheta)` = `(sectheta + tantheta)/(sectheta - tantheta) xx (sectheta + tantheta)/(sectheta + tantheta)` ......[On rationalising the denominator] = `(sectheta + tantheta)^2/(sec^2theta - tan^2theta)` = `(sectheta + tantheta)^2/1` ......`[(because 1 + tan^2theta = sec^2theta),(therefore sec^2theta - tan^2theta = 1)]` = (sec őł + tan őł) 2 = R.H.S ‚ąī `(1 + sintheta)/(1 - sin theta)` = (sec őł + tan őł) 2
items.solution_html:
L.H.S = `(1 + sintheta)/(1 - sin theta)`

= `((1 + sintheta)/(costheta))/((1 - sintheta)/(costheta))`¬† ......[Dividing numerator and denominator by cos őł]

= `(1/costheta + (sintheta)/(costheta))/(1/costheta - (sintheta)/(costheta)`

= `(sectheta + tantheta)/(sectheta - tantheta)`

= `(sectheta + tantheta)/(sectheta - tantheta) xx (sectheta + tantheta)/(sectheta + tantheta)` ......[On rationalising the denominator]

= `(sectheta + tantheta)^2/(sec^2theta - tan^2theta)`

= `(sectheta + tantheta)^2/1`¬† ¬†......`[(because 1 + tan^2theta = sec^2theta),(therefore sec^2theta - tan^2theta = 1)]`

= (sec őł + tan őł)2

= R.H.S

‚ąī `(1 + sintheta)/(1 - sin theta)` = (sec őł + tan őł)2¬†

items.title: Prove that 1+sinBcos B+cos B1+sinB = 2 sec B - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: Prove that `(1 + sin "B")/"cos B" + "cos B"/(1 + sin "B")` = 2 sec B
items.solution_html:
L.H.S = `(1 + sin "B")/"cos B" + "cos B"/(1 + sin "B")`

= `((1 +sin "B")^2 + cos^2"B")/(cos "B"(1 + sin "B"))`

= `(1 +2sin"B" + sin^2"B" + cos^2"B")/(cos"B"(1 + sin"B"))`¬† ¬† ......[‚ąĶ (a + b)2 = a2 + 2ab + b2]

= `(1 + 2sin"B" + 1)/(cos"B"(1+ sin"B"))`¬† ¬†.....[‚ąĶ sin2B + cos2B = 1]

= `(2 + 2sin"B")/(cos"B"(1 + sin"B"))`

= `(2(1 + sin"B"))/(cos"B"(1 + sin"B"))`

= `2/"cos B"`

= 2 sec B

= R.H.S

‚ąī `(1 + sin "B")/"cos B" + "cos B"/(1 + sin "B")` = 2 sec B

items.title: Prove that 1cosec őł-cotőł = cosec őł + cot őł - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: Prove that `1/("cosec" theta - cot theta)` = cosec őł + cot őł
items.solution_text: L.H.S = `1/("cosec" theta - cot theta)` = `1/("cosec" theta - cot theta) xx ("cosec"theta + cottheta)/("cosec"theta + cottheta)` ......[On rationalising the denominator] = `("cosec"theta + cottheta)/("cosec"^2theta - cot^2theta)` ......[‚ąĶ (a ‚Äď b)(a + b) = a 2 ‚Äď b 2 ] = `("cosec"theta cottheta)/1` ......`[(‚ąĶ 1 + cot^2őł = "cosec"^2őł),(‚ąī "cosec"^2őł - cot^2őł = 1)]` = cosecőł + cotőł = R.H.S ‚ąī `1/("cosec" theta - cot theta)` = cosec őł + cot őł
items.solution_html:
L.H.S = `1/("cosec"¬† theta - cot theta)`

= `1/("cosec"¬† theta - cot theta) xx ("cosec"theta + cottheta)/("cosec"theta + cottheta)`¬† ¬† ......[On rationalising the denominator]

= `("cosec"theta + cottheta)/("cosec"^2theta - cot^2theta)`¬† ¬†......[‚ąĶ (a ‚Äď b)(a + b) = a2 ‚Äď b2]

= `("cosec"theta¬† cottheta)/1`¬† ¬† ......`[(‚ąĶ 1 + cot^2őł = "cosec"^2őł),(‚ąī "cosec"^2őł - cot^2őł = 1)]`

= cosecőł + cotőł

= R.H.S

‚ąī `1/("cosec"¬† theta - cot theta)` = cosec őł + cot őł

items.title: Prove that 2(sin6A + cos6A) ‚Äď 3(sin4A + cos4A) + 1 = 0 - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: Prove that 2(sin 6 A + cos 6 A) ‚Äď 3(sin 4 A + cos 4 A) + 1 = 0
items.solution_text: sin 6 A + cos 6 A = (sin 2 A) 3 + (cos 2 A) 3 = (1 ‚Äď cos 2 A) 3 + (cos 2 A) 3 ......`[(because sin^2"A" + cos^2"A" = 1),(therefore 1 - cos^2"A" = sin^2"A")]` = 1 ‚Äď 3 cos 2 A + 3(cos 2 A) 2 ‚Äď (cos 2 A) 3 + cos 6 A ......[‚ąĶ (a ‚Äď b) 3 = a 3 ‚Äď 3a 2 b + 3ab 2 ‚Äď b 3 ] = 1 ‚Äď 3 cos 2 A(1 ‚Äď cos 2 A) ‚Äď cos 6 A + cos 6 A = 1 ‚Äď 3 cos 2 A sin 2 A sin 4 A + cos 4 A = (sin 2 A) 2 + (cos 2 A) 2 = (1 ‚Äď cos 2 A) 2 + (cos 2 A) 2 = 1 ‚Äď 2 cos 2 A + (cos 2 A) 2 + (cos 2 A) 2 ......[‚ąĶ (a ‚Äď b) 2 = a 2 ‚Äď 2ab + b 2 ] = 1 ‚Äď 2 cos 2 A + 2 cos 4 A = 1 ‚Äď 2 cos 2 A(1 ‚Äď cos 2 A) = 1 ‚Äď 2 cos 2 A sin 2 A L.H.S = 2(sin 6 A + cos 6 A) ‚Äď 3(sin 4 A + cos 4 A) + 1 = 2(1 ‚Äď 3 cos 2 A sin 2 A) ‚Äď 3(1 ‚Äď 2 cos 2 A sin 2 A) + 1 = 2 ‚Äď 6 cos 2 A sin 2 A ‚Äď 3 + 6 cos 2 A sin 2 A + 1 = 0 = R.H.S ‚ąī 2(sin 6 A + cos 6 A) ‚Äď 3(sin 4 A + cos 4 A) + 1 = 0
items.solution_html:
sin6A + cos6A = (sin2A)3 + (cos2A)3

¬†= (1 ‚Äď cos2A)3 + (cos2A)3 ¬† ......`[(because sin^2"A" + cos^2"A" = 1),(therefore 1 - cos^2"A" = sin^2"A")]`

= 1 ‚Äď 3 cos2A + 3(cos2A)2 ‚Äď (cos2A)3 + cos6A¬† ¬†......[‚ąĶ (a ‚Äď b)3 = a3 ‚Äď 3a2b + 3ab2 ‚Äď b3]

= 1 ‚Äď 3 cos2A(1 ‚Äď cos2A) ‚Äď cos6A + cos6A

= 1 ‚Äď 3 cos2A sin2A

sin4A + cos4A = (sin2A)2 + (cos2A)2

= (1 ‚Äď cos2A)2 + (cos2A)2

= 1 ‚Äď 2 cos2A + (cos2A)2 + (cos2A)2 ¬† ......[‚ąĶ (a ‚Äď b)2 = a2 ‚Äď 2ab + b2]

= 1 ‚Äď 2 cos2A + 2 cos4A

= 1 ‚Äď 2 cos2A(1 ‚Äď cos2A)

= 1 ‚Äď 2 cos2A sin2A

L.H.S = 2(sin6A + cos6A) ‚Äď 3(sin4A + cos4A) + 1

= 2(1 ‚Äď 3 cos2A sin2A) ‚Äď 3(1 ‚Äď 2 cos2A sin2A) + 1

= 2 ‚Äď 6 cos2A sin2A ‚Äď 3 + 6 cos2A sin2A + 1

= 0

= R.H.S

‚ąī 2(sin6A + cos6A) ‚Äď 3(sin4A + cos4A) + 1 = 0

items.title: Prove that angles inscribed in the same arc are congruent.Given: In a circle with center C, ‚ą†PQR and ‚ą†PSR are inscribed in same arc PQR. Arc PTR is intercepted by the angles. To prove: ‚ą†PQR ‚ČÖ ‚ą†PSR. P - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: Prove that angles inscribed in the same arc are congruent. Given: In a circle with center C, ‚ą†PQR and ‚ą†PSR are inscribed in same arc PQR. Arc PTR is intercepted by the angles. To prove: ‚ą†PQR ‚ČÖ ‚ą†PSR. Proof: m‚ą†PQR = `1/2 xx ["m"("arc PTR")]` ......(i) `square` m‚ą†`square` = `1/2 xx ["m"("arc PTR")]` ......(ii) `square` m‚ą†`square` = m‚ą†PSR .....[By (i) and (ii)] ‚ąī ‚ą†PQR ‚ČÖ ‚ą†PSR
items.solution_text: Proof: m‚ą†PQR = `1/2 xx ["m"("arc PTR")]` ......(i) [Inscribed angle theorem] m‚ą† PSR = `1/2 xx ["m"("arc PTR")]` ......(ii) [Inscribed angle theorem] m‚ą† PQR = m‚ą†PSR .....[By (i) and (ii)] ‚ąī ‚ą†PQR ‚ČÖ ‚ą†PSR
items.solution_html:
Proof:

m‚ą†PQR = `1/2 xx ["m"("arc PTR")]`¬† ¬†......(i) [Inscribed angle theorem]

m‚ą†PSR = `1/2 xx ["m"("arc PTR")]`¬† ¬†......(ii) [Inscribed angle theorem]

m‚ą†PQR = m‚ą†PSR¬† ¬† ¬† .....[By (i) and (ii)]

‚ąī ‚ą†PQR ‚ČÖ ‚ą†PSR

items.title: Prove that cosőł1+sinőł=1-sinőłcosőł - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: Prove that `costheta/(1 + sintheta) = (1 - sintheta)/(costheta)`
items.solution_text: L.H.S = `costheta/(1 + sintheta)` = `costheta/(1 + sintheta) xx (1 - sintheta)/(1 - sintheta)` ......[On rationalising the denominator] = `(costheta(1 - sintheta))/(1 - sin^2theta)` = `(costheta(1 - sintheta))/(cos^2theta)` ......`[(because sin^2theta +cos^2theta = 1),(therefore 1 -sin^2theta = cos^2theta)]` = `(1 - sintheta)/costheta` = R.H.S ‚ąī `costheta/(1 + sintheta) = (1 - sintheta)/(costheta)`
items.solution_html:
L.H.S = `costheta/(1 + sintheta)`

= `costheta/(1 + sintheta) xx (1 - sintheta)/(1 -¬† sintheta)`¬† ¬†......[On rationalising the denominator]

= `(costheta(1 - sintheta))/(1 - sin^2theta)`

= `(costheta(1 - sintheta))/(cos^2theta)`¬† ¬† ......`[(because sin^2theta +cos^2theta = 1),(therefore 1 -sin^2theta = cos^2theta)]`

= `(1 - sintheta)/costheta`

= R.H.S

‚ąī `costheta/(1 + sintheta) = (1 - sintheta)/(costheta)`

items.title: Prove that cos(90-A)sinA=sin(90-A)cosA - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: Prove that `(cos(90 - "A"))/(sin "A") = (sin(90 - "A"))/(cos "A")`
items.solution_text: L.H.S = `(cos(90 - "A"))/(sin "A")` = `"sin A"/"sin A"` = 1 R.H.S = `(sin(90 - "A"))/(cos "A")` = `"cos A"/"cos A"` = 1 ‚ąī L.H.S = R.H.S
items.solution_html:
L.H.S = `(cos(90 - "A"))/(sin "A")`

= `"sin A"/"sin A"`

= 1

R.H.S = `(sin(90 - "A"))/(cos "A")`

= `"cos A"/"cos A"`

= 1

‚ąī L.H.S = R.H.S

items.title: Prove that cos2őł . (1 + tan2őł) = 1. Complete the activity given below. Activity: L.H.S = ‚Ė° = cos2őł√ó‚Ė° .....[1+tan2őł=‚Ė°] = (cosőł√ó‚Ė°)2 = 12 = 1 = R.H.S - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: Prove that cos 2 őł . (1 + tan 2 őł) = 1. Complete the activity given below. Activity: L.H.S = `square` = `cos^2theta xx square .....[1 + tan^2theta = square]` = `(cos theta xx square)^2` = 1 2 = 1 = R.H.S
items.solution_text: L.H.S. = `cos^2theta*(1 + tan^2theta)` = `cos^2theta xx sec^2theta` .....`[1 + tan^2theta = sec^2theta]` = `(cos theta xx sectheta)^2` = 1 2 = 1 = R.H.S
items.solution_html:
L.H.S. = `cos^2theta*(1 + tan^2theta)`

= `cos^2theta xx sec^2theta`¬† ¬† .....`[1 + tan^2theta = sec^2theta]`

= `(cos theta xx sectheta)^2`

= 12

= 1

= R.H.S

items.title: Prove that cos2őłsinőł+sinőł = cosec őł - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: Prove that `(cos^2theta)/(sintheta) + sintheta` = cosec őł
items.solution_text: L.H.S = `(cos^2theta)/(sintheta) + sintheta` = `(cos^2theta + sin^2theta)/sintheta` = `1/sintheta` .......[‚ąĶ sin 2 őł + cos 2 őł = 1] = cosec őł = R.H.S ‚ąī `(cos^2theta)/(sintheta) + sintheta` = cosec őł
items.solution_html:
L.H.S = `(cos^2theta)/(sintheta) + sintheta`¬†

= `(cos^2theta + sin^2theta)/sintheta`

= `1/sintheta`¬† ¬†.......[‚ąĶ sin2őł + cos2őł = 1]

= cosec őł

= R.H.S

‚ąī `(cos^2theta)/(sintheta) + sintheta` = cosec őł

items.title: Prove that őłcosec őł√ó1-cos2őł = 1 - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: Prove that `"cosec" őł xx sqrt(1 - cos^2theta)` = 1
items.solution_text: L.H.S = `"cosec" őł xx sqrt(1 - cos^2theta)` = `"cosec" őł xx sqrt(sin^2theta)` ......`[(because sin^2theta + cos^2theta = 1),(therefore 1 - cos^2theta = sin^2theta)]` = cosec őł √ó sin őł = 1 ......[‚ąĶ sin őł √ó cosec őł = 1] = R.H.S
items.solution_html:
L.H.S = `"cosec"¬† őł xx sqrt(1 - cos^2theta)`

= `"cosec"¬† őł xx sqrt(sin^2theta)`¬† ¬†......`[(because sin^2theta + cos^2theta = 1),(therefore 1 - cos^2theta = sin^2theta)]`

= cosec őł √ó sin őł

= 1¬† ¬† ¬†......[‚ąĶ sin őł √ó cosec őł = 1]

= R.H.S

items.title: Prove that cosec őł ‚Äď cot őł = sinőł1+cosőł - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: Prove that cosec őł ‚Äď cot őł = `sin theta/(1 + cos theta)`
items.solution_text: L.H.S = cosec őł ‚Äď cot őł = `1/sintheta - costheta/sintheta` = `(1 -costheta)/sintheta` = `(1 - costheta)/sintheta xx (1 + costheta)/(1 +costheta)` .....[On rationalising the numerator] = `(1 - cos^2theta)/(sintheta(1 +costheta))` = `(sin^2theta)/(sintheta(1 + costheta))` .....`[(because sin^2theta + cos^2theta = 1),(therefore 1 - cos^2theta = sin^2theta)]` = `sintheta/(1 + costheta)` = R.H.S ‚ąī cosec őł ‚Äď cot őł = `sin theta/(1 + cos theta)`
items.solution_html:
L.H.S = cosec őł ‚Äď cot őł

= `1/sintheta - costheta/sintheta`

= `(1 -costheta)/sintheta`

= `(1 - costheta)/sintheta xx (1 + costheta)/(1 +costheta)`¬† ¬† .....[On rationalising the numerator]

= `(1 - cos^2theta)/(sintheta(1 +costheta))`

= `(sin^2theta)/(sintheta(1 + costheta))`¬† ¬†.....`[(because sin^2theta + cos^2theta = 1),(therefore 1 - cos^2theta = sin^2theta)]`

= `sintheta/(1 + costheta)`

= R.H.S

‚ąī cosec őł ‚Äď cot őł = `sin theta/(1 + cos theta)`

items.title: Prove that cot A1-cotA+tan A1-tanA = ‚Äď 1 - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: Prove that `"cot A"/(1 - cot"A") + "tan A"/(1 - tan "A")` = ‚Äď 1
items.solution_text: L.H.S = `"cot A"/(1 - cot"A") + "tan A"/(1 - tan "A")` = `"cot A"/(1 - 1/(tan"A")) + "tan A"/(1 - tan "A")` = `"cot A"/((tan "A" - 1)/(tan "A")) + "tan A"/(1 - tan "A")` = `"cot A tan A"/(tan "A" - 1) + "tan A"/(1 - tan "A")` = `1/(tan "A" - 1) + "tan A"/(1 - tan "A")` ......[‚ąĶ cot A tan A = 1] = `- 1/(1 - tan "A") + "tan A"/(1 - tan "A")` = `- (1/(1 -tan "A") - "tan A"/(1- tan "A"))` = `-((1 - tan "A")/(1 - tan "A"))` = ‚Äď 1 = R.H.S ‚ąī `"cot A"/(1 - cot"A") + "tan A"/(1 - tan "A")` = ‚Äď 1
items.solution_html:
L.H.S = `"cot A"/(1 - cot"A") + "tan A"/(1 - tan "A")`

= `"cot A"/(1 - 1/(tan"A")) + "tan A"/(1 - tan "A")`

= `"cot A"/((tan "A" - 1)/(tan "A")) + "tan A"/(1 - tan "A")`

= `"cot A tan A"/(tan "A" - 1) + "tan A"/(1 - tan "A")`

= `1/(tan "A" - 1) + "tan A"/(1 - tan "A")`¬† ¬†......[‚ąĶ cot A tan A = 1]

= `- 1/(1 - tan "A") + "tan A"/(1 - tan "A")`

= `- (1/(1 -tan "A") - "tan A"/(1- tan "A"))`

= `-((1 - tan "A")/(1 - tan "A"))`

= ‚Äď 1

= R.H.S

‚ąī `"cot A"/(1 - cot"A") + "tan A"/(1 - tan "A")` = ‚Äď 1

items.title: Prove that cot A1-tanA+tan A1-cotA = 1 + tan A + cot A = sec A . cosec A + 1 - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: Prove that `"cot A"/(1 - tan "A") + "tan A"/(1 - cot"A")` = 1 + tan A + cot A = sec A . cosec A + 1
items.solution_text: `"cot A"/(1 - tan "A") + "tan A"/(1 - cot"A")` = `((cos "A")/(sin "A"))/(1 - (sin "A")/(cos "A")) + ((sin "A")/(cos "A"))/(1 - (cos "A")/(sin "A"))` = `((cos "A")/(sin "A"))/((cos "A" - sin "A")/(cos "A")) + ((sin "A")/(cos "A"))/((sin "A" - cos "A")/(sin "A"))` = `"cos A"/"sin A" xx "cos A"/(cos "A" - sin "A") + "sin A"/"cos A" xx "sin A"/(sin "A" - cos "A")` = `(cos^2"A")/(sin "A"(cos "A" - sin "A")) + (sin^2"A")/(cos"A"(sin"A" - cos"A"))` = `1/(sin "A" - cos "A") ((-cos^3"A" + sin^3"A")/(sin"A" cos"A"))` = `1/(sin"A" - cos"A")((sin^3"A" - cos^3"A")/(sin"A" cos"A"))` = `1/(sin"A" - cos"A")xx ((sin"A" - cos"A")(sin^2"A" + sin"A" cos"A" + cos^2"A"))/(sin"A" cos"A")` ......[‚ąĶ a 3 ‚Äď b 3 = (a ‚Äď b)(a 2 + ab + b 2 )] = `(sin^2"A" +sin"A" cos"A" + cos^2"A")/(sin"A" cos"A"` ......(i) = `(1 + sin"A" cos"A")/(sin"A" cos"A")` .....[‚ąĶ sin 2 A + cos 2 A = 1] = `1/(sin"A" cos"A") + (sin"A" cos"A")/(sin"A" cos"A")` = cosec A sec A + 1 .....(ii) `"cot A"/(1 - tan "A") + "tan A"/(1 - cot "A")` = `(sin^2"A" + sin"A" cos"A" + cos^2"A")/(sin"A" cos"A")` ......[From (i)] = `(sin^2"A")/(sin"A" cos"A") + "sin A cos A"/"sin A cos A" + (cos^2"A")/"sin A cos A"` = `"sin A"/"cos A" + 1 + "cos A"/"sin A"` = tan A + 1 + cot A ......(iii) From (ii) and (iii), we get `"cot A"/(1 - tan "A") + "tan A"/(1 - cot "A")` = 1 + tan A + cot A = sec A . cosec A + 1
items.solution_html:
`"cot A"/(1 - tan "A") + "tan A"/(1 - cot"A")`

= `((cos "A")/(sin "A"))/(1 - (sin "A")/(cos "A")) + ((sin "A")/(cos "A"))/(1 - (cos "A")/(sin "A"))`

= `((cos "A")/(sin "A"))/((cos "A" -¬† sin "A")/(cos "A")) + ((sin "A")/(cos "A"))/((sin "A" -¬† cos "A")/(sin "A"))`

= `"cos A"/"sin A" xx "cos A"/(cos "A" - sin "A") + "sin A"/"cos A" xx "sin A"/(sin "A" - cos "A")`

= `(cos^2"A")/(sin "A"(cos "A" - sin "A")) + (sin^2"A")/(cos"A"(sin"A" - cos"A"))`

= `1/(sin "A" - cos "A") ((-cos^3"A" + sin^3"A")/(sin"A" cos"A"))`

= `1/(sin"A" - cos"A")((sin^3"A" - cos^3"A")/(sin"A" cos"A"))`

= `1/(sin"A" - cos"A")xx ((sin"A" - cos"A")(sin^2"A" + sin"A" cos"A" + cos^2"A"))/(sin"A" cos"A")`¬† ......[‚ąĶ a3 ‚Äď b3 = (a ‚Äď b)(a2 + ab + b2)]

= `(sin^2"A" +sin"A" cos"A" + cos^2"A")/(sin"A" cos"A"`¬† ......(i)

= `(1 + sin"A" cos"A")/(sin"A" cos"A")`¬† ¬†.....[‚ąĶ sin2A + cos2A = 1]

= `1/(sin"A" cos"A") + (sin"A" cos"A")/(sin"A" cos"A")`

= cosec A sec A + 1¬† .....(ii)

`"cot A"/(1 - tan "A") + "tan A"/(1 - cot "A")`

= `(sin^2"A" + sin"A" cos"A" + cos^2"A")/(sin"A" cos"A")`¬† ¬† ¬†......[From (i)]

= `(sin^2"A")/(sin"A" cos"A") + "sin A cos A"/"sin A cos A" + (cos^2"A")/"sin A cos A"`

= `"sin A"/"cos A" + 1 + "cos A"/"sin A"`

= tan A + 1 + cot A¬† ¬† ......(iii)

From (ii) and (iii), we get

`"cot¬† A"/(1¬† - tan "A") + "tan A"/(1 -¬† cot "A")` = 1 + tan A + cot A = sec A . cosec A + 1

items.title: Prove that cot2őł √ó sec2őł = cot2őł + 1 - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: Prove that cot 2 őł √ó sec 2 őł = cot 2 őł + 1
items.solution_text: L.H.S = cot 2 őł √ó sec 2 őł = `(cos^2theta)/(sin^2theta) xx 1/(cos^2theta)` = `1/(sin^2theta)` = cosec 2 őł = 1 + cot 2 őł ......[‚ąĶ 1 + cot 2 őł = cosec 2 őł] = R.H.S ‚ąī cot 2 őł √ó sec 2 őł = cot 2 őł + 1
items.solution_html:
L.H.S = cot2őł √ó sec2őł

= `(cos^2theta)/(sin^2theta) xx 1/(cos^2theta)`

= `1/(sin^2theta)`

= cosec2őł

= 1 + cot2őł¬† ¬† ......[‚ąĶ 1 + cot2őł = cosec2őł]

= R.H.S

‚ąī cot2őł √ó sec2őł = cot2őł + 1

items.title: Prove that cot2őł ‚Äď tan2őł = cosec2őł ‚Äď sec2őł - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: Prove that cot 2 őł ‚Äď tan 2 őł = cosec 2 őł ‚Äď sec 2 őł
items.solution_text: L.H.S = cot 2 őł ‚Äď tan 2 őł = (cosec 2 őł ‚ąí 1) ‚ąí (sec 2 őł ‚ąí 1) ......`[(because tan^2theta = sec^2theta - 1),(cot^2theta = "cosec"^2 theta - 1)]` = cosec 2 őł ‚ąí 1 ‚ąí sec 2 őł + 1 = cosec 2 őł ‚ąí sec 2 őł = R.H.S ‚ąī cot 2 őł ‚Äď tan 2 őł = cosec 2 őł ‚Äď sec 2 őł
items.solution_html:
L.H.S = cot2őł ‚Äď tan2őł

= (cosec2őł ‚ąí 1) ‚ąí (sec2őł ‚ąí 1)¬† ¬† ......`[(because tan^2theta = sec^2theta - 1),(cot^2theta = "cosec"^2 theta - 1)]`

= cosec2őł ‚ąí 1 ‚ąí sec2őł + 1

= cosec2őł ‚ąí sec2őł

= R.H.S

‚ąī cot2őł ‚Äď tan2őł = cosec2őł ‚Äď sec2őł¬†

items.title: Prove that cotA+cosec A-1cotA-cosec A+1=1+cosAsin A - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: Prove that `(cot "A" + "cosec A" - 1)/(cot"A" - "cosec A" + 1) = (1 + cos "A")/"sin A"`
items.solution_text: L.H.S = `(cot "A" + "cosec A" - 1)/(cot"A" - "cosec A" + 1)` = `(cot"A" + "cosec A" - ("cosec"^2"A" - cot^2"A"))/(cot"A" - "cosec A" + 1)` .....`[(because 1 + cot^2"A" = "cosec"^2"A"),(therefore "cosec"^2"A" - cot^2"A" = 1)]` = `(cot"A" + "cosec A" - ("cosec A" + cot"A")("cosec A" - cot"A"))/(cot"A" - "cosec A" + 1)` .....[‚ąĶ a 2 ‚Äď b 2 = (a + b) (a ‚Äď b)] = `((cot"A" + "cosec A")(1 - "cosec A" + cot "A"))/(cot"A" - "cosec A" + 1)` = cot A + cosec A = `"cos A"/"sin A" + 1/"sin A"` = `(cos "A" + 1)/"sin A"` = R.H.S ‚ąī `(cot "A" + "cosec A" - 1)/(cot"A" - "cosec A" + 1) = (1 + cos "A")/"sin A"`
items.solution_html:
L.H.S = `(cot "A" + "cosec A" - 1)/(cot"A" - "cosec A" + 1)`

= `(cot"A" + "cosec A" - ("cosec"^2"A" - cot^2"A"))/(cot"A" - "cosec A" + 1)`¬† ¬†.....`[(because 1 + cot^2"A" = "cosec"^2"A"),(therefore "cosec"^2"A" - cot^2"A" = 1)]`

= `(cot"A" + "cosec A" - ("cosec A" + cot"A")("cosec A" - cot"A"))/(cot"A" - "cosec A" + 1)`¬† ¬†.....[‚ąĶ a2 ‚Äď b2 = (a + b) (a ‚Äď b)]

= `((cot"A" + "cosec A")(1 - "cosec A" + cot "A"))/(cot"A" - "cosec A" + 1)`

= cot A + cosec A

= `"cos A"/"sin A" + 1/"sin A"`

= `(cos "A" + 1)/"sin A"`

= R.H.S

‚ąī `(cot "A" + "cosec¬† A" - 1)/(cot"A" - "cosec¬† A" + 1) = (1 + cos "A")/"sin A"`

items.title: Prove that sec2őł ‚ąí cos2őł = tan2őł + sin2őł - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: Prove that sec 2 őł ‚ąí cos 2 őł = tan 2 őł + sin 2 őł
items.solution_text: L.H.S = sec 2 őł ‚ąí cos 2 őł = 1 + tan 2 őł ‚Äď cos 2 őł .......[‚ąĶ 1 + tan 2 őł = sec 2 őł] = tan 2 őł + (1 ‚Äď cos 2 őł) = tan 2 őł + sin 2 őł ......`[(because sin^2theta +cos^2theta = 1),(therefore 1 - cos^2theta = sin^2theta)]` = R.H.S ‚ąī sec 2 őł ‚ąí cos 2 őł = tan 2 őł + sin 2 őł
items.solution_html:
L.H.S = sec2őł ‚ąí cos2őł

= 1 + tan2őł ‚Äď cos2őł¬† ¬† ¬†.......[‚ąĶ 1 + tan2őł = sec2őł]

= tan2őł + (1 ‚Äď cos2őł)

= tan2őł + sin2őł¬† ¬† ¬† ¬†......`[(because sin^2theta +cos^2theta = 1),(therefore 1 - cos^2theta = sin^2theta)]`

= R.H.S

‚ąī sec2őł ‚ąí cos2őł = tan2őł + sin2őł

items.title: Prove that sec2őł ‚Äď cos2őł = tan2őł + sin2őł - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: Prove that sec 2 őł ‚Äď cos 2 őł = tan 2 őł + sin 2 őł
items.solution_text: L.H.S = sec 2 őł ‚Äď cos 2 őł = sec 2 őł ‚Äď (1 ‚Äď sin 2 őł) ......`[(because sin^2theta + cos^2theta = 1),(therefore 1 - sin^2theta = cos^2theta)]` = sec 2 őł ‚Äď 1 + sin 2 őł = tan 2 őł + sin 2 őł ......`[(because 1 + tan^2theta = sec^2theta),(therefore tan^2theta = sec^2theta - 1)]` = R.H.S ‚ąī sec 2 őł ‚Äď cos 2 őł = tan 2 őł + sin 2 őł
items.solution_html:
L.H.S = sec2őł ‚Äď cos2őł

= sec2őł ‚Äď (1 ‚Äď sin2őł)¬† ¬† ......`[(because sin^2theta + cos^2theta = 1),(therefore 1 - sin^2theta = cos^2theta)]`

= sec2őł ‚Äď 1 + sin2őł

= tan2őł + sin2őł¬† ¬† ¬†......`[(because 1 + tan^2theta = sec^2theta),(therefore tan^2theta = sec^2theta - 1)]`

= R.H.S

‚ąī sec2őł ‚Äď cos2őł = tan2őł + sin2őł

items.title: Prove that sec2őł + cosec2őł = sec2őł √ó cosec2őł - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: Prove that sec 2 őł + cosec 2 őł = sec 2 őł √ó cosec 2 őł
items.solution_text: L.H.S = sec 2 őł + cosec 2 őł = `1/(cos^2theta) + 1/(sin^2theta)` = `(sin^2theta + cos^2theta)/(cos^2theta*sin^2theta)` = `1/(cos^2theta*sin^2theta)` ......[‚ąĶ sin 2 őł + cos 2 őł = 1] = `1/(cos^2theta) xx 1/(sin^2theta)` = sec 2 őł √ó cosec 2 őł = R.H.S ‚ąī sec 2 őł + cosec 2 őł = sec 2 őł √ó cosec 2 őł
items.solution_html:
L.H.S = sec2őł + cosec2őł

= `1/(cos^2theta) + 1/(sin^2theta)`

= `(sin^2theta + cos^2theta)/(cos^2theta*sin^2theta)`

= `1/(cos^2theta*sin^2theta)`¬† ¬†......[‚ąĶ sin2őł + cos2őł = 1]

= `1/(cos^2theta) xx 1/(sin^2theta)`

= sec2őł √ó cosec2őł

= R.H.S

‚ąī sec2őł + cosec2őł = sec2őł √ó cosec2őł

items.title: Prove that sec2A ‚Äď cosec2A = AAA2sin2A-1sin2A‚čÖcos2A - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: Prove that sec 2 A ‚Äď cosec 2 A = `(2sin^2"A" - 1)/(sin^2"A"*cos^2"A")`
items.solution_text: L.H.S = sec 2 A ‚Äď cosec 2 A = `1/(cos^2"A") - 1/(sin^2"A")` = `(sin^2"A" - cos^2"A")/(cos^2"A"*sin^2"A")` = `(sin^2"A" - (1 - sin^2"A"))/(sin^2"A"*cos^2"A")` .....`[(because sin^2"A" + cos^2"A" = 1),(therefore 1 - sin^2"A" = cos^2"A")]` = `(sin^2"A" - 1 + sin^2"A")/(sin^2"A"*cos^2"A")` = `(2sin^2"A" - 1)/(sin^2"A"*cos^2"A")` = R.H.S ‚ąī sec 2 A ‚Äď cosec 2 A = `(2sin^2"A" - 1)/(sin^2"A"*cos^2"A")`
items.solution_html:
L.H.S = sec2A ‚Äď cosec2A

= `1/(cos^2"A") - 1/(sin^2"A")`

= `(sin^2"A" - cos^2"A")/(cos^2"A"*sin^2"A")`

= `(sin^2"A" - (1 - sin^2"A"))/(sin^2"A"*cos^2"A")` .....`[(because sin^2"A" + cos^2"A" = 1),(therefore 1 - sin^2"A" = cos^2"A")]`

= `(sin^2"A" - 1 + sin^2"A")/(sin^2"A"*cos^2"A")`

= `(2sin^2"A" - 1)/(sin^2"A"*cos^2"A")`

= R.H.S

‚ąī sec2A ‚Äď cosec2A = `(2sin^2"A" - 1)/(sin^2"A"*cos^2"A")`

items.title: Prove that secAtanA+cotA = sin A - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: Prove that `sec"A"/(tan "A" + cot "A")` = sin A
items.solution_text: L.H.S = `sec"A"/(tan "A" + cot "A")` = `sec"A"/((sin"A")/(cos"A") + (cos"A")/(sin"A"))` = `sec"A"/((sin^2"A" + cos^2"A")/(cos"A" sin"A"))` = `sec"A"/(1/(cos"A" sin"A"))` ......[‚ąĶ sin 2 A + cos 2 A = 1] = sec A cos A sin A = `1/cos"A" xx cos "A" sin "A"` = sin A = R.H.S. ‚ąī `sec"A"/(tan "A" + cot "A")` = sin A
items.solution_html:
L.H.S = `sec"A"/(tan "A" + cot "A")`

= `sec"A"/((sin"A")/(cos"A") + (cos"A")/(sin"A"))`

= `sec"A"/((sin^2"A" + cos^2"A")/(cos"A" sin"A"))`

= `sec"A"/(1/(cos"A" sin"A"))`¬† ¬†......[‚ąĶ sin2A + cos2A = 1]

= sec A cos A sin A

= `1/cos"A" xx cos "A" sin "A"`

= sin A

= R.H.S.

‚ąī `sec"A"/(tan "A" + cot "A")` = sin A

items.title: Prove that sin őł (1 ‚Äď tan őł) ‚Äď cos őł (1 ‚Äď cot őł) = cosec őł ‚Äď sec őł - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: Prove that sin őł (1 ‚Äď tan őł) ‚Äď cos őł (1 ‚Äď cot őł) = cosec őł ‚Äď sec őł
items.solution_text: L.H.S = sin őł (1 ‚Äď tan őł) ‚Äď cos őł (1 ‚Äď cot őł) = `sintheta (1 - (sintheta)/(costheta)) - costheta (1 - (costheta)/(sintheta))` = `sintheta - (sin^2theta)/costheta - costheta + (cos^2theta)/sintheta` = `sintheta + (cos^2theta)/sintheta - (sin^2theta)/costheta - costheta` = `(sin^2theta + cos^2theta)/sintheta - ((sin^2theta + cos^2theta)/costheta)` = `1/sintheta - 1/costheta` ......[‚ąĶ sin 2 őł + cos 2 őł = 1] = cosec őł ‚Äď sec őł = R.H.S ‚ąī sin őł (1 ‚Äď tan őł) ‚Äď cos őł (1 ‚Äď cot őł) = cosec őł ‚Äď sec őł
items.solution_html:
L.H.S = sin őł (1 ‚Äď tan őł) ‚Äď cos őł (1 ‚Äď cot őł)

= `sintheta (1 - (sintheta)/(costheta)) - costheta (1 - (costheta)/(sintheta))`

= `sintheta - (sin^2theta)/costheta - costheta + (cos^2theta)/sintheta`

= `sintheta + (cos^2theta)/sintheta - (sin^2theta)/costheta - costheta`

= `(sin^2theta + cos^2theta)/sintheta - ((sin^2theta + cos^2theta)/costheta)`

= `1/sintheta - 1/costheta`¬† ¬†......[‚ąĶ sin2őł + cos2őł = 1]

= cosec őł ‚Äď sec őł

= R.H.S

‚ąī sin őł (1 ‚Äď tan őł) ‚Äď cos őł (1 ‚Äď cot őł) = cosec őł ‚Äď sec őł

items.title: Prove that sinőł+cosec őłsinőł = 2 + cot2őł - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: Prove that `(sintheta + "cosec" theta)/sin theta` = 2 + cot 2 őł
items.solution_text: L.H.S = `(sintheta + "cosec" theta)/sin theta` = `sintheta/sintheta + ("cosec"theta)/sintheta` = 1 + cosec őł √ó cosec őł ......`[‚ąĶ "cosec" theta = 1/sin theta]` = 1 + cosec 2 őł = 1 + 1 + cot 2 őł .......[‚ąĶ 1 + cot 2 őł = cosec 2 őł] = 2 + cot 2 őł = R.H.S ‚ąī `(sintheta + "cosec" theta)/sin theta` = 2 + cot 2 őł
items.solution_html:
L.H.S = `(sintheta + "cosec"¬† theta)/sin theta`

= `sintheta/sintheta + ("cosec"theta)/sintheta`

= 1 + cosec őł √ó cosec őł¬† ¬†......`[‚ąĶ "cosec"¬† theta = 1/sin theta]`

= 1 + cosec2őł

= 1 + 1 + cot2őł¬† ¬† ¬† .......[‚ąĶ 1 + cot2őł = cosec2őł]

= 2 + cot2őł

= R.H.S

‚ąī `(sintheta + "cosec"¬† theta)/sin theta` = 2 + cot2őł

items.title: Prove that sinőłsecőł+1+sinőłsecőł-1 = 2 cot őł - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: Prove that `sintheta/(sectheta+ 1) +sintheta/(sectheta - 1)` = 2 cot őł
items.solution_text: L.H.S = `sintheta/(sectheta+ 1) +sintheta/(sectheta - 1)` = `sintheta/(1/costheta + 1) + sintheta/(1/costheta - 1` = `sintheta/((1 + costheta)/costheta) + sintheta/((1 - costheta)/(costheta))` = `(sintheta costheta)/(1 + costheta) + (sintheta costheta)/(1 - costheta)` = `sin theta costheta (1 /(1 + costheta) + 1/(1 - costheta))` = `sintheta costheta [(1 - costheta + 1 + costheta)/((1 + costheta)(1 - costheta))]` = `sintheta costheta (2/(1 - cos^2theta))` ......[‚ąĶ (a + b)(a ‚Äď b) = a 2 ‚Äď b 2 ] = `sintheta costheta xx 2/(sin^2theta)` .....`[(because sin^2theta + cos^2theta = 1),(therefore 1 - cos^2theta = sin^2theta)]` = `2 xx (costheta)/(sintheta)` = 2cot őł = R.H.S ‚ąī `sintheta/(sectheta+ 1) +sintheta/(sectheta - 1)` = 2 cot őł
items.solution_html:
L.H.S = `sintheta/(sectheta+ 1) +sintheta/(sectheta - 1)`¬†

= `sintheta/(1/costheta + 1) + sintheta/(1/costheta - 1`

= `sintheta/((1 + costheta)/costheta) + sintheta/((1 - costheta)/(costheta))`

= `(sintheta costheta)/(1 + costheta) + (sintheta costheta)/(1 - costheta)`

= `sin theta costheta (1 /(1 + costheta) + 1/(1 -¬† costheta))`

= `sintheta costheta [(1 - costheta + 1 + costheta)/((1 + costheta)(1 - costheta))]`

= `sintheta costheta (2/(1 - cos^2theta))`¬† ¬†......[‚ąĶ (a + b)(a ‚Äď b) = a2 ‚Äď b2]

= `sintheta costheta xx 2/(sin^2theta)`¬† ¬†.....`[(because sin^2theta + cos^2theta = 1),(therefore 1 - cos^2theta = sin^2theta)]`

= `2 xx (costheta)/(sintheta)`

= 2cot őł

= R.H.S

‚ąī `sintheta/(sectheta+ 1) +sintheta/(sectheta - 1)` = 2 cot őł

items.title: Prove that sinőł+tanőłcothő∑ = tan őł(1 + sec őł) - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: Prove that `(sintheta + tantheta)/cos theta` = tan őł(1 + sec őł)
items.solution_text: L.H.S = `(sintheta + tantheta)/cos theta` = `sintheta/costheta + tantheta/costheta` = tan őł + tan őł sec őł = tan őł(1 + sec őł) = R.H.S ‚ąī `(sintheta + tantheta)/cos theta` = tan őł(1 + sec őł)
items.solution_html:
L.H.S = `(sintheta + tantheta)/cos theta`

= `sintheta/costheta + tantheta/costheta`

= tan őł + tan őł sec őł

= tan őł(1 + sec őł)

= R.H.S

‚ąī `(sintheta + tantheta)/cos theta` = tan őł(1 + sec őł)

items.title: Prove that sin2őłcosőł+cosőł = sec őł - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: Prove that `(sin^2theta)/(cos theta) + cos theta` = sec őł
items.solution_text: L.H.S = `(sin^2theta)/(cos theta) + cos theta` = `(sin^2theta + cos^2theta)/costheta` = `1/costheta` ......[‚ąĶ sin 2 őł + cos 2 őł = 1] = sec őł = R.H.S ‚ąī `(sin^2theta)/(cos theta) + cos theta` = sec őł
items.solution_html:
L.H.S = `(sin^2theta)/(cos theta) + cos theta`

= `(sin^2theta + cos^2theta)/costheta`

= `1/costheta`¬† ¬† ¬†......[‚ąĶ sin2őł + cos2őł = 1]

= sec őł

= R.H.S

‚ąī `(sin^2theta)/(cos theta) + cos theta` = sec őł

items.title: Prove that sin2A . tan A + cos2A . cot A + 2 sin A . cos A = tan A + cot A - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: Prove that sin 2 A . tan A + cos 2 A . cot A + 2 sin A . cos A = tan A + cot A
items.solution_text: L.H.S = sin 2 A . tan A + cos 2 A . cot A + 2 sin A . cos A = `sin^2"A"* (sin "A")/(cos "A") + cos^2"A"* (cos"A")/(sin"A") + 2sin"A" *cos"A"` = `(sin^3"A")/"cosA" + (cos^3"A")/"sinA" + 2sin"A"*cos"A"` = `(sin^4"A" + cos^4"A" + 2sin^2"A"cos^2"A")/(sin"A"cos"A")` = `(sin^2"A" + cos^2"A")^2/(sin"A"cos"A")` .....[‚ąĶ a 2 + b 2 + 2ab = (a + b) 2 ] = `1^2/(sin"A"cos"A")` ......[‚ąĶ sin 2 A + cos 2 A = 1] = `1/(sin"A"cos"A")` = `(sin^2"A"+ cos^2"A")/(sin"A"cos"A")` ......[‚ąĶ 1 = sin 2 A + cos 2 A] = `(sin^2"A")/(sin"A"cos"A") + (cos^2"A")/(sin"A"cos"A")` = `"sin A"/"cos A" + "cos A"/"sin A"` = tan A + cot A = R.H.S ‚ąī sin 2 A . tan A + cos 2 A . cot A + 2 sin A . cos A = tan A + cot A
items.solution_html:
L.H.S = sin2A . tan A + cos2A . cot A + 2 sin A . cos A

= `sin^2"A"* (sin "A")/(cos "A")¬† + cos^2"A"* (cos"A")/(sin"A") + 2sin"A" *cos"A"`

= `(sin^3"A")/"cosA" + (cos^3"A")/"sinA" + 2sin"A"*cos"A"`

= `(sin^4"A" + cos^4"A" + 2sin^2"A"cos^2"A")/(sin"A"cos"A")`

= `(sin^2"A" + cos^2"A")^2/(sin"A"cos"A")` .....[‚ąĶ a2 + b2 + 2ab = (a + b)2]

= `1^2/(sin"A"cos"A")`¬† ¬† ......[‚ąĶ sin2A + cos2A = 1]

=¬† `1/(sin"A"cos"A")` ¬†

= `(sin^2"A"+ cos^2"A")/(sin"A"cos"A")` ¬†......[‚ąĶ 1 = sin2A + cos2A]

= `(sin^2"A")/(sin"A"cos"A") + (cos^2"A")/(sin"A"cos"A")`

= `"sin A"/"cos A" + "cos A"/"sin A"`

= tan A + cot A

= R.H.S

‚ąī sin2A . tan A + cos2A . cot A + 2 sin A . cos A = tan A + cot A

items.title: Prove that sin4A ‚Äď cos4A = 1 ‚Äď 2cos2A - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: Prove that sin 4 A ‚Äď cos 4 A = 1 ‚Äď 2cos 2 A
items.solution_text: L.H.S = sin 4 A ‚Äď cos 4 A = (sin 2 A) 2 ‚Äď (cos 2 A) 2 = (sin 2 A + cos 2 A)(sin 2 A ‚Äď cos 2 A) .....[‚ąĶ a 2 ‚Äď b 2 = (a + b)(a ‚Äď b)] = (1)(sin 2 A ‚Äď cos 2 A) ......[‚ąĶ sin 2 A + cos 2 A = 1] = sin 2 A ‚Äď cos 2 A = (1 ‚Äď cos 2 A) ‚Äď cos 2 A ......`[(because sin^2"A" + cos^2"A" = 1),(therefore 1 - cos^2"" = sin^2"A")]` = 1 ‚Äď 2cos 2 A = R.H.S ‚ąī sin 4 A ‚Äď cos 4 A = 1 ‚Äď 2cos 2 A
items.solution_html:
L.H.S = sin4A ‚Äď cos4A¬†

= (sin2A)2 ‚Äď (cos2A)2

= (sin2A + cos2A)(sin2A ‚Äď cos2A)¬† ¬†.....[‚ąĶ¬† a2 ‚Äď b2 = (a + b)(a ‚Äď b)]

= (1)(sin2A ‚Äď cos2A)¬† ¬† ¬†......[‚ąĶ sin2A + cos2A = 1]

= sin2A ‚Äď cos2A

= (1 ‚Äď cos2A) ‚Äď cos2A¬† ¬†......`[(because sin^2"A" + cos^2"A" = 1),(therefore 1 -¬† cos^2"" = sin^2"A")]`

= 1 ‚Äď 2cos2A

= R.H.S

‚ąī sin4A ‚Äď cos4A = 1 ‚Äď 2cos2A

items.title: Prove that sin6A + cos6A = 1 ‚Äď 3sin2A . cos2A - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: Prove that sin 6 A + cos 6 A = 1 ‚Äď 3sin 2 A . cos 2 A
items.solution_text: L.H.S = sin 6 A + cos 6 A = (sin 2 A) 3 + (cos 2 A) 3 = (1 ‚Äď cos 2 A) 3 + (cos 2 A) 3 ......`[(because sin^2"A" + cos^2"A" = 1),(therefore 1 - cos^2"A" = sin^2A")]` = 1 ‚Äď 3cos 2 A + 3(cos 2 A) 2 ‚Äď (cos 2 A) 3 + cos 6 A ......[‚ąĶ (a ‚Äď b) 3 = a 3 ‚Äď 3a 2 b + 3ab 2 ‚Äď b 3 ] = 1 ‚Äď 3 cos 2 A(1 ‚Äď cos 2 A) ‚Äď cos 6 A + cos 6 A = 1 ‚Äď 3 cos 2 A sin 2 A = R.H.S ‚ąī sin 6 A + cos 6 A = 1 ‚Äď 3sin 2 A . cos 2 A
items.solution_html:
L.H.S = sin6A + cos6A

= (sin2A)3 + (cos2A)3¬† ¬†

= (1 ‚Äď cos2A)3 + (cos2A)3 ¬† ¬†......`[(because sin^2"A" + cos^2"A" = 1),(therefore 1 - cos^2"A" = sin^2A")]`

= 1 ‚Äď 3cos2A + 3(cos2A)2 ‚Äď (cos2A)3 + cos6A¬† ¬†......[‚ąĶ (a ‚Äď b)3 = a3 ‚Äď 3a2b + 3ab2 ‚Äď b3]

= 1 ‚Äď 3 cos2A(1 ‚Äď cos2A) ‚Äď cos6A + cos6A

= 1 ‚Äď 3 cos2A sin2A

= R.H.S

‚ąī sin6A + cos6A = 1 ‚Äď 3sin2A . cos2A

items.title: Prove that tan(90-őł)+cot(90-őł)cosecőł = sec őł - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: Prove that `(tan(90 - theta) + cot(90 - theta))/("cosec" theta)` = sec őł
items.solution_text: L.H.S = `(tan(90 - theta) + cot(90 - theta))/("cosec" theta)` = `1/("cosec" theta)(cottheta + tantheta)` .....`[(because tan(90 - theta) = cot theta),(cot(90 - theta) = tantheta)]` = sin őł (cot őł + tan őł) = `sintheta ((costheta)/(sintheta) + (sintheta)/(costheta))` = `sintheta ((cos^2theta + sin^2theta)/(sintheta costheta))` = `sintheta (1/(sintheta costheta))` ......[‚ąĶ sin 2 őł + cos 2 őł = 1] = `1/costheta` = sec őł = R.H.S ‚ąī `(tan(90 - theta) + cot(90 - theta))/("cosec" theta)` = sec őł
items.solution_html:
L.H.S = `(tan(90 - theta) + cot(90 - theta))/("cosec"¬† theta)`

= `1/("cosec"¬† theta)(cottheta + tantheta)`¬† .....`[(because tan(90 - theta) = cot theta),(cot(90 - theta) = tantheta)]`

= sin őł (cot őł + tan őł)

= `sintheta ((costheta)/(sintheta) + (sintheta)/(costheta))`

= `sintheta ((cos^2theta + sin^2theta)/(sintheta costheta))`

= `sintheta (1/(sintheta costheta))`¬† ¬†......[‚ąĶ sin2őł ¬†+ cos2őł = 1]

= `1/costheta`

= sec őł

= R.H.S

‚ąī `(tan(90 - theta) + cot(90 - theta))/("cosec"¬† theta)` = sec őł

items.title: Ratio of areas of two similar triangles is 9 : 25. _______ is the ratio of their corresponding sides. - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: Ratio of areas of two similar triangles is 9 : 25. _______ is the ratio of their corresponding sides.
items.solution_text: 3: 5 Let őĒABC and őĒPQR be two similar triangles. According to the given condition, `"A(őĒABC)"/"A(őĒPQR)" = 9/25` ...(Given) But `"A(őĒABC)"/"A(őĒPQR)" = "AB"^2/"PQ"^2` ...(By the theorem of areas of similar triangles) ‚ąī `"AB"^2/"PQ"^2 = 9/25` ‚ąī `"AB"/"PQ" = 3/5` ‚ąī 3: 5 is the ratio of their corresponding sides.
items.solution_html:
3: 5

Let őĒABC and őĒPQR be two similar triangles.¬†¬†

According to the given condition,

`"A(őĒABC)"/"A(őĒPQR)" = 9/25`¬† ¬† ¬† ...(Given)

But `"A(őĒABC)"/"A(őĒPQR)" = "AB"^2/"PQ"^2` ...(By the theorem of areas of similar triangles)

‚ąī `"AB"^2/"PQ"^2 = 9/25`

‚ąī `"AB"/"PQ" = 3/5`

‚ąī 3: 5 is the ratio of their corresponding sides.¬†

items.title: Ratio of corresponding sides of two similar triangles is 4:7, then find the ratio of their areas = ? - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: Ratio of corresponding sides of two similar triangles is 4:7, then find the ratio of their areas = ?
items.solution_text: Let the corresponding sides of similar triangles be s 1 and s 2 . Let A 1 and A 2 be their corresponding areas. s 1 : s 2 = 4 : 7 ......[Given] ‚ąī `"s"_1/"s"_2= 4/7` ......(i) by theorem of areas of similar triangles, `"A"_1/"A"_2 = "s"_1^2/"s"_2^2` `"A"_1/"A"_2 = ("s"_1/"s"_2)^2` `"A"_1/"A"_2 = (4/7)^2` ......[From (i)] `"A"_1/"A"_2 = 16/49` ‚ąī Ratio of areas of similar triangles = 16 : 49
items.solution_html:
Let the corresponding sides of similar triangles be s1 and s2.

Let A1 and A2 be their corresponding areas.

s1 : s2 = 4 : 7¬† ¬† ¬† ¬† ......[Given]

‚ąī `"s"_1/"s"_2= 4/7`¬† ¬† ¬†......(i)

by theorem of areas of similar triangles,

`"A"_1/"A"_2 = "s"_1^2/"s"_2^2`¬†¬†

`"A"_1/"A"_2 = ("s"_1/"s"_2)^2`

`"A"_1/"A"_2 = (4/7)^2`¬† ¬† ......[From (i)]

`"A"_1/"A"_2 = 16/49`

‚ąī Ratio of areas of similar triangles = 16 : 49

items.title: Seg RM and seg RN are tangent segments of a circle with centre O. Prove that seg OR bisects ‚ą†MRN as well as ‚ą†MON with the help of activity. - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: Seg RM and seg RN are tangent segments of a circle with centre O. Prove that seg OR bisects ‚ą†MRN as well as ‚ą†MON with the help of activity. Proof: In ‚ąÜRMO and ‚ąÜRNO, ‚ą†RMO ‚ČÖ ‚ą†RNO = 90¬į ......[`square`] hypt OR ‚ČÖ hypt OR ......[`square`] seg OM ‚ČÖ seg `square` ......[Radii of the same circle] ‚ąī ‚ąÜRMO ‚ČÖ ‚ąÜRNO ......[`square`] ‚ą†MOR ‚ČÖ ‚ą†NOR Similairy ‚ą†MRO ‚ČÖ `square` ......[`square`]
items.solution_text: Proof: In ‚ąÜRMO and ‚ąÜRNO, ‚ą†RMO ‚ČÖ ‚ą†RNO = 90¬į ......[ Tangent theorem ] hypt OR ‚ČÖ hypt OR ......[ Common side ] seg OM ‚ČÖ seg ON ......[Radii of the same circle] ‚ąī ‚ąÜRMO ‚ČÖ ‚ąÜRNO ......[ By Hypotenuse side test ] ‚ą†MOR ‚ČÖ ‚ą†NOR Similarly ‚ą†MRO ‚ČÖ ‚ą†NRO ......[ Corresponding angles of congruent triangles ]
items.solution_html:
Proof: In ‚ąÜRMO and ‚ąÜRNO,

‚ą†RMO ‚ČÖ ‚ą†RNO = 90¬į¬† ¬†......[Tangent theorem]

hypt OR ‚ČÖ hypt OR¬† ¬† ......[Common side]

seg OM ‚ČÖ seg ON¬† ¬† ......[Radii of the same circle]

‚ąī ‚ąÜRMO ‚ČÖ ‚ąÜRNO¬† ¬† ¬† ......[By Hypotenuse side test]

‚ą†MOR ‚ČÖ ‚ą†NOR

Similarly ‚ą†MRO ‚ČÖ ‚ą†NRO¬† ¬† ......[Corresponding angles of congruent triangles]

items.title: Show that A(1, 2), (1, 6), C(1 + 2 3, 4) are vertices of a equilateral triangle - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: Show that A(1, 2), (1, 6), C(1 + 2 `sqrt(3)`, 4) are vertices of a equilateral triangle
items.solution_text: Distance between two points = `sqrt((x_2 - x_1)^2 + (y_2 - y_1)^2` By distance formula, AB = `sqrt((1- 1)^2 + (6 - 2)^2` = `sqrt(0^2 + 4^2)` = `sqrt(4^2)` = 4 ......(i) BC = `sqrt((1 + 2sqrt(3) - 1)^2 + (4 - 6)^2` = `sqrt((2sqrt(3))^2 + (-2)^2` = `sqrt(12 + 4)` = `sqrt(16)` = 4 .....(ii) AC = `sqrt((1 + 2sqrt(3) - 1)^2 + (4 -2)^2` = `sqrt((2sqrt(3))^2 + 2^2` = `sqrt(12 + 4)` = `sqrt(16)` = 4 ......(iii) ‚ąī AB = BC = AC ......[From (i), (ii) and (iii)] ‚ąī ‚ąÜABC is an equilateral triangle. ‚ąī Points A, B and C are the vertices of an equilateral triangle.
items.solution_html:
Distance between two points = `sqrt((x_2 -¬† x_1)^2 + (y_2 - y_1)^2`

By distance formula,

AB = `sqrt((1- 1)^2 + (6 - 2)^2`

= `sqrt(0^2 + 4^2)`

= `sqrt(4^2)`

= 4¬† ¬† ......(i)

BC = `sqrt((1 + 2sqrt(3) - 1)^2 + (4 - 6)^2`

= `sqrt((2sqrt(3))^2 + (-2)^2`

= `sqrt(12 + 4)`

= `sqrt(16)`

= 4¬† ¬† .....(ii)

AC = `sqrt((1 + 2sqrt(3) - 1)^2 + (4 -2)^2`

= `sqrt((2sqrt(3))^2 + 2^2`

= `sqrt(12 + 4)`

= `sqrt(16)`

= 4¬† ¬†......(iii)

‚ąī AB = BC = AC¬† ¬†......[From (i), (ii) and (iii)]

‚ąī ‚ąÜABC is an equilateral triangle.

‚ąī Points A, B and C are the vertices of an equilateral triangle.

items.title: Show that P(‚Äď 2, 2), Q(2, 2) and R(2, 7) are vertices of a right angled triangle - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: Show that P(‚Äď 2, 2), Q(2, 2) and R(2, 7) are vertices of a right angled triangle
items.solution_text: Distance between two points = `sqrt((x_2 - x_1)^2 + (y_2 - y_1)^2` By distance formula, PQ = `sqrt([2 - (-2)]^2 + (2 - 2)^2` = `sqrt((2 + 2)^2 + (0)^2` = `sqrt((4)^2` = 4 .....(i) QR = `sqrt((2 - 2)^2 + (7 - 2)^2` = `sqrt((0)^2 + (5)^2` = `sqrt((5)^2` = 5 ......(ii) PR = `sqrt([2 -(-2)]^2 + (7 - 2)^2` = `sqrt((2 + 2)^2 + (5)^2` = `sqrt((4)^2 + (5)^2` = `sqrt(16 + 25)` = `sqrt(41)` Now, PR 2 = `(sqrt(41))^2` = 41 ......(iii) Consider, PQ 2 + QR 2 = 4 2 + 5 2 = 16 + 25 = 41 ......[From (i) and (ii)] ‚ąī PR 2 = PQ 2 + QR 2 ......[From (iii)] ‚ąī ‚ąÜPQR is a right angled triangle. ......[Converse of Pythagoras theorem] ‚ąī Points P, Q, and R are the vertices of a right angled triangle.
items.solution_html:
Distance between two points = `sqrt((x_2 - x_1)^2 + (y_2 - y_1)^2`

By distance formula,

PQ = `sqrt([2 - (-2)]^2 + (2 - 2)^2`

= `sqrt((2 + 2)^2 + (0)^2`

= `sqrt((4)^2`

= 4¬† ¬† .....(i)

QR = `sqrt((2 - 2)^2 + (7 - 2)^2`

= `sqrt((0)^2 + (5)^2`

= `sqrt((5)^2`

= 5¬† ¬†......(ii)

PR = `sqrt([2 -(-2)]^2 + (7 - 2)^2`

= `sqrt((2 + 2)^2 + (5)^2`

= `sqrt((4)^2 + (5)^2`

= `sqrt(16 + 25)`

= `sqrt(41)`

Now, PR2 = `(sqrt(41))^2` = 41¬† ¬†......(iii)

Consider, PQ2 + QR2

= 42 + 52

= 16 + 25

= 41¬† ¬† ......[From (i) and (ii)]

‚ąī PR2 = PQ2 + QR2 ¬† ......[From (iii)]

‚ąī ‚ąÜPQR is a right angled triangle.¬† ¬†......[Converse of Pythagoras theorem]

‚ąī Points P, Q, and R are the vertices of a right angled triangle.

items.title: Show that points A(‚Äď 4, ‚Äď7), B(‚Äď1, 2), C(8, 5) and D(5, ‚Äď 4) are the vertices of a parallelogram ABCD - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: Show that points A(‚Äď 4, ‚Äď7), B(‚Äď1, 2), C(8, 5) and D(5, ‚Äď 4) are the vertices of a parallelogram ABCD
items.solution_text: We know that, slope of line = `(y_2 - y_1)/(x_2 - x_1)` Slope of side AB = `(2 - (-7))/(-1 - (-4))` = `(2 + 7)/(-1 + 4)` = `9/3` = 3 ......(i) Slope of side BC = `(5 - 2)/(8 - (-1))` = `3/(8 + 1)` = `3/9` = `1/3` ......(ii) Slope of side CD = `(-4 - 5)/(5 - 8)` = `(-9)/(-3)` = 3 .....(iii) Slope of side AD = `(-4 - (-7))/(5 - (-4))` = `(-4 + 7)/(5 + 4)` =`3/9` = `1/3` ......(iv) ‚ąī Slope of side AB = Slope of side CD ......[From (i) and (iii)] ‚ąī Side AB || side CD ‚ąī Slope of side BC = Slope of side AD .......[From (ii) and (iv)] ‚ąī Side BC || side AD Both the pairs of opposite sides of ABCD are parallel ‚ąī ‚ĖĘABCD is a parallelogram. ‚ąī Points A(‚Äď 4, ‚Äď 7), B(‚Äď 1, 2), C(8, 5) and D(5, ‚Äď 4) are the vertices of a parallelogram.
items.solution_html:
We know that, slope of line = `(y_2 - y_1)/(x_2 - x_1)`

Slope of side AB = `(2 - (-7))/(-1 - (-4))`

= `(2 + 7)/(-1 + 4)`

= `9/3`

= 3¬† ¬† ......(i)

Slope of side BC = `(5 - 2)/(8 - (-1))`

= `3/(8 + 1)`

= `3/9`

= `1/3`¬† ......(ii)

Slope of side CD = `(-4 - 5)/(5 - 8)`

= `(-9)/(-3)`

= 3¬† ¬† ¬† ¬†.....(iii)

Slope of side AD = `(-4 - (-7))/(5 - (-4))`

= `(-4 + 7)/(5 + 4)`

=`3/9`

= `1/3`¬† ¬†......(iv)

‚ąī Slope of side AB = Slope of side CD¬† ¬†......[From (i) and (iii)]

‚ąī Side AB || side CD

‚ąī Slope of side BC = Slope of side AD¬† ¬†.......[From (ii) and (iv)]

‚ąī Side BC || side AD

Both the pairs of opposite sides of ABCD are parallel

‚ąī ‚ĖĘABCD is a parallelogram.

‚ąī Points A(‚Äď 4, ‚Äď 7), B(‚Äď 1, 2), C(8, 5) and D(5, ‚Äď 4) are the vertices of a parallelogram.

items.title: Show that tan 7¬į √ó tan 23¬į √ó tan 60¬į √ó tan 67¬į √ó tan 83¬į = 3 - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: Show that tan 7¬į √ó tan 23¬į √ó tan 60¬į √ó tan 67¬į √ó tan 83¬į = `sqrt(3)`
items.solution_text: L.H.S = tan 7¬į √ó tan 23¬į √ó tan 60¬į √ó tan 67¬į √ó tan 83¬į = tan 7¬į √ó tan 23¬į √ó `sqrt(3)` √ó tan(90¬į ‚Äď 23¬į) √ó tan(90¬į ‚Äď 7¬į) = `sqrt(3)` √ó [tan 7¬į √ó tan(90¬į ‚Äď 7¬į)] √ó [tan 23¬į √ó tan(90¬į ‚Äď 23¬į)] = `sqrt(3) xx 1 xx 1` ......[‚ąĶ tan őł √ó tan(90¬į ‚Äď őł) = 1] = `sqrt(3)` = R.H.S ‚ąī tan 7¬į √ó tan 23¬į √ó tan 60¬į √ó tan 67¬į √ó tan 83¬į = `sqrt(3)`
items.solution_html:
L.H.S = tan 7¬į √ó tan 23¬į √ó tan 60¬į √ó tan 67¬į √ó tan 83¬į

= tan 7¬į √ó tan 23¬į √ó `sqrt(3)` √ó tan(90¬į ‚Äď 23¬į) √ó tan(90¬į ‚Äď 7¬į)

= `sqrt(3)` √ó [tan 7¬į √ó tan(90¬į ‚Äď 7¬į)] √ó [tan 23¬į √ó tan(90¬į ‚Äď 23¬į)]

= `sqrt(3) xx 1 xx 1`¬† ¬† ......[‚ąĶ tan őł √ó tan(90¬į ‚Äď őł) = 1]

= `sqrt(3)`

= R.H.S

‚ąī tan 7¬į √ó tan 23¬į √ó tan 60¬į √ó tan 67¬į √ó tan 83¬į = `sqrt(3)`

items.title: Show that the point (0, 9) is equidistant from the points (‚Äď 4, 1) and (4, 1) - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: Show that the point (0, 9) is equidistant from the points (‚Äď 4, 1) and (4, 1)
items.solution_html:
Let P(x1, y1) = P(0, 9), Q(x2, y2) = Q(‚Äď 4, 1), R(x3, y3) = R(4, 1)

By distance formula,

d(P, Q) = `sqrt((x_2 - x_1)^2 + (y_2 - y_1)^2`

= `sqrt([(-4) - 0]^2 + (1 - 9)^2`

= `sqrt((-4)^2 + (-8)^2`

= `sqrt(16 + 64)`

= `sqrt(80)`

= `4sqrt(5)`

And

d(P, R) = `sqrt((x_3 - x_1)^2 + (y_3 - y_1)^2`

= `sqrt((4 - 0)^2 + (1 - 9)^2`

= `sqrt(4^2 + (-8)^2`

= `sqrt(16 + 64)`

= `sqrt(80)`

= `4sqrt(5)`

Here, d(P, Q) = d(P, R)

‚ąī The point (0, 9) is equidistant from (‚Äď 4, 1) and (4, 1).

items.title: Show that the points (0, ‚Äď1), (8, 3), (6, 7) and (‚Äď 2, 3) are vertices of a rectangle. - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: Show that the points (0, ‚Äď1), (8, 3), (6, 7) and (‚Äď 2, 3) are vertices of a rectangle.
items.solution_text: Let the points be P(0, ‚Äď1), Q(8, 3), R(6, 7), S(‚Äď2, 3) Distance between two points= `sqrt((x_2 - x_1)^2 + (y_2 - y_1)^2` ‚ąī By distance formula, d(P, Q) = `sqrt((8 - 0)^2 + [3 - (-1)]^2` = `sqrt((8 - 0)^2 + (3 + 1)^2` = `sqrt(8^2 + 4^2)` = `sqrt(64 + 16)` = `sqrt(80)` ......(i) d(Q, R) = `sqrt((6 - 8)^2 + (7 - 3)^2` = `sqrt((-2)^2 + (4)^2` = `sqrt(4 + 16)` = `sqrt(20)` ......(ii) d(R, S) = `sqrt([(-2) - 6]^2 + (3 - 7)^2` = `sqrt((-8)^2 + (-4)^2` = `sqrt(64 + 16)` =`sqrt(80)` ......(iii) d(P, S) = `sqrt([(-2) - 0]^2 + [3 - (-1)^2]` = `sqrt((-2)^2+ (3+ 1)^2` = `sqrt((-2)^2 + 4^2` = `sqrt(4 + 16)` = `sqrt(20)` ......(iv) In ‚ĖĘPQRS, ‚ąī side PQ = side RS .......[From (i) and (iii)] side QR = side PS ......[From (ii) and (iv)] ‚ąī ‚ĖĘPQRS is a parallelogram ......[A quadrilateral is a parallelogram, if both the pairs of its opposite sides are congruent] d(P, R) = `sqrt((6 - 0)^2 + [7 - (-1)]^2` = `sqrt((6 - 0)^2 + (7 + 1)^2` = `sqrt(6^2 + 8^2)` = `sqrt(36 + 64)` = `sqrt(100)` = 10 ......(iv) d(Q, S) = `sqrt([(-2) - 8]^2 + [3 - 3]^2` = `sqrt((-10)^2 + (0)^2` = `sqrt(100 + 0)` = `sqrt(100)` = 10 ......(vi) In parallelogram PQRS, PR = QS .......[From (v) and (vi)] ‚ąī ‚ĖĘPQRS is a rectangle. .......[A parallelogram is a rectangle if its diagonals are equal]

items.title: Show that the points (2, 0), (‚Äď 2, 0) and (0, 2) are vertices of a triangle. State the type of triangle with reason - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: Show that the points (2, 0), (‚Äď 2, 0) and (0, 2) are vertices of a triangle. State the type of triangle with reason
items.solution_text: Let the points be P(2, 0), Q(‚Äď 2, 0) and R(0, 2) Distance between two points = `sqrt((x_2 - x_1)^2 + (y_2 - y_1)^2` By distance formula, d(P, Q) = `sqrt([(-2) - 2]^2 + (0 - 0)^2` = `sqrt((-4)^2 + (0)^2` = `sqrt(16 + 0)` = 4 .....(i) d(Q, R) = `sqrt([0 - (-2)]^2 + (2 - 0)^2` = `sqrt((2)^2 + (2)^2` = `sqrt(4 + 4)` = `sqrt(8)` ......(ii) d (P, R) = `sqrt((0 -2)^2 + (2 - 0)^2` = `sqrt((- 2)^2 + (2)^2` = `sqrt(4 + 4)` = `sqrt(8)` ......(iii) On adding (ii) and (iii), d(P, Q) + d(Q, R) = `4 + sqrt(8)` `4 + sqrt(8) > sqrt(8)` ‚ąī d(P, Q) + d(Q, R) > d(P, R) ‚ąī Points P, Q, R are non colinear points. We can construct a triangle through 3 non collinear points. ‚ąī The segment joining the given points form a triangle. Since P(Q, R) = P(P, R) ‚ąī ‚ąÜPQR is an isosceles triangle. ‚ąī The segment joining the points (2, 0), (‚Äď 2, 0) and (0, 2) will form an isosceles triangle.
items.solution_html:
Let the points be P(2, 0), Q(‚Äď 2, 0) and R(0, 2)

Distance between two points = `sqrt((x_2 - x_1)^2 + (y_2 - y_1)^2`

By distance formula,

d(P, Q) = `sqrt([(-2) - 2]^2 + (0 - 0)^2`

= `sqrt((-4)^2 + (0)^2`

= `sqrt(16 + 0)`

= 4¬† ¬† .....(i)

d(Q, R) = `sqrt([0 - (-2)]^2 + (2 - 0)^2`

= `sqrt((2)^2 + (2)^2`

= `sqrt(4 + 4)`

= `sqrt(8)`¬† ......(ii)

d (P, R) =¬†`sqrt((0 -2)^2 + (2 - 0)^2`

= `sqrt((- 2)^2 + (2)^2`

= `sqrt(4 + 4)`

= `sqrt(8)`¬† ......(iii)

On adding (ii) and (iii),

d(P, Q) + d(Q, R) = `4 + sqrt(8)`

`4 + sqrt(8) > sqrt(8)`

‚ąī d(P, Q) + d(Q, R) > d(P, R)

‚ąī Points P, Q, R are non colinear points.

We can construct a triangle through 3 non collinear points.

‚ąī The segment joining the given points form a triangle.

Since P(Q, R) = P(P, R)

‚ąī ‚ąÜPQR is an isosceles triangle.

‚ąī The segment joining the points (2, 0), (‚Äď 2, 0) and (0, 2) will form an isosceles triangle.

items.question: sin 2 őł + sin 2 (90 ‚Äď őł) = ?
items.solution_text: 1 Explanation: (sin (90 ‚Äď őł)) 2 = (cosőł) 2 sin 2 (90 ‚Äď őł) = cos 2 őł ...(1) sin 2 őł + cos 2 őł = 1 ‚ąī sin 2 őł + sin 2 (90 ‚Äď őł) = 1 ...From (1)
items.solution_html:
1

Explanation:

(sin (90 ‚Äď őł))2 = (cosőł)2¬†

sin2 (90 ‚Äď őł) = cos2őł¬† ¬† ¬†...(1)

sin2őł + cos2őł = 1¬† ¬† ¬†¬†

‚ąī sin2őł + sin2(90 ‚Äď őł) = 1¬† ¬† ¬†...From (1)¬†

items.title: Sin4A ‚Äď cos4A = 1 ‚Äď 2cos2A. For proof of this complete the activity given below. Activity: L.H.S = ‚Ė° = (sin2A + cos2A) (‚Ė°) = 1(‚Ė°) .....[sin2A+‚Ė°=1] = ‚Ė° ‚Äď cos2A .....[sin2A = 1 ‚Äď cos2A] = ‚Ė° = R.H.S - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: sin 4 A ‚Äď cos 4 A = 1 ‚Äď 2cos 2 A. For proof of this complete the activity given below. Activity: L.H.S = `square` = (sin 2 A + cos 2 A) `(square)` = `1 (square)` .....`[sin^2"A" + square = 1]` = `square` ‚Äď cos 2 A .....[sin 2 A = 1 ‚Äď cos 2 A] = `square` = R.H.S
items.solution_text: L.H.S = sin 4 A ‚Äď cos 4 A = (sin 2 A) 2 ‚Äď (cos 2 A) 2 = (sin 2 A + cos 2 A) ( sin 2 A ‚Äď cos 2 A ) .....[‚ąĶ a 2 ‚Äď b 2 = (a + b)(a ‚Äď b)] = 1( sin 2 A ‚Äď cos 2 A ) .....[‚ąĶ sin 2 A + cos 2 A = 1] = sin 2 A ‚Äď cos 2 A = 1 ‚Äď cos 2 A ‚Äď cos 2 A .....[sin 2 A = 1 ‚Äď cos 2 A] = 1 ‚Äď 2cos 2 A = R.H.S
items.solution_html:
L.H.S = sin4A ‚Äď cos4A¬†

= (sin2A)2 ‚Äď (cos2A)2

¬†= (sin2A + cos2A) (sin2A ‚Äď cos2A)¬† ¬† .....[‚ąĶ a2 ‚Äď b2 = (a + b)(a ‚Äď b)]

= 1(sin2A ‚Äď cos2A)¬† ¬† ¬† ¬†.....[‚ąĶ sin2A + cos2A = 1]

= sin2A ‚Äď cos2A

= 1 ‚Äď cos2A ‚Äď cos2A¬† ¬† .....[sin2A = 1 ‚Äď cos2A]

= 1 ‚Äď 2cos2A

= R.H.S

items.title: Sin75‚ąėcos15‚ąė = ? - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: `(sin 75^circ)/(cos 15^circ)` = ?
items.solution_text: `(sin 75^circ)/(cos 15^circ)` = `(sin(90^circ - 15^circ))/(cos 15^circ)` = `(cos 15^circ)/(cos 15^circ)` .....[‚ąĶ sin(90¬į ‚Äď őł) = cos őł] = 1
items.solution_html:
`(sin 75^circ)/(cos 15^circ)` = `(sin(90^circ - 15^circ))/(cos 15^circ)`

= `(cos 15^circ)/(cos 15^circ)`¬† ¬† .....[‚ąĶ sin(90¬į ‚Äď őł) = cos őł]

= 1

items.question: tan 2 őł ‚Äď sin 2 őł = tan 2 őł √ó sin 2 őł. For proof of this complete the activity given below. Activity: L.H.S = `square` = `square (1 - (sin^2theta)/(tan^2theta))` = `tan^2theta (1 - square/((sin^2theta)/(cos^2theta)))` = `tan^2theta (1 - (sin^2theta)/1 xx (cos^2theta)/square)` = `tan^2theta (1 - square)` = `tan^2theta xx square` .....[1 ‚Äď cos 2 őł = sin 2 őł] = R.H.S
items.solution_text: L.H.S = tan 2 őł ‚Äď sin 2 őł = `tan^2theta (1 - (sin^2theta)/(tan^2theta))` = `tan^2theta (1 - (sin^2theta)/((sin^2theta)/(cos^2theta)))` = `tan^2theta (1 - (sin^2theta)/1 xx (cos^2theta)/sin^2theta)` = `tan^2theta (1 - cos^2theta)` = tan 2 őł √ó sin 2 őł .....[1 ‚Äď cos 2 őł = sin 2 őł] = R.H.S
items.solution_html:
L.H.S = tan2őł ‚Äď sin2őł¬†

= `tan^2theta (1 - (sin^2theta)/(tan^2theta))`

= `tan^2theta (1 - (sin^2theta)/((sin^2theta)/(cos^2theta)))`

= `tan^2theta (1 - (sin^2theta)/1 xx (cos^2theta)/sin^2theta)`

= `tan^2theta (1 - cos^2theta)`

= tan2őł √ó sin2őł¬† ¬† ¬†.....[1 ‚Äď cos2őł = sin2őł]

= R.H.S

items.title: Tangent segments drawn from an external point to a circle are congruent, prove this theorem. Complete the following activity.Given: ‚Ė° To Prove: ‚Ė° Proof: Draw radius AP and radius AQ and complete - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: Tangent segments drawn from an external point to a circle are congruent, prove this theorem. Complete the following activity. Given: `square` To Prove: `square` Proof: Draw radius AP and radius AQ and complete the following proof of the theorem. In ‚ąÜPAD and ‚ąÜQAD, seg PA ‚ČÖ `square` .....[Radii of the same circle] seg AD ‚ČÖ seg AD ......[`square`] ‚ą†APD ‚ČÖ ‚ą†AQD = 90¬į .....[Tangent theorem] ‚ąī ‚ąÜPAD ‚ČÖ ‚ąÜQAD ....[`square`] ‚ąī seg DP ‚ČÖ seg DQ .....[`square`]
items.solution_text: Given: A is the centre of the circle. Tangents through external point D Touch the circle at the points P and Q. To Prove: seg DP ‚ČÖ seg DQ Proof: In ‚ąÜPAD and ‚ąÜQAD, seg PA ‚ČÖ seg QA .....[Radii of the same circle] seg AD ‚ČÖ seg AD ......[ Common side ] ‚ą†APD = ‚ą†AQD = 90¬į .....[Tangent theorem] ‚ąī ‚ąÜPAD ‚ČÖ ‚ąÜQAD .....[ By Hypotenuse side test ] ‚ąī seg DP ‚ČÖ seg DQ .....[ Corresponding sides of congruent triangles ]
items.solution_html:
Given: A is the centre of the circle.¬†Tangents through external point D¬†Touch the circle at the points P and Q.

To Prove: seg DP ‚ČÖ seg DQ

Proof:¬†

In ‚ąÜPAD and ‚ąÜQAD,

seg PA ‚ČÖ seg QA ¬† ¬† ¬†.....[Radii of the same circle]

seg AD ‚ČÖ seg AD¬† ¬† ......[Common side]

‚ą†APD = ‚ą†AQD = 90¬į¬† ¬† ¬†.....[Tangent theorem]

‚ąī ‚ąÜPAD ‚ČÖ ‚ąÜQAD¬† ¬† .....[By Hypotenuse side test]

‚ąī seg DP ‚ČÖ seg DQ¬† .....[Corresponding sides of congruent triangles]

items.title: The angle inscribed in the semicircle is a right angle. Prove the result by completing the following activity.Given: ‚ą†ABC is inscribed angle in a semicircle with center M T - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: The angle inscribed in the semicircle is a right angle. Prove the result by completing the following activity. Given: ‚ą†ABC is inscribed angle in a semicircle with center M To prove: ‚ą†ABC is a right angle. Proof: Segment AC is a diameter of the circle. ‚ąī m(arc AXC) = `square` Arc AXC is intercepted by the inscribed angle ‚ą†ABC ‚ą†ABC = `square` ......[Inscribed angle theorem] = `1/2 xx square` ‚ąī m‚ą†ABC = `square` ‚ąī ‚ą†ABC is a right angle.
items.solution_text: Proof: Segment AC is a diameter of the circle. ‚ąī m(arc AXC) = 180¬į ......(i) [Measure of semi circular arc is 180¬į] Arc AXC is intercepted by the inscribed angle ‚ą†ABC ‚ą†ABC = `1/2 "m"("arc AXC")` ......[Inscribed angle theorem] = `1/2 xx 180^circ` ......[From (i)] ‚ąī m‚ą†ABC = 90¬į ‚ąī ‚ą†ABC is a right angle.
items.solution_html:
Proof: Segment AC is a diameter of the circle.

‚ąī m(arc AXC) = 180¬į¬† ¬† ......(i) [Measure of semi circular arc is 180¬į]

Arc AXC is intercepted by the inscribed angle ‚ą†ABC

‚ą†ABC = `1/2 "m"("arc AXC")`¬† ¬† ¬† ......[Inscribed angle theorem]

= `1/2 xx 180^circ`¬† ¬† ¬†......[From (i)]

‚ąī m‚ą†ABC = 90¬į

‚ąī ‚ą†ABC is a right angle.

[SAS test of congruency] ‚ąī chord AC ‚ČÖ chord DE ......[Corresponding sides of congruent triangles]

In őĒABC and őĒDBE,

side AB ‚ČÖ side DB¬† ¬† ......[Radii of the same circle]

items.title: The coordinates of diameter AB of a circle are A(2, 7) and B(4 , 5), then find the coordinates of the centre - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: The coordinates of diameter AB of a circle are A(2, 7) and B(4, 5), then find the coordinates of the centre
items.solution_text: Let C(x, y) be the centre of the circle, A(x 1 , y 1 ) = A(2, 7), B(x 2 , y 2 ) = B(4, 5) ‚ąī x 1 = 2, y 1 = 7, x 2 = 4, y 2 = 5 C is the mid-point of seg AB. ‚ąī By midpoint formula, C(x, y) = `((x_1 + x_2)/2, (y_1 + y_2)/2)` = `((2 + 4)/2, (7 + 5)/2)` = `(6/2, 12/2)` ‚ąī C(x, y) = C(3, 6) ‚ąī The co-ordinates of the centre of the circle are (3, 6).
items.solution_html:
Let C(x, y) be the centre of the circle,

A(x1, y1) = A(2, 7), B(x2, y2) = B(4, 5)

‚ąī x1 = 2, y1 = 7, x2 = 4, y2 = 5

C is the mid-point of seg AB.

‚ąī By midpoint formula,

C(x, y) = `((x_1 + x_2)/2, (y_1 + y_2)/2)`

= `((2 + 4)/2, (7 + 5)/2)`

= `(6/2, 12/2)`

‚ąī C(x, y) = C(3, 6)

‚ąī The co-ordinates of the centre of the circle are (3, 6).

items.title: The coordinates of the vertices of a triangle ABC are A (‚Äď7, 6), B(2, ‚Äď2) and C(8, 5). Find coordinates of its centroid. Solution: Suppose A(x1, y1) and B(x2, y2) and C(x3, y3) x1 = ‚Äď7, y1 = 6 and - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: The coordinates of the vertices of a triangle ABC are A (‚Äď7, 6), B(2, ‚Äď2) and C(8, 5). Find coordinates of its centroid. Solution: Suppose A(x 1 , y 1 ) and B(x 2 , y 2 ) and C(x 3 , y 3 ) x 1 = ‚Äď7, y 1 = 6 and x 2 = 2, y 2 = ‚Äď2 and x 3 = 8, y 3 = 5 Using Centroid formula ‚ąī Coordinates of the centroid of a traingle ABC = `((x_1 + x_2 + x_3)/3, (y_1 + y_2 + y_3)/3)` = `(square/3, square/3)` ‚ąī Coordinates of the centroid of a triangle ABC = `(3/3, square)` ‚ąī Coordinates of the centroid of a triangle ABC = `(1 , square)`
items.solution_text: Suppose A(x 1 , y 1 ) and B(x 2 , y 2 ) and C(x 3 , y 3 ) x 1 = ‚Äď7, y 1 = 6 and x 2 = 2, y 2 = ‚Äď2 and x 3 = 8, y 3 = 5 Using Centroid formula ‚ąī Coordinates of the centroid of a traingle ABC = `((x_1 + x_2 + x_3)/3, (y_1 + y_2 + y_3)/3)` = `((-7 + 2 + 8)/3, (6 - 2 + 5)/3)` ‚ąī Coordinates of the centroid of a triangle ABC = `(3/3, 9/3)` ‚ąī Coordinates of the centroid of a triangle ABC = (1 , 3 )
items.solution_html:
Suppose A(x1, y1) and B(x2, y2) and C(x3, y3)

¬†x1 = ‚Äď7, y1 = 6 and x2 = 2, y2 = ‚Äď2 and x3 = 8, y3 = 5

Using Centroid formula

‚ąī Coordinates of the centroid of a traingle

ABC = `((x_1 + x_2 + x_3)/3, (y_1 + y_2 + y_3)/3)`

= `((-7 + 2 + 8)/3, (6 - 2 + 5)/3)`

‚ąī Coordinates of the centroid of a triangle ABC = `(3/3, 9/3)`

‚ąī Coordinates of the centroid of a triangle ABC = (1 , 3)

items.title: The distance between point P(2, 2) and Q(5, x) is 5 cm, then the value of x ______ - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: The distance between point P(2, 2) and Q(5, x) is 5 cm, then the value of x ______
items.solution_text: 6 Let P(x 1 , y 1 ) = P( 2, 2) and Q(x 2 , y 2 ) = Q(5, x) Here, x 1 = 2, y 1 = 2, x 2 = 5, y 2 = x By distance formula, d(P, Q) = `sqrt((x_2 - x_1)^2 + (y_2 - y_1)^2)` ‚ąī 5 = `sqrt((5 - 2)^2 + (x - 2)^2)` ‚ąī 5 = `sqrt(9 + x^2 - 4x + 4)` ‚ąī 5 2 = x 2 - 4x + 13 ...[Squaring both sides] ‚ąī 25 = x 2 - 4x + 13 ‚ąī x 2 - 4x + 13 - 25 = 0 ‚ąī x 2 - 4x - 12 = 0 ‚ąī (x - 6) (x + 2) = 0 ‚ąī x - 6 = 0 or x + 2 = 0 ‚ąī x = 6 or x = -2
items.solution_html:
6

Let P(x1, y1) = P( 2, 2) and Q(x2, y2) = Q(5, x)

Here, x1 = 2, y1 = 2, x2 = 5, y2 = x

By distance formula,

d(P, Q) = `sqrt((x_2 - x_1)^2 + (y_2 - y_1)^2)`

‚ąī 5 = `sqrt((5 - 2)^2 + (x - 2)^2)`

‚ąī 5 = `sqrt(9 + x^2 - 4x + 4)`

‚ąī 52 = x2 - 4x + 13¬† ¬† ¬† ...[Squaring both sides]

‚ąī 25¬†= x2 - 4x + 13¬†

‚ąī x2 - 4x + 13 - 25 = 0

‚ąī x2 - 4x - 12 = 0

‚ąī (x - 6) (x + 2) = 0

‚ąī x - 6 = 0¬† or x + 2 = 0

‚ąī x = 6¬† or x = -2

items.title: The distance between points P(‚Äď1, 1) and Q(5, ‚Äď7) is - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: The distance between points P(‚Äď1, 1) and Q(5, ‚Äď7) is ______
items.solution_html:
10 cm

Let P(x1, y1) = P( -1, 1) and Q(x2, y2) = Q(5, -7)

Here, x1 = -1, y1 = 1, x2 = 5, y2 = -7

By distance formula,

d(P, Q) = `sqrt((x_2 - x_1)^2 + (y_2 - y_1)^2)`

‚ąī d(P, Q) = `sqrt([5 - (-1)]^2 + (-7- 1)^2)`

‚ąī d(P, Q) = `sqrt(36 + 64)`

‚ąī d(P, Q) = `sqrt(100)`

‚ąī d(P, Q) = 10 cm

items.title: The figure őĒABC is an isosceles triangle with a perimeter of 44 cm. The sides AB and BC are congruent and the length of the base AC is 12 cm. If a circle touches all three sides as shown in the fi - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: The figure őĒABC is an isosceles triangle with a perimeter of 44 cm. The sides AB and BC are congruent and the length of the base AC is 12 cm. If a circle touches all three sides as shown in the figure, then find the length of the tangent segment drawn to the circle from point B.
items.solution_text: Given: AB + BC + AC = 44 cm AC = 12 cm To find: l(BP) , l(BR) Solution: `{:("seg AP" ‚ČÖ "seg AQ"),("seg QC" ‚ČÖ "seg RC"),("seg BP" ‚ČÖ "seg BR"):}}` .......[Tangent Segment theorem] `{:("Let" l("AP") = l("AQ") = x","),(l("QC") = l("RC") = y","),(l("BP") = l("BR") = "z"):}}` .....(i) AC = AQ + QC ......[A ‚Äď Q ‚Äď C] ‚ąī AC = x + y ‚ąī x + y = 12 ......(ii) [Given] AB + BC + AC = 44 ......[Given] ‚ąī (AP + PB) + (BR + RC) + (AQ + QC) = 44 ......[A‚ÄďP‚ÄďB, B‚ÄďR‚ÄďC, A‚ÄďQ‚ÄďC] ‚ąī x + z + z + y + x + y = 44 ......[From (i)] ‚ąī 2x + 2y + 2z = 44 ‚ąī 2(x + y) + 2z = 44 ‚ąī 2(12) + 2z = 44 ......[From (ii)] ‚ąī 24 + 2z = 44 ‚ąī 2z = 44 ‚Äď 24 ‚ąī 2z = 20 ‚ąī z = 10 ......(iiii) ‚ąī l(BP) = l(BR) = 10 cm ......[From (i) and (iii)]
items.solution_html:
Given: AB + BC + AC = 44 cm

AC = 12 cm

To find: l(BP) , l(BR)

Solution:

`{:("seg AP" ‚ČÖ "seg AQ"),("seg QC" ‚ČÖ "seg RC"),("seg BP" ‚ČÖ "seg BR"):}}` .......[Tangent Segment theorem]

`{:("Let"¬† l("AP") = l("AQ") = x","),(l("QC") = l("RC") = y","),(l("BP") = l("BR") = "z"):}}`¬† ¬†.....(i)

AC = AQ + QC¬† ¬† ......[A ‚Äď Q ‚Äď C]

‚ąī AC = x + y

‚ąī x + y = 12¬† ¬† ......(ii) [Given]

AB + BC + AC = 44¬† ¬†......[Given]

‚ąī (AP + PB) + (BR + RC) + (AQ + QC) = 44¬† ......[A‚ÄďP‚ÄďB, B‚ÄďR‚ÄďC, A‚ÄďQ‚ÄďC]

‚ąī x + z + z + y + x + y = 44¬† ¬†......[From (i)]

‚ąī 2x + 2y + 2z = 44

‚ąī 2(x + y) + 2z = 44

‚ąī 2(12) + 2z = 44¬† ¬† ......[From (ii)]

‚ąī 24 + 2z = 44

‚ąī 2z = 44 ‚Äď 24

‚ąī 2z = 20

‚ąī z = 10¬† ¬†......(iiii)

‚ąī l(BP) = l(BR) = 10 cm¬† ¬† ......[From (i) and (iii)]

items.title: The point Q divides segment joining A(3, 5) and B(7, 9) in the ratio 2 : 3. Find the X-coordinate of Q - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: The point Q divides segment joining A(3, 5) and B(7, 9) in the ratio 2 : 3. Find the X-coordinate of Q
items.solution_text: Let the co-ordinates of point Q be (x, y) and A (x 1 , y 1 ), B (x 2 , y 2 ) be the given points. Here, x 1 = 3, y 1 = 5, x 2 = 7, y 2 = 9, m = 2, n = 3 ‚ąī By section formula, x = `("m"x_2 + "n"x_1)/("m" + "n")` = `(2(7) + 3(3))/(2 + 3)` = `(14 + 9)/5` = `23/5` y = `("m"y_2 + "n"y_1)/("m" + "n")` = `(2(9) + 3(5))/(2 + 3)` = `(18 + 15)/5` = `33/5` ‚ąī The co-ordinates of point Q are `(23/5, 33/5)`.
items.solution_html:
Let the co-ordinates of point Q be (x, y) and A (x1, y1), B (x2, y2) be the given points.

Here, x1 = 3, y1 = 5, x2 = 7, y2 = 9, m = 2, n = 3

‚ąī By section formula,

x = `("m"x_2 + "n"x_1)/("m" + "n")`

= `(2(7) + 3(3))/(2 + 3)`

= `(14 + 9)/5`

= `23/5`

y = `("m"y_2 + "n"y_1)/("m" + "n")`

= `(2(9) + 3(5))/(2 + 3)`

= `(18 + 15)/5`

= `33/5`

‚ąī The co-ordinates of point Q are `(23/5, 33/5)`.

items.title: The points (7, ‚Äď 6), (2, k) and (h, 18) are the vertices of triangle. If (1, 5) are the coordinates of centroid, find the value of h and k - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: The points (7, ‚Äď 6), (2, k) and (h, 18) are the vertices of triangle. If (1, 5) are the coordinates of centroid, find the value of h and k
items.solution_text: Let (7, ‚Äď 6) ‚Č° (x 1 , y 1 ), (2, k) ‚Č° (x 2 , y 2 ), (h, 18) ‚Č° (x 3 , y 3 ) be the three vertices of the triangle. (1, 5) are coordinates of centroid. ‚ąī By Centroid formula, 1 = `(x_1 + x_2 + x_3)/3` ‚ąī 1 = `(7 + 2 + "h")/3` ‚ąī 3 = 9 + h ‚ąī h = ‚Äď 6 5 = `(y_1 + y_2 + y_3)/3` ‚ąī 5 = `(-6 + "k" + 18)/3` ‚ąī 15 = 12 + k ‚ąī k = 3
items.solution_html:
Let (7, ‚Äď 6) ‚Č° (x1, y1), (2, k) ‚Č° (x2, y2), (h, 18) ‚Č° (x3, y3) be the three vertices of the triangle.

(1, 5) are coordinates of centroid.

‚ąī By Centroid formula,

1 = `(x_1 + x_2 + x_3)/3`

‚ąī 1 = `(7 + 2 + "h")/3`

‚ąī 3 = 9 + h

‚ąī h = ‚Äď 6

5 = `(y_1 + y_2 + y_3)/3`

‚ąī 5 = `(-6 + "k" + 18)/3`

‚ąī 15 = 12 + k

‚ąī k = 3

items.title: There are two poles having heights 8 m and 4 m on plane ground as shown in fig. Because of sunlight shadows of smaller pole is 6m long, then find the length of shadow of longer pole. - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: There are two poles having heights 8 m and 4 m on plane ground as shown in fig. Because of sunlight shadows of smaller pole is 6m long, then find the length of shadow of longer pole.
items.solution_text: Here, AC and PR represent the bigger and smaller poles, and BC and QR represent their shadows respectively. Now, őĒACB ‚ąľ őĒPRQ .....[Vertical poles and their shadows form similar figures] ‚ąī `"CB"/"RQ" = "AC"/"PR"` ......[Corresponding sides of similar triangles] ‚ąī `x/6 = 8/4` ‚ąī x = `(8 xx 6)/4` ‚ąī x = 12 m ‚ąī The shadow of the bigger pole will be 12 metres long at that time.
items.solution_html:
Here, AC and PR represent the bigger and smaller poles, and BC and QR represent their shadows respectively.

Now,

őĒACB ‚ąľ őĒPRQ¬† ¬† .....[Vertical poles and their shadows form similar figures]

‚ąī `"CB"/"RQ" = "AC"/"PR"`¬† ¬†......[Corresponding sides of similar triangles]

‚ąī `x/6 = 8/4`

‚ąī x = `(8 xx 6)/4`

‚ąī x = 12 m

‚ąī The shadow of the bigger pole will be 12 metres long at that time.

items.title: To prove cot őł + tan őł = cosec őł √ó sec őł, complete the activity given below. Activity: L.H.S = ‚Ė° = ‚Ė°sinőł+sinőłcosőł = cos2őł+sin2őł‚Ė° = 1sinőł‚čÖcosőł ......[cos2őł+sin2őł=‚Ė°] = 1sinőł√ó1‚Ė° = ‚Ė° = R.H.S - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: To prove cot őł + tan őł = cosec őł √ó sec őł, complete the activity given below. Activity: L.H.S = `square` = `square/sintheta + sintheta/costheta` = `(cos^2theta + sin^2theta)/square` = `1/(sintheta*costheta)` ......`[cos^2theta + sin^2theta = square]` = `1/sintheta xx 1/square` = `square` = R.H.S
items.solution_text: L.H.S = cot őł + tan őł = `costheta/sintheta + sintheta/costheta` = `(cos^2theta + sin^2theta)/(sintheta*costheta)` = `1/(sintheta*costheta)` ......[cos 2 őł + sin 2 őł = 1] = `1/sintheta xx 1/costheta` = cosecőł √ó secőł = R.H.S.
items.solution_html:
L.H.S = cot őł + tan őł

= `costheta/sintheta + sintheta/costheta`

= `(cos^2theta + sin^2theta)/(sintheta*costheta)`

= `1/(sintheta*costheta)`¬† ¬† ¬†......[cos2őł + sin2őł = 1]

= `1/sintheta xx 1/costheta`

= cosecőł √ó secőł

= R.H.S.

items.title: Two Circles Having Radii 3.5 Cm and 4.8 Cm Touch Each Other Internally. Find the Distance Between Their Centres. - Geometry Mathematics 2
items.question: Two circles having radii 3.5 cm and 4.8 cm touch each other internally. Find the distance between their centres.
items.solution_text: It is given that two circle having radii 3.5 cm and 4.8 cm touch each other internally. We know, the distance between the centres of the circles touching internally is equal to the difference of their radii. ‚ąī Distance between the centres of the two circles = 4.8 cm ‚ąí 3.5 cm = 1.3 cm Thus, the distance between their centres is 1.3 cm.
items.solution_html:
It is given that two circle having radii 3.5 cm and 4.8 cm touch each other internally.

We know, the distance between the centres of the circles touching internally is equal to the difference of their radii.

‚ąī Distance between the centres of the two circles = 4.8 cm ‚ąí 3.5 cm = 1.3 cm

Thus, the distance between their centres is 1.3 cm.
